DANIEL DERONDA 

 by George Eliot 

  Let thy chief terror be of thine own soul : 
  there , ' mid the throng of hurry desire 
  that trample on the dead to seize their spoil , 
  lurk vengeance , footless , irresistible 
  as exhalation laden with slow death , 
  and o'er the fair troop of capture joy 
  breathe pallid pestilence . 

 BOOK I. the SPOILED child . 

 CHAPTER I. 

 Men can do nothing without the make - believe of a beginning . even science , the strict measurer , be oblige to start with a make - believe unit , and must fix on a point in the star ' uncease journey when his sidereal clock shall pretend that time be at Nought . his less accurate grandmother Poetry have always be understand to start in the middle ; but on reflection it appear that her proceeding be not very different from his ; since Science , too , reckon backward as well as forward , divide his unit into billion , and with his clock - finger at Nought really set off in media re . no retrospect will take we to the true beginning ; and whether our prologue be in heaven or on earth , it be but a fraction of that all - presuppose fact with which our story set out . 

 be she beautiful or not beautiful ? and what be the secret of form or expression which give the dynamic quality to her glance ? be the good or the evil genius dominant in those beam ? probably the evil ; else why be the effect that of unrest rather than of undisturbed charm ? why be the wish to look again feel as coercion and not as a longing in which the whole be consent ? 

 she who raise these question in Daniel Deronda 's mind be occupy in gambling : not in the open air under a southern sky , toss copper on a ruin wall , with rag about her limb ; but in one of those splendid resort which the enlightenment of age have prepare for the same specie of pleasure at a heavy cost of guilt moulding , dark - tone color and chubby nudity , all correspondingly heavy form a suitable condenser for human breath belong , in great part , to the high fashion , and not easily procurable to be breathe in elsewhere in the like proportion , at least by person of little fashion . 

 it be near four o'clock on a September day , so that the atmosphere be well - brew to a visible haze . there be deep stillness , break only by a light rattle , a light chink , a small sweeping sound , and an occasional monotone in French , such as might be expect to issue from an ingeniously construct automaton . round two long table be gather two serrie crowd of human being , all save one have their face and attention bent on the table . the one exception be a melancholy little boy , with his knee and calf simply in their natural clothing of epidermis , but for the rest of his person in a fancy dress . he alone have his face turn toward the doorway , and fix on it the blank gaze of a bedizened child station as a masquerade advertisement on the platform of an itinerant show , stand close behind a lady deeply engage at the roulette - table . 

 about this table fifty or sixty person be assemble , many in the outer row , where there be occasionally a deposit of new - comer , be mere spectator , only that one of they , usually a woman , might now and then be observe put down a five - franc with a simper air , just to see what the passion of gambling really be . those who be take their pleasure at a high strength , and be absorb in play , show very distant variety of european type : Livonian and Spanish , Graeco - italian and miscellaneous German , english aristocratic and english plebeian . here certainly be a striking admission of human equality . the white bejewel finger of an english countess be very near touch a bony , yellow , crab - like hand stretch a bared wrist to clutch a heap of coin a hand easy to sort with the square , gaunt face , deep - set eye , grizzle eyebrow , and ill - combed scanty hair which seem a slight metamorphosis of the vulture . and where else would her ladyship have graciously consent to sit by that dry - lipped feminine figure prematurely old , wither after short bloom like her artificial flower , hold a shabby velvet reticule before she , and occasionally put in her mouth the point with which she prick her card ? there too , very near the fair countess , be a respectable London tradesman , blonde and soft - handed , his sleek hair scrupulously part behind and before , conscious of circular address to the nobility and gentry , whose distinguished patronage enable he to take his holiday fashionably , and to a certain extent in their distinguished company . not his gambler 's passion that nullifie appetite , but a well - feed leisure , which , in the interval of win money in business and spend it showily , see no well resource than win money in play and spend it yet more showily reflect always that Providence have never manifest any disapprobation of his amusement , and dispassionate enough to leave off if the sweetness of win much and see other lose have turn to the sourness of lose much and see other win . for the vice of gambling lie in lose money at it . in his bearing there might be something of the tradesman , but in his pleasure he be fit to rank with the owner of the old title . stand close to his chair be a handsome italian , calm , statuesque , reach across he to place the first pile of napoleon from a new bagful just bring he by an envoy with a scrolled mustache . the pile be in half a minute push over to an old bewigge woman with eye - glass pinch her nose . there be a slight gleam , a faint mumbling smile about the lip of the old woman ; but the statuesque Italian remain impassive , and probably secure in an infallible system which place his foot on the neck of chance immediately prepare a new pile . so do a man with the air of an emaciated beau or wear - out libertine , who look at life through one eye - glass , and hold out his hand tremulously when he ask for change . it could surely be no severity of system , but rather some dream of white crow , or the induction that the eighth of the month be lucky , which inspire the fierce yet totter impulsiveness of his play . 

 but , while every single player differ markedly from every other , there be a certain uniform negativeness of expression which have the effect of a mask as if they have all eat of some root that for the time compel the brain of each to the same narrow monotony of action . 

 Deronda 's first thought when his eye fall on this scene of dull , gas - poison absorption , be that the gambling of spanish shepherd - boy have seem to he more enviable : so far Rousseau might be justify in maintain that art and science have do a poor service to mankind . but suddenly he feel the moment become dramatic . his attention be arrest by a young lady who , stand at an angle not far from he , be the last to whom his eye travel . she be bend and speak English to a middle - aged lady seat at play beside she : but the next instant she return to her play , and show the full height of a graceful figure , with a face which might possibly be look at without admiration , but could hardly be pass with indifference . 

 the inward debate which she raise in Deronda give to his eye a grow expression of scrutiny , tend far and far away from the glow of mingle undefined sensibility form admiration . at one moment they follow the movement of the figure , of the arm and hand , as this problematic sylph bend forward to deposit her stake with an air of firm choice ; and the next they return to the face which , at present unaffected by beholder , be direct steadily toward the game . the sylph be a winner ; and as her taper finger , delicately gloved in pale - gray , be adjust the coin which have be push toward she in order to pass they back again to the winning point , she look round she with a survey too markedly cold and neutral not to have in it a little of that nature which we call art conceal an inward exultation . 

 but in the course of that survey her eye meet Deronda 's , and instead of avert they as she would have desire to do , she be unpleasantly conscious that they be arrest how long ? the darting sense that he be measure she and look down on she as an inferior , that he be of different quality from the human dross around she , that he feel himself in a region outside and above she , and be examine she as a speciman of a low order , rouse a tingling resentment which stretch the moment with conflict . it do not bring the blood to her cheek , but it send it away from her lip . she control herself by the help of an inward defiance , and without other sign of emotion than this lip - paleness turn to her play . but Deronda 's gaze seem to have act as an evil eye . her stake be go . no matter ; she have be win ever since she take to roulette with a few napoleon at command , and have a considerable reserve . she have begin to believe in her luck , other have begin to believe in it : she have vision of be follow by a cortege who would worship she as a goddess of luck and watch her play as a directing augury . such thing have be know of male gambler ; why should not a woman have a like supremacy ? her friend and chaperon who have not wish she to play at first be begin to approve , only administer the prudent advice to stop at the right moment and carry money back to England advice to which Gwendolen have reply that she care for the excitement of play , not the winning . on that supposition the present moment ought to have make the flood - tide in her eager experience of gambling . yet , when her next stake be sweep away , she feel the orbit of her eye get hot , and the certainty she have   without look   of that man still watch she be something like a pressure which begin to be torture . the more reason to she why she should not flinch , but go on play as if she be indifferent to loss or gain . her friend touch her elbow and propose that they should quit the table . for reply Gwendolen put ten louis on the same spot : she be in that mood of defiance in which the mind lose sight of any end beyond the satisfaction of enraged resistance ; and with the puerile stupidity of a dominant impulse include luck among its object of defiance . since she be not win strikingly , the next good thing be to lose strikingly . she control her muscle , and show no tremor of mouth or hand . each time her stake be sweep off she double it . many be now watch she , but the sole observation she be conscious of be Deronda 's , who , though she never look toward he , she be sure have not move away . such a drama take no long while to play out : development and catastrophe can often be measure by nothing clumsier than the moment - hand . " faite votre jeu , madame et monsieur , " say the automatic voice of destiny from between the mustache and imperial of the croupier : and Gwendolen 's arm be stretch to deposit her last poor heap of napoleon . " Le jeu ne va plus , " say destiny . and in five second Gwendolen turn from the table , but turn resolutely with her face toward Deronda and look at he . there be a smile of irony in his eye as their glance meet ; but it be at least well that he should have disregard she as one of an insect swarm who have no individual physiognomy . besides , in spite of his superciliousness and irony , it be difficult to believe that he do not admire her spirit as well as her person : he be young , handsome , distinguish in appearance not one of these ridiculous and dowdy Philistines who think it incumbent on they to blight the gaming - table with a sour look of protest as they pass by it . the general conviction that we be admirable do not easily give way before a single negative ; rather when any of Vanity 's large family , male or female , find their performance receive coldly , they be apt to believe that a little more of it will win over the unaccountable dissident . in Gwendolen 's habit of mind it have be take for grant that she know what be admirable and that she herself be admire . this basis of her thinking have receive a disagreeable concussion , and reel a little , but be not easily to be overthrow . 

 in the evening the same room be more stiflingly heat , be brilliant with gas and with the costume of lady who float their train along it or be seat on the othman . 

 the Nereid in sea - green robe and silver ornament , with a pale sea - green feather fasten in silver fall backward over her green hat and light brown hair , be Gwendolen Harleth . she be under the wing , or rather soar by the shoulder , of the lady who have sit by she at the roulette - table ; and with they be a gentleman with a white mustache and clipped hair : solid - browed , stiff and german . they be walk about or stand to chat with acquaintance , and Gwendolen be much observe by the seat group .

 CHAPTER II . 

  this man contrive a secret ' twixt we two , 
  that he may quell I with his meeting eye 
  like one who quell a lioness at bay . 
 this be the letter Gwendolen find on her table : 

 DEAREST CHILD . I have be expect to hear from you for a week . in your last you say the Langens think of leave Leubronn and go to Baden . how could you be so thoughtless as to leave I in uncertainty about your address ? I be in the great anxiety lest this should not reach you . in any case , you be to come home at the end of September , and I must now entreat you to return as quickly as possible , for if you spend all your money it would be out of my power to send you any more , and you must not borrow of the Langens , for I could not repay they . this be the sad truth , my child I wish I could prepare you for it well but a dreadful calamity have befall we all . you know nothing about business and will not understand it ; but Grapnell    Co. have fail for a million , and we be totally ruin your aunt Gascoigne as well as I , only that your uncle have his benefice , so that by put down their carriage and get interest for the boy , the family can go on . all the property our poor father save for we go to pay the liability . there be nothing I can call my own . it be well you should know this at once , though it rend my heart to have to tell it you . of course we can not help think what a pity it be that you go away just when you do . but I shall never reproach you , my dear child ; I would save you from all trouble if I could . on your way home you will have time to prepare yourself for the change you will find . we shall perhaps leave Offendene at once , for we hope that Mr. Haynes , who want it before , may be ready to take it off my hand . of course we can not go to the rectory there be not a corner there to spare . we must get some hut or other to shelter we , and we must live on your uncle Gascoigne 's charity , until I see what else can be do . I shall not be able to pay the debt to the tradesman besides the servant ' wage . Summon up your fortitude , my dear child ; we must resign ourselves to God 's will . but it be hard to resign one 's self to Mr. Lassman 's wicked recklessness , which they say be the cause of the failure . your poor sister can only cry with I and give I no help . if you be once here , there might be a break in the cloud I always feel it impossible that you can have be mean for poverty . if the Langens wish to remain abroad , perhaps you can put yourself under some one else 's care for the journey . but come as soon as you can to your afflict and love mamma , 

 FANNY davilow . 

 the first effect of this letter on Gwendolen be half - stupefying . the implicit confidence that her destiny must be one of luxurious ease , where any trouble that occur would be well clothe and provide for , have be strong in her own mind than in her mamma 's , be feed there by her youthful blood and that sense of superior claim which make a large part of her consciousness . it be almost as difficult for she to believe suddenly that her position have become one of poverty and of humiliating dependence , as it would have be to get into the strong current of her bloom life the chill sense that her death would really come . she stand motionless for a few minute , then toss off her hat and automatically look in the glass . the coil of her smooth light - brown hair be still in order perfect enough for a ball - room ; and as on other night , Gwendolen might have look lingeringly at herself for pleasure   surely an allowable indulgence ; but now she take no conscious note of her reflect beauty , and simply star right before she as if she have be jar by a hateful sound and be wait for any sign of its cause . by - and - by she throw herself in the corner of the red velvet sofa , take up the letter again and read it twice deliberately , let it at last fall on the ground , while she rest her clasp hand on her lap and sit perfectly still , shed no tear . her impulse be to survey and resist the situation rather than to wail over it . there be no inward exclamation of " poor mamma ! " her mamma have never seem to get much enjoyment out of life , and if Gwendolen have be at this moment dispose to feel pity she would have bestow it on herself for be she not naturally and rightfully the chief object of her mamma 's anxiety too ? but it be anger , it be resistance that possess she ; it be bitter vexation that she have lose her gain at roulette , whereas if her luck have continue through this one day she would have have a handsome sum to carry home , or she might have go on play and win enough to support they all . even now be it not possible ? she have only four napoleon leave in her purse , but she possess some ornament which she could sell : a practice so common in stylish society at german bath that there be no need to be ashamed of it ; and even if she have not receive her mamma 's letter , she would probably have decide to get money for an etruscan necklace which she happen not to have be wear since her arrival ; nay , she might have do so with an agreeable sense that she be live with some intensity and escape humdrum . with ten louis at her disposal and a return of her former luck , which seem probable , what could she do well than go on play for a few day ? if her friend at home disapprove of the way in which she get the money , as they certainly would , still the money would be there . Gwendolen 's imagination dwelt on this course and create agreeable consequence , but not with unbroken confidence and rise certainty as it would have do if she have be touch with the gambler 's mania . she have go to the roulette - table not because of passion , but in search of it : her mind be still sanely capable of picture balanced probability , and while the chance of win allure she , the chance of lose thrust itself on she with alternate strength and make a vision from which her pride sink sensitively . for she be resolve not to tell the Langens that any misfortune have befall her family , or to make herself in any way indebted to their compassion ; and if she be to part with her jewelry to any observable extent , they would interfere by inquiry and remonstrance . the course that hold the least risk of intolerable annoyance be to raise money on her necklace early in the morning , tell the Langens that her mother desire her immediate return without give a reason , and take the train for Brussels that evening . she have no maid with she , and the Langens might make difficulty about her return home , but her will be peremptory . 

 instead of go to bed she make as brilliant a light as she could and begin to pack , work diligently , though all the while visit by the scene that might take place on the come day now by the tiresome explanation and farewell , and the whirling journey toward a change home , now by the alternative of stay just another day and stand again at the roulette - table . but always in this latter scene there be the presence of that Deronda , watch she with exasperating irony , and the two keen experience be inevitably revive together behold she again forsake by luck . this importunate image certainly help to sway her resolve on the side of immediate departure , and to urge her pack to the point which would make a change of mind inconvenient . it have strike twelve when she come into her room , and by the time she be assure herself that she have leave out only what be necessary , the faint dawn be steal through the white blind and dull her candle . what be the use of go to bed ? her cold bath be refreshment enough , and she see that a slight trace of fatigue about the eye only make she look the more interesting . before six o'clock she be completely equip in her gray travel dress even to her feel hat , for she mean to walk out as soon as she could count on see other lady on their way to the spring . and happen to be seat sideways before the long strip of mirror between her two window she turn to look at herself , lean her elbow on the back of the chair in an attitude that might have be choose for her portrait . it be possible to have a strong self - love without any self - satisfaction , rather with a self - discontent which be the more intense because one 's own little core of egoistic sensibility be a supreme care ; but Gwendolen know nothing of such inward strife . she have a naive delight in her fortunate self , which any but the harsh saintliness will have some indulgence for in a girl who have every day see a pleasant reflection of that self in her friend ' flattery as well as in the look - glass . and even in this beginning of trouble , while for lack of anything else to do she sit gaze at her image in the grow light , her face gather a complacency gradual as the cheerfulness of the morning . her beautiful lip curl into a more and more decided smile , till at last she take off her hat , lean forward and kiss the cold glass which have look so warm . how could she believe in sorrow ? if it attack she , she feel the force to crush it , to defy it , or run away from it , as she have do already . anything seem more possible than that she could go on bear misery , great or small . 

 Madame von Langen never go out before breakfast , so that Gwendolen could safely end her early walk by take her way homeward through the Obere Strasse in which be the need shop , sure to be open after seven . at that hour any observer whom she mind would be either on their walk in the region of the spring , or would be still in their bedroom ; but certainly there be one grand hotel , the Czarina from which eye might follow she up to Mr. Wiener 's door . this be a chance to be risk : might she not be go in to buy something which have strike her fancy ? this implicit falsehood pass through her mind as she remember that the Czarina be Deronda 's hotel ; but she be then already far up the Obere Strasse , and she walk on with her usual float movement , every line in her figure and drapery fall in gentle curve attractive to all eye except those which discern in they too close a resemblance to the serpent , and object to the revival of serpent - worship . she look neither to the right hand nor to the left , and transact her business in the shop with a coolness which give little Mr. Weiner nothing to remark except her proud grace of manner , and the superior size and quality of the three central turquoise in the necklace she offer he . they have belong to a chain once her father 's : but she have never know her father ; and the necklace be in all respect the ornament she could most conveniently part with . who suppose that it be an impossible contradiction to be superstitious and rationalize at the same time ? roulette encourage a romantic superstition as to the chance of the game , and the most prosaic rationalism as to human sentiment which stand in the way of raise needful money . Gwendolen 's dominant regret be that after all she have only nine louis to add to the four in her purse : these Jew dealer be so unscrupulous in take advantage of Christians unfortunate at play ! but she be the Langens ' guest in their hire apartment , and have nothing to pay there : thirteen louis would do more than take her home ; even if she determine on risk three , the remain ten would more than suffice , since she mean to travel right on , day and night . as she turn homeward , nay , enter and seat herself in the salon to await her friend and breakfast , she still waver as to her immediate departure , or rather she have conclude to tell the Langens simply that she have have a letter from her mamma desire her return , and to leave it still undecided when she should start . it be already the usual breakfast - time , and hear some one enter as she be lean back rather tired and hungry with her eye shut , she rise expect to see one or other of the Langens the word which might determine she linger at least another day , ready - form to pass her lip . but it be the servant bring in a small packet for Miss Harleth , which have at that moment be leave at the door . Gwendolen take it in her hand and immediately hurry into her own room . she look pale and more agitated than when she have first read her mamma 's letter . something she never quite know what reveal to she before she open the packet that it contain the necklace she have just part with . underneath the paper it be wrap in a cambric handkerchief , and within this be a scrap of tear - off note - paper , on which be write with a pencil , in clear but rapid handwriting " a stranger who have find Miss Harleth 's necklace return it to she with the hope that she will not again risk the loss of it . " 

 Gwendolen redden with the vexation of wounded pride . a large corner of the handkerchief seem to have be recklessly tear off to get rid of a mark ; but she at once believe in the first image of " the stranger " that present itself to her mind . it be Deronda ; he must have see she go into the shop ; he must have go in immediately after and repurchase the necklace . he have take an unpardonable liberty , and have dare to place she in a thoroughly hateful position . what could she do ? not , assuredly , act on her conviction that it be he who have send she the necklace and straightway send it back to he : that would be to face the possibility that she have be mistake ; nay , even if the ' stranger " be he and no other , it would be something too gross for she to let he know that she have divine this , and to meet he again with that recognition in their mind . he know very well that he be entangle she in helpless humiliation : it be another way of smile at she ironically , and take the air of a supercilious mentor . Gwendolen feel the bitter tear of mortification rise and roll down her cheek . no one have ever before dare to treat she with irony and contempt . one thing be clear : she must carry out her resolution to quit this place at once ; it be impossible for she to reappear in the public salon , still less stand at the gaming - table with the risk of see Deronda . now come an importunate knock at the door : breakfast be ready . Gwendolen with a passionate movement thrust necklace , cambric , scrap of paper , and all into her nťcessaire , press her handkerchief against her face , and after pause a minute or two to summon back her proud self - control , go to join her friend . such sign of tear and fatigue as be leave seem accordant enough with the account she at once give of she have sit up to do she packing , instead of wait for help from her friend 's maid . there be much protestation , as she have expect , against her travel alone , but she persist in refuse any arrangement for companionship . she would be put into the lady ' compartment and go right on . she could rest exceedingly well in the train , and be afraid of nothing . 

 in this way it happen that Gwendolen never reappear at the roulette - table , but that Thursday evening leave Leubronn for Brussels , and on Saturday morning arrive at Offendene , the home to which she and her family be soon to say a last good - bye . 

 CHAPTER III . 

 " let no flower of the spring pass by we ; let we crown ourselves with rosebud before they be wither . " BOOK of wisdom . 

 Pity that Offendene be not the home of Miss Harleth 's childhood , or endear to she by family memory ! a human life , I think , should be well rooted in some spot of a native land , where it may get the love of tender kinship for the face of earth , for the labor man go forth to , for the sound and accent that haunt it , for whatever will give that early home a familiar unmistakable difference amid the future widening of knowledge : a spot where the definiteness of early memory may be inwrought with affection , and kindly acquaintance with all neighbor , even to the dog and donkey , may spread not by sentimental effort and reflection , but as a sweet habit of the blood . at five year old , mortal be not prepared to be citizen of the world , to be stimulate by abstract noun , to soar above preference into impartiality ; and that prejudice in favor of milk with which we blindly begin , be a type of the way body and soul must get nourish at least for a time . the good introduction to astronomy be to think of the nightly heavens as a little lot of star belong to one 's own homestead . 

 but this bless persistence in which affection can take root have be want in Gwendolen 's life . it be only a year before her recall from Leubronn that Offendene have be choose as her mamma 's home , simply for its nearness to Pennicote Rectory , and that Mrs. Davilow , Gwendolen , and her four half - sister   the governess and the maid follow in another vehicle   have be drive along the avenue for the first time , on a late October afternoon when the rook be crawe loudly above they , and the yellow elm - leaf be whirl . 

 the season suit the aspect of the old oblong red - brick house , rather too anxiously ornament with stone at every line , not except the double row of narrow window and the large square portico . the stone encourage a greenish lichen , the brick a powdery gray , so that though the building be rigidly rectangular there be no harshness in the physiognomy which it turn to the three avenue cut east , west and south in the hundred yard ' breadth of old plantation encircle the immediate ground . one would have like the house to have be lift on a knoll , so as to look beyond its own little domain to the long thatched roof of the distant village , the church tower , the scatter homestead , the gradual rise of surge wood , and the green breadth of undulate park which make the beautiful face of the earth in that part of Wessex . but though stand thus behind , a screen amid flat pasture , it have on one side a glimpse of the wide world in the lofty curve of the chalk down , grand steadfast form play over by the change day . 

 the house be but just large enough to be call a mansion , and be moderately rent , have no manor attach to it , and be rather difficult to let with its sombre furniture and fade upholstery . but inside and outside it be what no beholder could suppose to be inhabit by retire trade - people : a certainty which be worth many convenience to tenant who not only have the taste that shrink from new finery , but also be in that border - territory of rank where annexation be a burn topic : and to take up her abode in a house which have once suffice for dowager countess give a perceptible tinge to Mrs. Davilow 's satisfaction in have an establishment of her own . this , rather mysteriously to Gwendolen , appear suddenly possible on the death of her step - father , Captain Davilow , who have for the last nine year join his family only in a brief and fitful manner , enough to reconcile they to his long absence ; but she care much more for the fact than for the explanation . all her prospect have become more agreeable in consequence . she have dislike their former way of life , rove from one foreign watering - place or parisian apartment to another , always feel new antipathy to new suite of hire furniture , and meet new people under condition which make she appear of little importance ; and the variation of have pass two year at a showy school , where , on all occasion of display , she have be put foremost , have only deepen her sense that so exceptional a person as herself could hardly remain in ordinary circumstance or in a social position less than advantageous . any fear of this latter evil be banish now that her mamma be to have an establishment ; for on the point of birth Gwendolen be quite easy . she have no notion how her maternal grandfather get the fortune inherit by his two daughter ; but he have be a West Indian which seem to exclude further question ; and she know that her father 's family be so high as to take no notice of her mamma , who nevertheless preserve with much pride the miniature of a Lady Molly in that connection . she would probably have know much more about her father but for a little incident which happen when she be twelve year old . Mrs. Davilow have bring out , as she do only at wide interval , various memorial of her first husband , and while show his miniature to Gwendolen recall with a fervor which seem to count on a peculiar filial sympathy , the fact that dear papa have die when his little daughter be in long clothe . Gwendolen , immediately think of the unlovable step - father whom she have be acquaint with the great part of her life while her frock be short , say 

 " why do you marry again , mamma ? it would have be nice if you have not . " 

 Mrs. Davilow color deeply , a slight convulsive movement pass over her face , and straightway shut up the memorial she say , with a violence quite unusual in her 

 " you have no feeling , child ! " 

 Gwendolen , who be fond of her mamma , feel hurt and ashamed , and have never since dare to ask a question about her father . 

 this be not the only instance in which she have bring on herself the pain of some filial compunction . it be always arrange , when possible , that she should have a small bed in her mamma 's room ; for Mrs. Davilow 's motherly tenderness clung chiefly to her eld girl , who have be bear in her happy time . one night under an attack of pain she find that the specific regularly place by her bedside have be forget , and beg Gwendolen to get out of bed and reach it for she . that healthy young lady , snug and warm as a rosy infant in her little couch , object to step out into the cold , and lie perfectly still , grumble a refusal . Gwendolen 's nature be not remorseless , but she like to make her penance easy , and now that she be twenty and more , some of her native force have turn into a self - control by which she guard herself from penitential humiliation . there be more show of fire and will in she than ever , but there be more calculation underneath it . 

 on this day of arrival at Offendene , which not even Mrs. Davilow have see before the place have be take for she by her brother - in - law , Mr. Gascoigne when all have get down from the carriage , and be stand under the porch in front of the open door , so that they could have a general view of the place and a glimpse of the stone hall and staircase hang with sombre picture , but enliven by a bright wood fire , no one speak ; mamma , the four sister and the governess all look at Gwendolen , as if their feeling depend entirely on her decision . of the girl , from Alice in her sixteenth year to Isabel in her tenth , hardly anything could be say on a first view , but that they be girlish , and that their black dress be get shabby . " say Mrs. Davilow , with a look of mere disgust ; but Gwendolen shudder silently , and Isabel , a plain and altogether inconvenient child with an alarming memory , say

 but her first movement be to go to the tall mirror between the window , which reflect herself and the room completely , while her mamma sit down and also look at the reflection .

I wish you have give I your perfectly straight nose ; it would have do for any sort of character a nose of all work . mine be only a happy nose ; it would not do so well for tragedy . "

 when Mr. and Mrs. Gascoigne and their daughter come , Gwendolen , far from be scornful , behave as prettily as possible to they . she be introduce herself anew to relative who have not see she since the comparatively unfinished age of sixteen , and she be anxious no , not anxious , but resolve that they should admire she . 

 Mrs. Gascoigne bear a family likeness to her sister . but she be dark and slighter , her face be unworn by grief , her movement be less languid , her expression more alert and critical as that of a rector 's wife bind to exert a beneficent authority . their close resemblance lie in a non - resistant disposition , incline to imitation and obedience ; but this , owe to the difference in their circumstance , have lead they to very different issue . the young sister have be indiscreet , or at least unfortunate in her marriage ; the elder believe herself the most enviable of wife , and her pliancy have end in she sometimes take shape of surprising definiteness . many of her opinion , such as those on church government and the character of Archbishop Laud , seem too decide under every alteration to have be arrive at otherwise than by a wifely receptiveness . and there be much to encourage trust in her husband 's authority . he have some agreeable virtue , some striking advantage , and the failing that be impute to he all lean toward the side of success . 

 one of his advantage be a fine person , which perhaps be even more impressive at fifty - seven than it have be early in life . there be no distinctively clerical line in the face , no trick of starchiness or of affected ease : in his inverness cape he could not have be identify except as a gentleman with handsome dark feature , a nose which begin with an intention to be aquiline but suddenly become straight , and iron - gray , hair . perhaps he owe this freedom from the sort of professional make - up which penetrate skin , tone and gesture and defy all drapery , to the fact that he have once be Captain Gaskin , have take order and a diphthong but shortly before his engagement to Miss Armyn . if any one have object that his preparation for the clerical function be inadequate , his friend might have ask who make a well figure in it , who preach well or have more authority in his parish ? he have a native gift for administration , be tolerant both of opinion and conduct , because he feel himself able to overrule they , and be free from the irritation of conscious feebleness . he smile pleasantly at the foible of a taste which he do not share at floriculture or antiquarianism for example , which be much in vogue among his fellow - clergyman in the diocese : for himself , he prefer follow the history of a campaign , or divine from his knowledge of Nesselrode 's motif what would have be his conduct if our cabinet have take a different course . Mr. Gascoigne 's tone of thinking after some long - quiet fluctuation have become ecclesiastical rather than theological ; not the modern Anglican , but what he would have call sound English , free from nonsense ; such as become a man who look at a national religion by daylight , and see it in its relation to other thing . no clerical magistrate have great weight at session , or less of mischievous impracticableness in relation to worldly affair . indeed , the bad imputation throw out against he be worldliness : it could not be prove that he forsook the less fortunate , but it be not to be deny that the friendship he cultivate be of a kind likely to be useful to the father of six son and two daughter ; and bitter observer for in Wessex , say ten year ago , there be person whose bitterness may now seem incredible remark that the color of his opinion have change in consistency with this principle of action . but cheerful , successful worldliness have a false air of be more selfish than the acrid , unsuccessful kind , whose secret history be sum up in the terrible word , " sell , but not pay for .  CHAPTER IV . 

 " Gorgibus .        Je te dis que le mariage e une choose sainte et sacrťe : et que c'est faire en honnete gen , que de dťbuter par la .

 it would be a little hard to blame the rector of Pennicote that in the course of look at thing from every point of view , he look at Gwendolen as a girl likely to make a brilliant marriage . why should he be expect to differ from his contemporary in this matter , and wish his niece a bad end of her charming maidenhood than they would approve as the good possible ? it be rather to be set down to his credit that his feeling on the subject be entirely good - natured . and in consider the relation of mean to end , it would have be mere folly to have be guide by the exceptional and idyllic to have recommend that Gwendolen should wear a gown as shabby as Griselda 's in order that a marquis might fall in love with she , or to have insist that since a fair maiden be to be seek , she should keep herself out of the way . Mr. Gascoigne 's calculation be of the kind call rational , and he do not even think of get a too frisky horse in order that Gwendolen might be threaten with an accident and be rescue by a man of property . he wish his niece well , and he mean she to be see to advantage in the good society of the neighborhood . 

 her uncle 's intention fall in perfectly with Gwendolen 's own wish . but let no one suppose that she also contemplate a brilliant marriage as the direct end of she witch the world with her grace on horseback , or with any other accomplishment . that she be to be marry some time or other she would have feel oblige to admit ; and that her marriage would not be of a middling kind , such as most girl be content with , she feel quietly , unargumentatively sure . but her thought never dwelt on marriage as the fulfillment of her ambition ; the drama in which she imagine herself a heroine be not work up to that close . to be very much sue or hopelessly sigh for as a bride be indeed an indispensable and agreeable guarantee of womanly power ; but to become a wife and wear all the domestic fetter of that condition , be on the whole a vexatious necessity . her observation of matrimony have incline she to think it rather a dreary state in which a woman could not do what she like , have more child than be desirable , be consequently dull , and become irrevocably immerse in humdrum . of course marriage be social promotion ; she could not look forward to a single life ; but promotion have sometimes to be take with bitter herb a peerage will not quite do instead of leadership to the man who mean to lead ; and this delicate - limbed sylph of twenty mean to lead . for such passion dwell in feminine breast also . in Gwendolen 's , however , they dwelt among strictly feminine furniture , and have no disturbing reference to the advancement of learn or the balance of the constitution ; her knowledge be such as with no sort of standing - room or length of lever could have be expect to move the world . she mean to do what be pleasant to herself in a striking manner ; or rather , whatever she could do so as to strike other with admiration and get in that reflect way a more ardent sense of living , seem pleasant to her fancy . 

 " Gwendolen will not rest without have the world at her foot , " say Miss Merry , the meek governess : hyperbolical word which have long come to carry the most moderate meaning ; for who have not hear of private person have the world at their foot in the shape of some half - dozen item of flattering regard generally know in a genteel suburb ? and word could hardly be too wide or vague to indicate the prospect that make a hazy largeness about poor Gwendolen on the height of her young self - exultation . other people allow themselves to be make slave of , and to have their life blow hither and thither like empty ship in which no will be present . it be not to be so with she ; she would no long be sacrifice to creature worth less than herself , but would make the very good of the chance that life offer she , and conquer circumstance by her exceptional cleverness . certainly , to be settle at Offendene , with the notice of Lady Brackenshaw , the archery club , and invitation to dine with the arrowpoint , as the high light in her scenery , be not a position that seem to offer remarkable chance ; but Gwendolen 's confidence lie chiefly in herself . she feel well equipped for the mastery of life . with regard to much in her lot hitherto , she hold herself rather hardly deal with , but as to her " education , " she would have admit that it have leave she under no disadvantage . in the school - room her quick mind have take readily that strong starch of unexplained rule and disconnected fact which save ignorance from any painful sense of limpness ; and what remain of all thing knowable , she be conscious of be sufficiently acquaint with through novel , play and poem . about her french and music , the two justify accomplishment of a young lady , she feel no ground for uneasiness ; and when to all these qualification , negative and positive , we add the spontaneous sense of capability some happy person be bear with , so that any subject they turn their attention to impress they with their own power of form a correct judgment on it , who can wonder if Gwendolen feel ready to manage her own destiny ? 

 there be many subject in the world perhaps the majority in which she feel no interest , because they be stupid ; for subject be apt to appear stupid to the young as light seem dull to the old ; but she would not have feel at all helpless in relation to they if they have turn up in conversation . it must be remember that no one have dispute her power or her general superiority . as on the arrival at Offendene , so always , the first thought of those about she have be , what will Gwendolen think ? if the footman trod heavily in creak boot , or if the laundress 's work be unsatisfactory , the maid say , " this will never do for Miss Harleth " ; if the wood smoke in the bedroom fireplace , Mrs. Davilow , whose own weak eye suffer much from this inconvenience , speak apologetically of it to Gwendolen . if , when they be under the stress of travel , she do not appear at the breakfast table till every one else have finish , the only question be , how Gwendolen 's coffee and toast should still be of the hot and crispest ; and when she appear with her freshly - brush light - brown hair stream backward and await her mamma 's hand to coil it up , her large brown eye glance bright as a wave - wash onyx from under their long lash , it be always she herself who have to be tolerant to beg that Alice who sit wait on she would not stick up her shoulder in that frightful manner , and that Isabel , instead of push up to she and ask question , would go away to Miss Merry . 

 always she be the princess in exile , who in time of famine be to have her breakfast - roll make of the finest - bolt flour from the seven thin ear of wheat , and in a general decampment be to have her silver fork keep out of the baggage . how be this to be account for ? the answer may seem to lie quite on the surface : in her beauty , a certain unusualness about she , a decision of will which make itself feel in her graceful movement and clear unhesitate tone , so that if she come into the room on a rainy day when everybody else be flaccid and the use of thing in general be not apparent to they , there seem to be a sudden , sufficient reason for keep up the form of life ; and even the waiter at hotel show the more alacrity in do away with crumb and crease and dreg with struggle fly in they . this potent charm , add to the fact that she be the eld daughter , toward whom her mamma have always be in an apologetic state of mind for the evil bring on she by a step - father , may seem so full a reason for Gwendolen 's domestic empire , that to look for any other would be to ask the reason of daylight when the sun be shin . but beware of arrive at conclusion without comparison . I remember have see the same assiduous , apologetic attention award to person who be not at all beautiful or unusual , whose firmness show itself in no very graceful or euphonious way , and who be not eld daughter with a tender , timid mother , compunctious at have subject they to inconvenience . some of they be a very common sort of man . and the only point of resemblance among they all be a strong determination to have what be pleasant , with a total fearlessness in make themselves disagreeable or dangerous when they do not get it . who be so much cajole and serve with trembling by the weak female of a household as the unscrupulous male capable , if he have not free way at home , of go and do bad elsewhere ? hence I be force to doubt whether even without her potent charm and peculiar filial position Gwendolen might not still have play the queen in exile , if only she have keep her inborn energy of egoistic desire , and her power of inspire fear as to what she might say or do . however , she have the charm , and those who fear she be also fond of she ; the fear and the fondness be perhaps both heighten by what may be call the iridescence of her character the play of various , nay , contrary tendency . for Macbeth 's rhetoric about the impossibility of be many opposite thing in the same moment , refer to the clumsy necessity of action and not to the subtler possibility of feeling . we can not speak a loyal word and be meanly silent ; we can not kill and not kill in the same moment ; but a moment be wide enough for the loyal and mean desire , for the outlash of a murderous thought and the sharp backward stroke of repentance .

Gwendolen , in spite of her wound egoism , have fullness of nature enough to feel the power of this playing , and it gradually turn she inward sob of mortification into an excitement which lift she for the moment into a desperate indifference about her own doing , or at least a determination to get a superiority over they by laugh at they as if they belong to somebody else . her eye have become bright , her cheek slightly flushed , and her tongue ready for any mischievous remark .

 that lofty criticism have cause Gwendolen a new sort of pain . she would not have choose to confess how unfortunate she think herself in not have have Miss Arrowpoint 's musical advantage , so as to be able to question Herr Klesmer 's taste with the confidence of thorough knowledge ; still less , to admit even to herself that Miss Arrowpoint each time they meet raise an unwonted feeling of jealousy in she : not in the least because she be an heiress , but because it be really provoke that a girl whose appearance you could not characterize except by say that her figure be slight and of middle stature , her feature small , her eye tolerable , and her complexion sallow , have nevertheless a certain mental superiority which could not be explain away an exasperating thoroughness in her musical accomplishment , a fastidious discrimination in her general taste , which make it impossible to force her admiration and keep you in awe of her standard . this insignificant - looking young lady of four - and - twenty , whom any one 's eye would have pass over negligently if she have not be Miss Arrowpoint , might be suspect of a secret opinion that Miss Harleth 's acquirement be rather of a common order , and such an opinion be not make agreeable to think of by be always veil under a perfect kindness of manner .

Gwendolen be as inwardly rebellious against the restraint of family condition , and as ready to look through obligation into her own fundamental want of feeling for they , as if she have be sustain by the bold speculation ; but she really have no such speculation , and would at once have mark herself off from any sort of theoretical or practically reform woman by satirize they . she rejoice to feel herself exceptional ; but her horizon be that of the genteel romance where the heroine 's soul pour out in her journal be full of vague power , originality , and general rebellion , while her life move strictly in the sphere of fashion ; and if she wander into a swamp , the pathos lie partly , so to speak , in she have on her satin shoe . here be a restraint which nature and society have provide on the pursuit of striking adventure ; so that a soul burn with a sense of what the universe be not , and ready to take all existence as fuel , be nevertheless hold captive by the ordinary wirework of social form and do nothing particular . 

 this commonplace result be what Gwendolen find herself threaten with even in the novelty of the first winter at Offendene . what she be clear upon be , that she do not wish to lead the same sort of life as ordinary young lady do ; but what she be not clear upon be , how she should set about lead any other , and what be the particular act which she would assert her freedom by do . I will be Leontes , and Miss Merry , Paulina , one on each side . our dress will not   signify , " he go on laughingly ; " it will be more shakespearian and romantic if leonte look like Napoleon , and Paulina like a modern spinster .  and Hermione be choose ; all agree that age be of no consequence , but Gwendolen urge that instead of the mere tableau there should be just enough act of the scene to introduce the striking up of the music as a signal for she to step down and advance ; when leonte , instead of embrace she , be to kneel and kiss the hem of her garment , and so the curtain be to fall . the antechamber with fold door lend itself admirably to the purpose of a stage , and the whole of the establishment , with the addition of Jarrett the village carpenter , be absorb in the preparation for an entertainment , which , consider that it be an imitation of acting , be likely to be successful , since we know from ancient fable that an imitation may have more chance of success than the original .

 and a quick fire of undertone question and answer go round .

"

 " please forgive I , Gwendolen . " 

 the forgiveness be soon bestow than it would have be if Gwendolen have not desire to dismiss from her own and every one else 's memory any case in which she have show her susceptibility to terror . she wonder at herself in these occasional experience , which seem like a brief remember madness , an unexplained exception from her normal life ; and in this instance she feel a peculiar vexation that her helpless fear have show itself , not , as usual , in solitude , but in well - light company . her ideal be to be dare in speech and reckless in brave danger , both moral and physical ; and though her practice fall far behind her ideal , this shortcoming seem to be due to the pettiness of circumstance , the narrow theatre which life offer to a girl of twenty , who can not conceive herself as anything else than a lady , or as in any position which would lack the tribute of respect . she have no permanent consciousness of other fetter , or of more spiritual restraint , have always dislike whatever be present to she under the name of religion , in the same way that some people dislike arithmetic and account : it have raise no other emotion in she , no alarm , no longing ; so that the question whether she believe it have not occur to she any more than it have occur to she to inquire into the condition of colonial property and banking , on which , as she have have many opportunity of know , the family fortune be dependent . all these fact about herself she would have be ready to admit , and even , more or less indirectly , to state . what she unwillingly recognize , and would have be glad for other to be unaware of , be that liability of hers to fit of spiritual dread , though this fountain of awe within she have not find its way into connection with the religion teach she or with any human relation . she be ashamed and frighten , as at what might happen again , in remember her tremor on suddenly feel herself alone , when , for example , she be walk without companionship and there come some rapid change in the light . solitude in any wide scene impress she with an undefined feeling of immeasurable existence aloof from she , in the midst of which she be helplessly incapable of assert herself . the little astronomy teach she at school use sometimes to set her imagination at work in a way that make her tremble : but always when some one join she she recover her indifference to the vastness in which she seem an exile ; she find again her usual world in which her will be of some avail , and the religious nomenclature belong to this world be no more identify for she with those uneasy impression of awe than her uncle 's surplice see out of use at the rectory . with human ear and eye about she , she have always hitherto recover her confidence , and feel the possibility of win empire . 

 to her mamma and other her fit of timidity or terror be sufficiently account for by her ' sensitiveness " or the " excitability of her nature " ; but these explanatory phrase require conciliation with much that seem to be blank indifference or rare self - mastery . heat be a great agent and a useful word , but consider as a means of explain the universe it require an extensive knowledge of difference ; and as a means of explain character ' sensitiveness " be in much the same predicament . but who , love a creature like Gwendolen , would not be incline to regard every peculiarity in she as a mark of preeminence ? that be what Rex do . after the Hermione scene he be more persuade than ever that she must be instinct with all feeling , and not only ready to respond to a worshipful love , but able to love well than other girl . Rex feel the summer on his young wing and soar happily .

 " the kindliest symptom , yet the most alarming crisis in the ticklish state of youth ; the nourisher and destroyer of hopeful wit ;        the servitude above freedom ; the gentle mind 's religion ; the liberal superstition . " CHARLES LAMB . 

 the first sign of the unimagined snow - storm be like the transparent white cloud that seem to set off the blue . Anna be in the secret of Rex 's feeling ; though for the first time in their life he have say nothing to she about what he most think of , and he only take it for grant that she know it . for the first time , too , Anna could not say to Rex what be continually in her mind . perhaps it might have be a pain which she would have have to conceal , that he should so soon care for some one else more than for herself , if such a feeling have not be thoroughly neutralize by doubt and anxiety on his behalf . Anna admire her cousin would have say with simple sincerity , " Gwendolen be always very good to I , " and hold it in the order of thing for herself to be entirely subject to this cousin ; but she look at she with mingled fear and distrust , with a puzzled contemplation as of some wondrous and beautiful animal whose nature be a mystery , and who , for anything Anna know , might have an appetite for devour all the small creature that be her own particular pet . and now Anna 's heart be sink under the heavy conviction which she dare not utter , that Gwendolen would never care for Rex . what she herself hold in tenderness and reverence have constantly seem indifferent to Gwendolen , and it be easy to imagine her scorn Rex than return any tenderness of his . besides , she be always think of be something extraordinary . and poor Rex ! Papa would be angry with he if he know . and of course he be too young to be in love in that way ; and she , Anna have think that it would be year and year before any thing of that sort come , and that she would be Rex 's housekeeper ever so long . but what a heart must that be which do not return his love ! Anna , in the prospect of his suffering , be begin to dislike she too fascinating cousin . 

 it seem to she , as it do to Rex , that the week have be fill with a tumultuous life evident to all observer : if he have be question on the subject he would have say that he have no wish to conceal what he hop would be an engagement which he should immediately tell his father of : and yet for the first time in his life he be reserve not only about his feeling but which be more remarkable to Anna about certain action . she , on her side , be nervous each time her father or mother begin to speak to she in private lest they should say anything about Rex and Gwendolen . but the elder be not in the least alive to this agitate drama , which go forward chiefly in a sort of pantomime extremely lucid in the mind thus express themselves , but easily miss by spectator who be run their eye over the Guardian or the Clerical Gazette , and regard the triviality of the young one with scarcely more interpretation than they give to the action of lively ant . 

 " where be you go , Rex ? " say Anna one gray morning when her father have set off in his carriage to the session , Mrs. Gascoigne with he , and she have observe that her brother have on his antigropelo , the utmost approach he possess to a hunting equipment . 

 " go to see the hound throw off at the Three Barns . "

 " she tell you , do she ? " 

 " no , but I think do papa know you be go ? " 

 " not that I be aware of . I do not   suppose he would trouble himself about the matter . "

 " he know I do that whenever I can . "

 " papa and mamma and aunt davilow all wish she not to . they think it be not right for she . " 

 " why should you suppose she be go to do what be not right ? " 

 " gwendolen mind nobody sometimes , " say Anna get bold by dint of a little anger . 

 " then she would not mind I , " say Rex , perversely make a joke of poor Anna 's anxiety . 

 " oh Rex , I can not bear it . you will make yourself very unhappy . " here 
 Anna burst into tear . 
 " Nannie , Nannie , what on earth be the matter with you ? " say Rex , a little impatient at be keep in this way , hat on and whip in hand . 

 " she will not care for you one bit I know she never will ! " say the poor child in a sob whisper . she have lose all control of herself . 

 Rex redden and hurry away from she out of the hall door , leave she to the miserable consciousness of have make herself disagreeable in vain . 

 he do think of her word as he ride along ; they have the unwelcomeness which all unfavorable fortune - telling have , even when laugh at ; but he quickly explain they as spring from little Anna 's tenderness , and begin to be sorry that he be oblige to come away without soothe she . every other feeling on the subject , however , be quickly merge in a resistant belief to the contrary of her , accompany with a new determination to prove that he be right . this sort of certainty have just enough kinship to doubt and uneasiness to hurry on a confession which an untouched security might have delay . 

 Gwendolen be already mount and rid up and down the avenue when Rex appear at the gate . she have provide herself against disappointment in case he do not appear in time by have the groom ready behind she , for she would not have wait beyond a reasonable time . but now the groom be dismiss , and the two ride away in delightful freedom . Goodness be a large , often a prospective word ; like harvest , which at one stage when we talk of it lie all underground , with an indeterminate future ; be the germ prosper in the darkness ? at another , it have put forth delicate green blade , and by - and - by the trembling blossom be ready to be dash off by an hour of rough wind or rain . each stage have its peculiar blight , and may have the healthy life choke out of it by a particular action of the foul land which rear or neighbor it , or by damage bring from foulness afar .

 eight month after the arrival of the family at Offendene , that be to say in the end of the following June , a rumor be spread in the neighborhood which to many person be matter of exciting interest . it have no reference to the result of the american war , but it be one which touch all class within a certain circuit round Wanchester : the corn - factor , the brewer , the horse - dealer , and saddler , all hold it a laudable thing , and one which be to be rejoice in on abstract ground , as show the value of an aristocracy in a free country like England ; the blacksmith in the hamlet of Diplow feel that a good time have come round ; the wife of labor man hop their nimble boy of ten or twelve would be take into employ by the gentleman in livery ; and the farmer about Diplow admit , with a tincture of bitterness and reserve that a man might now again perhaps have an easy market or exchange for a rick of old hay or a wagon - load of straw . if such be the hope of low person not in society , it may be easily infer that their better have well reason for satisfaction , probably connect with the pleasure of life rather than its business . marriage , however , must be consider as come under both head ; and just as when a visit of majesty be announce , the dream of knighthood or a baronetcy be to be find under various municipal nightcap , so the news in question raise a float indeterminate vision of marriage in several well - breed imagination .

 Brackenshaw Park , where the Archery Meeting be hold , look out from its gentle height far over the neighboring valley to the outlying eastern down and the broad , slow rise of cultivate country , hang like a vast curtain toward the west . the castle which stand on the high platform of the cluster hill , be build of rough - hewn limestone , full of light and shadow make by the dark dust of lichen and the washing of the rain . mass of beech and fir shelter it on the north , and spread down here and there along the green slope like flock seek the water which gleam below . the archery - ground be a carefully - keep enclosure on a bit of table - land at the farth end of the park , protect toward the southwest by tall elm and a thick screen of holly , which keep the gravel walk and the bit of newly - mow turf where the target be place in agreeable afternoon shade . the Archery Hall with an arcade in front show like a white temple against the greenery on the north side . 

 what could make a well background for the flower - group of lady , move and bow and turn their neck as it would become the leisurely lily to do if they take to locomotion . the sound too be very pleasant to hear , even when the military band from Wanchester cease to play : musical laugh in all the register and a harmony of happy , friendly speech , now rise toward mild excitement , now sink to an agreeable murmur . 

 no open - air amusement could be much free from those noisy , crowd condition which spoil most modern pleasure ; no Archery Meeting could be more select , the number of friend accompany the member be restrict by an award of ticket , so as to keep the maximum within the limit of convenience for the dinner and ball to be hold in the castle . within the enclosure no plebeian spectator be admit except Lord Brackenshaw 's tenant and their family , and of these it be chiefly the feminine member who use the privilege , bring their little boy and girl or young brother and sister . the male among they relieve the insipidity of the entertainment by imaginative betting , in which the stake be " anything you like , " on their favorite archer ; but the young maiden , have a different principle of discrimination , be consider which of those sweetly - dress lady they would choose to be , if the choice be allow they . probably the form these rural soul would most have strive for as a tabernacle , be some other than Gwendolen 's one with more pink in her cheek and hair of the most fashionable yellow ; but among the male judge in the rank immediately surround she there be unusual unanimity in pronounce she the fine girl present . 

 no wonder she enjoy her existence on that July day . Pre - eminence be sweet to those who love it , even under mediocre circumstance . perhaps it be not quite mythical that a slave have be proud to be buy first ; and probably a barn - door fowl on sale , though he may not have understand himself to be call the good of a bad lot , may have a self - inform consciousness of his relative importance , and strut console . but for complete enjoyment the outward and the inward must concur . and that concurrence be happen to Gwendolen . 

 who can deny that bow and arrow be among the prettiest weapon in the world for feminine form to play with ? they prompt attitude full of grace and power , where that fine concentration of energy see in all markmanship , be free from association of bloodshed . the time - honor british resource of " kill something " be no long carry on with bow and quiver ; band defend their pass against an invade nation fight under another sort of shade than a cloud of arrow ; and poison dart be harmless survival either in rhetoric or in region comfortably remote . archery have no ugly smell of brimstone ; break nobody 's shin , breed no athletic monster ; its only danger be that of failing , which for generous blood be enough to mould skilful action . and among the Brackenshaw archer the prize be all of the nobler symbolic kind ; not properly to be carry off in a parcel , degrade honor into gain ; but the gold arrow and the silver , the gold star and the silver , to be wear for a long time in sign of achievement and then transfer to the next who do excellently . these sign of pre - eminence have the virtue of wreath without their inconvenience , which might have produce a melancholy effect in the heat of the ball - room . altogether the Brackenshaw Archery Club be an institution frame with good taste , so as not to have by necessity any ridiculous incident . 

 and to - day all incalculable element be in its favor . there be mild warmth , and no wind to disturb either hair or drapery or the course of the arrow ; all skillful preparation have fair play , and when there be a general march to extract the arrow , the promenade of joyous young creature in light speech and laughter , the graceful movement in common toward a common object , be a show worth look at . here Gwendolen seem a calypso among her nymph . it be in her attitude and movement that every one be oblige to admit her surpass charm .

 could there be a slenderer , more insignificant thread in human history than this consciousness of a girl , busy with her small inference of the way in which she could make her life pleasant ? in a time , too , when idea be with fresh vigor make army of themselves , and the universal kinship be declare itself fiercely ; when woman on the other side of the world would not mourn for the husband and son who die bravely in a common cause , and man stint of bread on our side of the world hear of that willing loss and be patient : a time when the soul of man be walk to pulse which have for century be beat in he unfelt , until their full sum make a new life of terror or of joy .

 CHAPTER XII . 

  " o gentleman , the time of life be short ; 
  to spend that shortness basely be too long , 
  if life do ride upon a dial 's point , 
  still end at the arrival of an hour . " 
  SHAKESPEARE : Henry IV . 
 on the second day after the Archery Meeting , Mr. Henleigh Mallinger Grandcourt be at his breakfast - table with Mr. Lush . everything around they be agreeable : the summer air through the open window , at which the dog could walk in from the old green turf on the lawn ; the soft , purplish color of the park beyond , stretch toward a mass of border wood ; the still life in the room , which seem the stiller for its sober antiquated elegance , as if it keep a conscious , well - breed silence , unlike the restlessness of vulgar furniture . 

 whether the gentleman be agreeable to each other be less evident .  CHAPTER XIII . 

 " Philistia , be thou glad of I ! " 

 Grandcourt have make up his mind to marry Miss Harleth , show a power of adapt mean to end . during the next fortnight there be hardly a day on which by some arrangement or other he do not see she , or prove by emphatic attention that she occupy his thought . his cousin , Mrs. Torrington , be now do the honor of his house , so that Mrs. Davilow and Gwendolen could be invite to a large party at Diplow in which there be many witness how the host distinguish the dowerless beauty , and show no solicitude about the heiress . the world I mean Mr. Gascoigne and all the family worth speak of within visit distance of Pennicote feel an assurance on the subject which in the rector 's mind convert itself into a resolution to do his duty by his niece and see that the settlement be adequate . indeed the wonder to he and Mrs. Davilow be that the offer for which so many suitable occasion present themselves have not be already make ; and in this wonder Grandcourt himself be not without a share . when he have tell his resolution to Lush he have think that the affair would be conclude more quickly , and to his own surprise he have repeatedly promise himself in a morning that he would to - day give Gwendolen the opportunity of accept he , and have find in the evening that the necessary formality be still unaccomplished . this remarkable fact serve to heighten his determination on another day . he have never admit to himself that Gwendolen might refuse he , but heaven help we all ! we be often unable to act on our certainty ; our objection to a contrary issue   be it possible   be so strong that it rise like a spectral illusion between we and our certainty ; we be rationally sure that the blind worm can not bite we mortally , but it would be so intolerable to be bite , and the creature have a biting look we decline to handle it . 

 he have ask leave to have a beautiful horse of his bring for Gwendolen to ride . Mrs. Davilow be to accompany she in the carriage , and they be to go to Diplow to lunch , Grandcourt conduct they . it be a fine mid - harvest time , not too warm for a noonday ride of five mile to be delightful ; the poppy glow on the border of the field , there be enough breeze to move gently like a social spirit among the ear of uncut corn , and to wing the shadow of a cloud across the soft gray down ; here the sheaf be stand , there the horse be strain their muscle under the last load from a wide space of stubble , but everywhere the green pasture make a broad setting for the corn - field , and the cattle take their rest under wide branch . the road lie through a bit of country where the dairy - farm look much as they do in the day of our forefather where peace and permanence seem to find a home away from the busy change that send the railway train fly in the distance . 

 but the spirit of peace and permanence do not penetrate poor Mrs. Davilow 's mind so as to overcome her habit of uneasy foreboding . Gwendolen and Grandcourt canter in front of she , and then slacken their pace to a conversational walk till the carriage come up with they again , make a gratify sight ; but it serve chiefly to keep up the conflict of hope and fear about her daughter 's lot . here be an irresistible opportunity for a lover to speak and put an end to all uncertainty , and Mrs. Davilow could only hope with tremble that Gwendolen 's decision would be favorable . certainly if Rex 's love have be repugnant to she , Mr. Grandcourt have the advantage of be in complete contrast with Rex ; and that he have produce some quite novel impression on she seem evident in her marked abstinence from satirical observation , nay , her total silence about his characteristic , a silence which Mrs. Davilow do not dare to break . " be he a man she would be happy with ? " be a question that inevitably arise in the mother 's mind . " well , perhaps as happy as she would be with any one else or as most other woman be " be the answer with which she try to quiet herself ; for she could not imagine Gwendolen under the influence of any feeling which would make she satisfied in what we traditionally call " mean circumstance . " 

 Grandcourt 's own thought be look in the same direction : he want to have do with the uncertainty that belong to his not have speak . as to any further uncertainty well , it be something without any reasonable basis , some quality in the air which act as an irritant to his wish .

" 

 Grandcourt 's speech this morning be , as usual , all of that brief sort which never fail to make a conversational figure when the speaker be hold important in his circle . stop so soon , they give sign of a suppressed and formidable ability so say more , and have also the meritorious quality of allow lengthiness to other . 

 " how do you like Criterion 's pace ? " he say , after they have enter the park and be slack from a canter to a walk . 

 " he be delightful to ride . I should like to have a leap with he , if it would not frighten mamma . there be a good wide channel we pass five minute ago . I should like to have a gallop back and take it . " 

 " pray do . we can take it together . " 

 " no , thank . Mamma be so timid if she see I it might make she ill . " 

 " let I go and explain . criterion would take it without fail . "

 CHAPTER XV . 

 " Festina lente celerity should be contempere with cunctation . " SIR THOMAS BROWNE . 

 Gwendolen , we have see , pass her time abroad in the new excitement of gambling , and in imagine herself an empress of luck , have bring from her late experience a vague impression that in this confused world it signify nothing what any one do , so that they amuse themselves . we have see , too , that certain person , mysteriously symbolize as Grapnell    Co. , have also think of reign in the realm of luck , and be also bent on amuse themselves , no matter how , have bring about a painful change in her family circumstance ; whence she have return home carry with she , against her inclination , a necklace which she have pawn and some one else have redeem . 

 while she be go back to England , Grandcourt be come to find she ; come , that is , after his own manner not in haste by express straight from Diplow to Leubronn , where she be understand to be ; but so entirely without hurry that he be induce by the presence of some russian acquaintance to linger at Baden - Baden and make various appointment with they , which , however , his desire to be at Leubronn ultimately cause he to break . Grandcourt 's passion be of the intermittent , flicker kind : never flame out strongly . but a great deal of life go on without strong passion : myriad of cravat be carefully tie , dinner attend , even speech make propose the health of august personage without the z arise from a strong desire . and a man may make a good appearance in high social position may be suppose to know the classic , to have his reserve on science , a strong though repress opinion on politic , and all the sentiment of the english gentleman , at a small expense of vital energy . also , he may be obstinate or persistent at the same low rate , and may even show sudden impulse which have a false air of daemonic strength because they seem inexplicable , though perhaps their secret lie merely in the want of regulated channel for the soul to move in good and sufficient duct of habit without which our nature easily turn to mere ooze and mud , and at any pressure yield nothing but a spurt or a puddle . 

 Grandcourt have not be altogether displease by Gwendolen 's run away from the splendid chance he be hold out to she . the act have some piquancy for he . he like to think that it be due to resentment of his careless behavior in Cardell Chase , which , when he come to consider it , do appear rather cool . to have bring she so near a tender admission , and then to have walk headlong away from further opportunity of win the consent which he have make she understand he to be ask for , be enough to provoke a girl of spirit ; and to be worth his mastering it be proper that she should have some spirit .
