 " there be Harry Brendall . he can take the name . " 

 " Harry Brendall will get through it all in no time , " say Mr. 
 Arrowpoint , relighte his cigar . 
 and thus , with nothing settle but the determination of the lover , 
 Klesmer have leave Quetcham . 
 CHAPTER XXIII . 

 among the heir of Art , as be the division of the promise land , each have to win his portion by hard fighting : the bestowal be after the manner of prophecy , and be a title without possession . to carry the map of an ungotten estate in your pocket be a poor sort of copyhold . and in fancy to cast his shoe over Eden be little warrant that a man shall ever set the sole of his foot on an acre of his own there . 

 the most obstinate belief that mortal entertain about themselves be such as they have no evidence for beyond a constant , spontaneous pulsing of their self - satisfaction as it be a hidden seed of madness , a confidence that they can move the world without precise notion of stand - place or lever . 

 " pray go to church , mamma , " say Gwendolen the next morning . " I prefer see Herr Klesmer alone . "   he have write in reply to her note that he would be with she at eleven . 

 " that be hardly correct , I think , " say Mrs. Davilow , anxiously . 

 " our affair be too serious for we to think of such nonsensical rule , " say Gwendolen , contemptuously . " they be insult as well as ridiculous . " 

 " you would not mind Isabel sit with you ? she would be read in a corner . " 

 " no ; she could not : she would bite her nail and stare . it would be too irritate . trust my judgment , mamma , I must be alone , take they all to church . " 

 Gwendolen have her way , of course ; only that Miss Merry and two of the girl stay at home , to give the house a look of habitation by sit at the dining - room window . 

 it be a delicious Sunday morning . the melancholy wan sunshine of autumn rest on the half - strown grass and come mildly through the window in slant band of brightness over the old furniture , and the glass panel that reflect the furniture ; over the tapestrie chair with their fade flower - wreath , the dark enigmatic picture , the superannuate organ at which Gwendolen have please herself with act Saint Cecelia on her first joyous arrival , the crowd of pallid , dusty knicknack see through the open door of the antechamber where she have achieve the wearing of her greek dress as Hermione . this last memory be just now very busy in she ; for have not Klesmer then be strike with admiration of her pose and expression ? whatever he have say , whatever she imagine he to have think , be at this moment point with keenest interest for she : perhaps she have never before in her life feel so inwardly dependent , so consciously in need of another person 's opinion . there be a new fluttering of spirit within she , a new element of deliberation in her self - estimate which have hitherto be a blissful gift of intuition . still it be the recurrent burden of her inward soliloquy that Klesmer have see but little of she , and any unfavorable conclusion of his must have too narrow a foundation . she really feel clever enough for anything . 

 to fill up the time she collect her volume and piece of music , and lay they on the top of the piano , set herself to classify they . then catch the reflection of her movement in the glass panel , she be divert to the contemplation of the image there and walk toward it . dress in black , without a single ornament , and with the warm whiteness of her skin set off between her light - brown coronet of hair and her square - cut bodice , she might have tempt an artist to try again the roman trick of a statue in black , white , and tawny marble . see her image slowly advance , she think " I be beautiful " not exultingly , but with grave decision . be beautiful be after all the condition on which she most need external testimony . if any one object to the turn of her nose or the form of her neck and chin , she have not the sense that she could presently show her power of attainment in these branch of feminine perfection . 

 there be not much time to fill up in this way before the sound of wheel , the loud ring , and the opening door assure she that she be not by any accident to be disappoint . this slightly increase her inward flutter . in spite of her self - confidence , she dread Klesmer as part of that unmanageable world which be independent of she wish something vitriolic that would not cease to burn because you smile or frown at it . poor thing ! she be at a high crisis of her woman 's fate than in her last experience with Grandcourt . the questioning then , be whether she should take a particular man as a husband . the inmost fold of her questioning now be whether she need take a husband at all whether she could not achieve substantially for herself and know gratified ambition without bondage . 

 Klesmer make his most deferential bow in the wide doorway of the antechamber show also the deference of the fine gray kerseymere trouser and perfect glove   the ' master of those who know ' be happily altogether human . Gwendolen meet he with unusual gravity , and hold out her hand say , " it be most kind of you to come , Herr Klesmer . I hope you have not think I presumptuous . " 

 " I take your wish as a command that do I honor , " say Klesmer , with answer gravity . he be really put by his own affair in order to give his utmost attention to what Gwendolen might have to say ; but his temperament be still in a state of excitation from the event of yesterday , likely enough to give his expression a more than usually bite edge . 

 Gwendolen for once be under too great a strain of feeling to remember formality . she continue stand near the piano , and Klesmer take his stand near the other end of it with his back to the light and his terribly omniscient eye upon she . no affectation be of use , and she begin without delay . 

 " I wish to consult you , Herr Klesmer . we have lose all our fortune ; we have nothing . I must get my own bread , and I desire to provide for my mamma , so as to save she from any hardship . the only way I can think of and I should like it well than anything be to be an actress to go on the stage . but , of course , I should like to take a high position , and I think if you think I could " here Gwendolen become a little more nervous " it would be well for I to be a singer to study singing also . " 

 Klesmer put down his hat upon the piano , and fold his arm as if to concentrate himself . 

 " I know , " Gwendolen resume , turn from pale to pink and back again " I know that my method of singing be very defective ; but I have be ill taught . I could be well teach ; I could study . and you will understand my wish : to sing and act too , like Grisi , be a much high position . naturally , I should wish to take as high rank as I can . and I can rely on your judgment . I be sure you will tell I the truth . " 

 Gwendolen somehow have the conviction that now she make this serious appeal the truth would be favorable . 

 still Klesmer do not speak . he draw off his glove quickly , toss they into his hat , rest his hand on his hip , and walk to the other end of the room . he be fill with compassion for this girl : he want to put a guard on his speech . when he turn again , he look at she with a mild frown of inquiry , and say with gentle though quick utterance , " you have never see anything , I think , of artist and their life ? I mean of musician , actor , artist of that kind ? " 

 " oh , no , " say Gwendolen , not perturb by a reference to this obvious fact in the history of a young lady hitherto well provide for . 

 " you be pardon I , " say Klesmer , again pause near the piano " in come to a conclusion on such a matter as this , everything must be take into consideration you be perhaps twenty ? " 

 " I be twenty - one , " say Gwendolen , a slight fear rise in she . " do you think I be too old ? " 

 Klesmer pout his under lip and shake his long finger upward in a manner totally enigmatic . 

 " many person begin later than other , " say Gwendolen , betray by her habitual consciousness of have valuable information to bestow . 

 Klesmer take no notice , but say with more study gentleness than ever , " you have probably not think of an artistic career until now : you do not entertain the notion , the longing what shall I say ? you do not wish yourself an actress , or anything of that sort , till the present trouble ? " 

 " not exactly : but I be fond of act . I have act ; you see I , if you remember you see I here in charade , and as Hermione , " say Gwendolen , really fear that Klesmer have forget . 

 " yes , yes , " he answer quickly , " I remember I remember perfectly , " and again walk to the other end of the room , it be difficult for he to refrain from this kind of movement when he be in any argument either audible or silent . 

 Gwendolen feel that she be be weigh . the delay be unpleasant . but she do not yet conceive that the scale could dip on the wrong side , and it seem to she only graceful to say , " I shall be very much oblige to you for take the trouble to give I your advice , whatever it maybe . " 

 " Miss Harleth , " say Klesmer , turn toward she and speak with a slight increase of accent , " I will veil nothing from you in this matter . I should reckon myself guilty if I put a false visage on thing make they too black or too white . the god have a curse for he who willingly tell another the wrong road . and if I mislead one who be so young , so beautiful who , I trust , will find her happiness along the right road , I should regard myself as a BŲsewicht . " in the last word Klesmer 's voice have drop to a loud whisper . 

 Gwendolen feel a sinking of heart under this unexpected solemnity , and keep a sort of fascinated gaze on Klesmer 's face , as he go on . 

 " you be a beautiful young lady you have be bring up in ease you have do what you would you have not say to yourself , ' I must know this exactly , ' ' I must understand this exactly , ' ' I must do this exactly , ' " in utter these three terrible must , Klesmer lift up three long finger in succession . " in sum , you have not be call upon to be anything but a charming young lady , whom it be an impoliteness to find fault with . " 

 he pause an instant ; then rest his finger on his hip again , and thrust out his powerful chin , he say 

 " well , then , with that preparation , you wish to try the life of an artist ; you wish to try a life of arduous , uncease work , and uncertain praise . your praise would have to be earn , like your bread ; and both would come slowly , scantily what do I say ? they may hardly come at all . " 

 this tone of discouragement , which Klesmer have hop might suffice without anything more unpleasant , rouse some resistance in Gwendolen . with a slight turn of her head away from he , and an air of pique , she say 

 " I think that you , be an artist , would consider the life one of the most honorable and delightful . and if I can do nothing well ? I suppose I can put up with the same risk as other people do . " 

 " do nothing well ? " say Klesmer , a little fire . " no , my dear Miss Harleth , you could do nothing well neither man nor woman could do anything well if you could do what be best or good of its kind . I be not decry the life of the true artist . I be exalt it . I say , it be out of the reach of any but choice organization nature frame to love perfection and to labor for it ; ready , like all true lover , to endure , to wait , to say , I be not yet worthy , but she Art , my mistress be worthy , and I will live to merit she . an honorable life ? yes . but the honor come from the inward vocation and the hard - won achievement : there be no honor in don the life as a livery . " 

 some excitement of yesterday have revive in Klesmer and hurry he into speech a little aloof from his immediate friendly purpose . he have wish as delicately as possible to rouse in Gwendolen a sense of her unfitness for a perilous , difficult course ; but it be his will not to be angry with the pretension of incompetence , and he be in danger of get chafe . conscious of this , he pause suddenly . but Gwendolen 's chief impression be that he have not yet deny she the power of do what would be good of its kind . Klesmer 's fervor seem to be a sort of glamor such as he be prone to throw over thing in general ; and what she desire to assure he of be that she be not afraid of some preliminary hardship . the belief that to present herself in public on the stage must produce an effect such as she have be use to feel certain of in private life ; be like a bit of her flesh it be not to be peel off readily , but must come with blood and pain . she say , in a tone of some insistance 

 " I be quite prepared to bear hardship at first . of course no one can become celebrate all at once . and it be not necessary that every one should be first - rate either actress or singer . if you would be so kind as to tell I what step I should take , I shall have the courage to take they . I do not   mind go up hill . it will be easy than the dead level of be a governess . I will take any step you recommend . " 

 Klesmer be convince now that he must speak plainly . 

 " I will tell you the step , not that I recommend , but that will be force upon you . it be all one , so far , what your goal will be excellence , celebrity , second , third rateness it be all one . you must go to town under the protection of your mother . you must put yourself under training musical , dramatic , theatrical : whatever you desire to do you have to learn " here Gwendolen look as if she be go to speak , but Klesmer lift up his hand and say , decisively , " I know . you have exercise your talent you recite you sing from the drawing - room standpunkt . my dear Fršulein , you must unlearn all that . you have not yet conceive what excellence be : you must unlearn your mistaken admiration . you must know what you have to strive for , and then you must subdue your mind and body to unbroken discipline . your mind , I say . for you must not be think of celebrity : put that candle out of your eye , and look only at excellence . you would of course earn nothing you could get no engagement for a long while . you would need money for yourself and your family . but that , " here Klesmer frown and shake his finger as if to dismiss a triviality , " that could perhaps be find . " 

 Gwendolen turn pink and pale during this speech . her pride have feel a terrible knife - edge , and the last sentence only make the smart keener . she be conscious of appear move , and try to escape from her weakness by suddenly walk to a seat and point out a chair to Klesmer . he do not take it , but turn a little in order to face she and lean against the piano . at that moment she wish that she have not send for he : this first experience of be take on some other ground than that of her social rank and her beauty be become bitter to she . Klesmer , preoccupy with a serious purpose , go on without change of tone . 

 " now , what sort of issue might be fairly expect from all this self - denial ? you would ask that . it be right that your eye should be open to it . I will tell you truthfully . this issue would be uncertain , and , most probably , would not be worth much . " 

 at these relentless word Klesmer put out his lip and look through his spectacle with the air of a monster impenetrable by beauty . 

 Gwendolen 's eye begin to burn , but the dread of show weakness urge she to add self - control . she compel herself to say , in a hard tone 

 " you think I want talent , or be too old to begin . " 

 Klesmer make a sort of hum , and then descend on an emphatic " yes ! the desire and the training should have begin seven year ago or a good deal early . a mountebank 's child who help her father to earn shilling when she be six year old a child that inherit a singing throat from a long line of chorister and learn to sing as it learn to talk , have a likeli beginning . any great achievement in act or in music grow with the growth . whenever an artist have be able to say , ' I come , I see , I conquer , ' it have be at the end of patient practice . genius at first be little more than a great capacity for receive discipline . singing and acting , like the fine dexterity of the juggler with his cup and ball , require a shaping of the organ toward a fine and fine certainty of effect . your muscle your whole frame must go like a watch , true , true to a hair . that be the work of spring - time , before habit have be determine . " 

 " I do not pretend to genius , " say Gwendolen , still feel that she might somehow do what Klesmer want to represent as impossible . " I only suppose that I might have a little talent enough to improve . " 

 " I do not   deny that , " say Klesmer . " if you have be put in the right track some year ago and have work well you might now have make a public singer , though I do not   think your voice would have count for much in public . for the stage your personal charm and intelligence might then have tell without the present drawback of inexperience lack of discipline lack of instruction . " 

 certainly Klesmer seem cruel , but his feeling be the reverse of cruel . our speech , even when we be most single - minded , can never take its line absolutely from one impulse ; but Klesmer 's be , as far as possible , direct by compassion for poor Gwendolen 's ignorant eagerness to enter on a course of which he see all the miserable detail with a definiteness which he could not if he would have convey to her mind . 

 Gwendolen , however , be not convince . her self - opinion rally , and since the counselor whom she have call in give a decision of such severe peremptoriness , she be tempt to think that his judgment be not only fallible but biased . it occur to she that a simple and wise step for she to have take would have be to send a letter through the post to the manager of a London theatre , ask he to make an appointment . she would make no further reference to her singing ; Klesmer , she see , have set himself against her singing . but she feel equal to argue with he about she go on the stage , and she answer in a resistant tone 

 " I understand , of course , that no one can be a finished actress at once . it may be impossible to tell beforehand whether I should succeed ; but that seem to I a reason why I should try . I should have think that I might have take an engagement at a theatre meanwhile , so as to earn money and study at the same time . " 

 " can not   be do , my dear Miss Harleth I speak plainly it can not   be do . I must clear your mind of these notion which have no more resemblance to reality than a pantomime . lady and gentleman think that when they have make their toilet and draw on their glove they be as presentable on the stage as in a drawing - room . no manager think that . with all your grace and charm , if you be to present yourself as an aspirant to the stage , a manager would either require you to pay as an amateur for be allow to perform or he would tell you to go and be teach train to bear yourself on the stage , as a horse , however beautiful , must be train for the circus ; to say nothing of that study which would enable you to personate a character consistently , and animate it with the natural language of face , gesture , and tone . for you to get an engagement fit for you straight away be out of the question . " 

 " I really can not understand that , " say Gwendolen , rather haughtily then , check herself , she add in another tone " I shall be oblige to you if you will explain how it be that such poor actress get engage . I have be to the theatre several time , and I be sure there be actress who seem to I to act not at all well and who be quite plain . " 

 " ah , my dear Miss Harleth , that be the easy criticism of the buyer . we who buy slipper toss away this pair and the other as clumsy ; but there go an apprenticeship to the making of they . excuse I ; you could not at present teach one of those actress ; but there be certainly much that she could teach you . for example , she can pitch her voice so as to be hear : ten to one you could not do it till after many trial . merely to stand and move on the stage be an art require practice . it be understand that we be not now talk of a comparse in a petty theatre who earn the wage of a needle - woman . that be out of the question for you . "

I suppose I have no particular talent , but I must think it be an advantage , even on the stage , to be a lady and not a perfect fright . "

" I be speak of what you would have to go through if you aim at become a real artist if you take music and the drama as a high vocation in which you would strive after excellence . on that head , what I have say stand fast . you would find after your education in do thing slackly for one - and - twenty year great difficulty in study ; you would find mortification in the treatment you would get when you present yourself on the footing of skill . you would be subject to test ; people would no long feign not to see your blunder . you would at first only be accept on trial . you would have to bear what I may call a glare insignificance : any success must be win by the utmost patience . you would have to keep your place in a crowd , and after all it be likely you would lose it and get out of sight . if you determine to face these hardship and still try , you will have the dignity of a high purpose , even though you may have choose unfortunately . you will have some merit , though you may win no prize . you have ask my judgment on your chance of win . I do not   pretend to speak absolutely ; but measure probability , my judgment be : you will hardly achieve more than mediocrity . " 

 Klesmer have deliver himself with emphatic rapidity , and now pause a moment . Gwendolen be motionless , look at her hand , which lie over each other on her lap , till the deep - tone , long - draw " but , " with which he resume , have a startling effect , and make she look at he again . 

 " but there be certainly other idea , other disposition with which a young lady may take up an art that will bring she before the public . she may rely on the unquestioned power of her beauty as a passport . she may desire to exhibit herself to an admiration which dispense with skill . this go a certain way on the stage : not in music : but on the stage , beauty be take when there be nothing more commanding to be have . not without some drilling , however : as I have say before , technicality have in any case to be master . but these excepted , we have here nothing to do with art . the woman who take up this career be not an artist : she be usually one who think of enter on a luxurious life by a short and easy road perhaps by marriage that be her most brilliant chance , and the rare . still , her career will not be luxurious to begin with : she can hardly earn her own poor bread independently at once , and the indignity she will be liable to are such as I will not speak of .  Gwendolen 's face have begin to burn . that Klesmer be about to marry Miss Arrowpoint cause she no surprise , and at another moment she would have amuse herself in quickly imagine the scene that must have occur at Quetcham . but what engross her feeling , what fill her imagination now , be the panorama of her own immediate future that Klesmer 's word seem to have unfold . the suggestion of Miss Arrowpoint as a patroness be only another detail add to its repulsiveness : Klesmer 's proposal to help she seem an additional irritation after the humiliating judgment he have pass on her capability . his word have really bite into her self - confidence and turn it into the pain of a bleed wound ; and the idea of present herself before other judge be now poison with the dread that they also might be harsh ; they also would not recognize the talent she be conscious of . but she control herself , and rise from her seat before she make any answer . it seem natural that she should pause . she go to the piano and look absently at leaf of music , pinch up the corner . at last she turn toward Klesmer and say , with almost her usual air of proud equality , which in this interview have not be hitherto perceptible .

at home , at school , among acquaintance , she have be use to have her conscious superiority admit ; and she have move in a society where everything , from low arithmetic to high art , be of the amateur kind , politely suppose to fall short of perfection only because gentleman and lady be not oblige to do more than they like otherwise they would probably give forth abler writing , and show themselves more commanding artist than any the world be at present oblige to put up with . the self - confident vision that have beguile she be not of a highly exceptional kind ; and she have at least show some rationality in consult the person who know the most and have flatter she the least . in ask Klesmer 's advice , however , she have rather be bear up by a belief in his latent admiration than bent on know anything more unfavorable that might have lain behind his slight objection to her singing ; and the truth she have ask for , with an expectation that it would be agreeable , have come like a lacerate thong .

" say Gwendolen , aloud , start from her seat as she hear the step and voice of her mamma and sister come in from church . she hurry to the piano and begin gather together her piece of music with assume diligence , while the expression on her pale face and in her burn eye be what would have suit a woman endure a wrong which she might not resent , but would probably revenge .

I dread give way . help I to be quiet . "

 CHAPTER XXIV . 

  " I question thing but do not find 
  one that will answer to my mind : 
  and all the world appear unkind . " 
  WORDSWORTH . 
 Gwendolen be glad that she have get through her interview with Klesmer before meet her uncle and aunt . she have make up her mind now that there be only disagreeable before she , and she feel able to maintain a dogged calm in the face of any humiliation that might be propose . 

 the meeting do not happen until the Monday , when Gwendolen go to the rectory with her mamma . they have call at Sawyer 's Cottage by the way , and have see every cranny of the narrow room in a mid - day light , unsoftene by blind and curtain ; for the furnishing to be do by gleaning from the rectory have not yet begin . 

 " how shall you endure it , mamma ? " say Gwendolen , as they walk away . she have not open her lip while they be look round at the bare wall and floor , and the little garden with the cabbage - stalk , and the yew arbor all dust and cobwebs within . " you and the four girl all in that closet of a room , with the green and yellow paper press on your eye ? and without I ? " 

 " it will be some comfort that you have not to bear it too , dear . " 

 " if it be not that I must get some money , I would rather be there than go to be a governess . " 

 " do not   set yourself against it beforehand , Gwendolen . if you go to the palace you will have every luxury about you . and you know how much you have always care for that . you will not find it so hard as go up and down those steep narrow stair , and hear the crockery rattle through the house , and the dear girl talk . " 

 " it be like a bad dream , " say Gwendolen , impetuously . " I can not believe that my uncle will let you go to such a place . he ought to have take some other step . " 

 " do not   be unreasonable , dear child . what could he have do ? " 

 " that be for he to find out . it seem to I a very extraordinary world if people in our position must sink in this way all at once , " say Gwendolen , the other world with which she be conversant be construct with a sense of fitness that arrange her own future agreeably . 

 it be her temper that frame her sentence under this entirely new pressure of evil : she could have speak more suitably on the vicissitude in other people 's life , though it be never her aspiration to express herself virtuously so much as cleverly a point to be remember in extenuation of her word , which be usually bad than she be . 

 and , notwithstanding the keen sense of her own bruise , she be capable of some compunction when her uncle and aunt receive she with a more affectionate kindness than they have ever show before . she could not but be strike by the dignified cheerfulness with which they talk of the necessary economy in their way of living , and in the education of the boy . Mr. Gascoigne 's worth of character , a little obscure by worldly opportunity as the poetic beauty of woman be obscure by the demand of fashionable dressing show itself to great advantage under this sudden reduction of fortune . prompt and methodical , he have set himself not only to put down his carriage , but to reconsider his worn suit of clothe , to leave off meat for breakfast , to do without periodical , to get Edwy from school and arrange hour of study for all the boy under himself , and to order the whole establishment on the spare foot possible . for all healthy people economy have its pleasure ; and the rector 's spirit have spread through the household . Mrs. Gascoigne and Anna , who always make papa their model , really do not miss anything they care about for themselves , and in all sincerity feel that the sad part of the family loss be the change for Mrs. Davilow and her child .

 the rector smile . " do not   be alarm , my dear . she would like to have a more precise idea of you than my report can give . and a mother be naturally scrupulous about a companion for her daughter .  " I think so highly of the position , that I should have be tempt to try and get it for Anna , if she have be at all likely to meet Mrs. Mompert 's want . it be really a home , with a continuance of education in the high sense : ' governess ' be a misnomer . the bishop 's view be of a more decidedly low Church color than my own he be a close friend of Lord Grampian 's ; but , though privately strict , he be not by any mean narrow in public matter . indeed , he have create as little dislike in his diocese as any bishop on the bench . he have always remain friendly to I , though before his promotion , when he be an incumbent of this diocese , we have a little controversy about the Bible Society . " and Gwendolen 's memory of school suggest other particular which force she to admit to herself that this alternative would be no relief . she turn to her uncle again and say , apparently in acceptance of his idea

though here too she would certainly never be pet or have her susceptibility consult . her rebellion against this hard necessity which have come just to she of all people in the world to she whom all circumstance have concur in prepare for something quite different be exaggerate instead of diminish as one hour follow another , with the imagination of what she might have expect in her lot and what it be actually to be . the family trouble , she think , be easy for every one than for she even for poor dear mamma , because she have always use herself to not enjoy . as to hop that if she go to the Momperts ' and be patient a little while , thing might get well it would be stupid to entertain hope for herself after all that have happen : her talent , it appear , would never be recognize as anything remarkable , and there be not a single direction in which probability seem to flatter her wish . some beautiful girl who , like she , have read romance where even plain governess be centre of attraction and be seek in marriage , might have solace themselves a little by transport such picture into their own future ; but even if Gwendolen 's experience have lead she to dwell on love - making and marriage as her elysium , her heart be too much oppress by what be near to she , in both the past and the future , for she to project her anticipation very far off . she have a world - nausea upon she , and see no reason all through her life why she should wish to live . no religious view of trouble help she : her trouble have in her opinion all be cause by other people 's disagreeable or wicked conduct ; and there be really nothing pleasant to be count on in the world : that be her feeling ; everything else she have hear say about trouble be mere phrase - make not attractive enough for she to have catch it up and repeat it . as to the sweetness of labor and fulfilled claim ; the supreme worth of the teacher 's vocation ; these , even if they have be eloquently preach to she , could have be no more than faintly apprehend doctrine : the fact which work upon she be her invariable observation that for a lady to become a governess to " take a situation " be to descend in life and to be treat at well with a compassionate patronage . and poor Gwendolen have never dissociate happiness from personal pre - eminence and ťclat . that where these threaten to forsake she , she should take life to be hardly worth the having , can not make she so unlike the rest of we , man or woman , that we should cast she out of our compassion ; our moment of temptation to a mean opinion of thing in general be usually dependent on some susceptibility about ourselves and some dullness to subject which every one else would consider more important . surely a young creature be pitiable who have the labyrinth of life before she and no clue to whom distrust in herself and her good fortune have come as a sudden shock , like a rent across the path that she be treading carelessly .

 how trace the why and wherefore in a mind reduce to the barrenness of a fastidious egoism , in which all direct desire be dull , and have dwindle from motif into a vacillating expectation of motif : a mind make up of mood , where a fitful impulse spring here and there conspicuously rank amid the general weediness ?  CHAPTER XXVI . 

  he bring white ass laden with the freight

 Mr. Gascoigne one day come to Offendene with what he feel to be the satisfactory news that Mrs. Mompert have fix Tuesday in the following week for her interview with Gwendolen at Wanchester . he say nothing of his have incidentally hear that Mr. Grandcourt have return to diplow ; know no more than she do that Leubronn have be the goal of her admirer 's journeying , and feel that it would be unkind uselessly to revive the memory of a brilliant prospect under the present revers . in his secret soul he think of his niece 's unintelligible caprice with regret , but he vindicate she to himself by consider that Grandcourt have be the first to behave oddly , in suddenly walk away when there have the good opportunity for crown his marked attention . the rector 's practical judgment tell he that his chief duty to his niece now be to encourage she resolutely to face the change in her lot , since there be no manifest promise of any event that would avert it . 

 " you will find an interest in varied experience , my dear , and I have no doubt you will be a more valuable woman for have sustain such a part as you be call to . " 

 " I can not pretend to believe that I shall like it , " say Gwendolen , for the first time show her uncle some petulance . " but I be quite aware that I be oblige to bear it . " 

 she remember have submit to his admonition on a different occasion when she be expect to like a very different prospect . 

 " and your good sense will teach you to behave suitably under it , " say Mr. Gascoigne , with a shade more gravity . " I feel sure that Mrs. Mompert will be pleased with you . you will know how to conduct yourself to a woman who hold in all sens the relation of a superior to you . this trouble have come on you young , but that make it in some respect easy , and there be a benefit in all chastisement if we adjust our mind to it . " 

 this be precisely what Gwendolen be unable to do ; and after her uncle be go , the bitter tear , which have rarely come during the late trouble , rise and fall slowly as she sit alone . her heart deny that the trouble be easy because she be young . when be she to have any happiness , if it do not come while she be young ? not that her vision of possible happiness for herself be as unmixe with necessary evil as they use to be not that she could still imagine herself pluck the fruit of life without suspicion of their core . but this general disenchantment with the world nay , with herself , since it appear that she be not make for easy pre - eminence only intensify her sense of forlornness ; it be a visibly sterile distance enclose the dreary path at her foot , in which she have no courage to tread . she be in that first crisis of passionate youthful rebellion against what be not fitly call pain , but rather the absence of joy that first rage of disappointment in life 's morning , which we whom the year have subdue be apt to remember but dimly as part of our own experience , and so to be intolerant of its self - enclose unreasonableness and impiety . what passion seem more absurd , when we have get outside it and look at calamity as a collective risk , than this amazed anguish that I and not Thou , he or she , should be just the smite one ? yet perhaps some who have afterward make themselves a willing fence before the breast of another , and have carry their own heart - wound in heroic silence some who have make their deed great , nevertheless begin with this angry amazement at their own smart , and on the mere denial of their fantastic desire rag as if under the sting of wasp which reduce the universe for they to an unjust infliction of pain . this be nearly poor Gwendolen 's condition . what though such a reverse as hers have often happen to other girl ?  imagine a rambling , patchy house , the good part build of gray stone , and red - tile , a round tower jut at one of the corner , the mellow darkness of its conical roof surmount by a weather - cock make an agreeable object either amidst the gleam and greenth of summer or the low - hang cloud and snowy branch of winter : the ground shady with spread tree : a great tree flourish on one side , backward some Scotch fir on a broken bank where the root hang naked , and beyond , a rookery : on the other side a pool overhang with bush , where the water - fowl flutter and scream : all around , a vast meadow which might be call a park , border by an old plantation and guard by stone ledge which look like little prison . outside the gate the country , once entirely rural and lovely , now black with coal mine , be chiefly people by man and brother with candle stick in their hat , and with a diabolic complexion which lay they peculiarly open to suspicion in the eye of the child at Gadsmere Mrs. Glasher 's four beautiful child , who have dwell there for about three year . now , in November , when the flower - bed be empty , the tree leafless , and the pool blackly shivering , one might have say that the place be sombrely in keep with the black road and black mound which seem to put the district in mourning ; Most of those who remember the affair now wonder what have become of that Mrs. Glasher , whose beauty and brilliancy have make she rather conspicuous to they in foreign place , where she be know to be live with young Grandcourt .

Grandcourt have of course get weary of she . he be much give to the pursuit of woman : but a man in his position would by this time desire to make a suitable marriage with the fair young daughter of a noble house . no one talk of Mrs. Glasher now , any more than they talk of the victim in a trial for manslaughter ten year before : she be a lost vessel after whom nobody would send out an expedition of search ; but Grandcourt be see in harbor with his color fly , register as seaworthy as ever . 

 yet , in fact , Grandcourt have never disentangle himself from Mrs. Glasher . his passion for she have be the strong and most lasting he have ever know ; and though it be now as dead as the music of a crack flute , it have leave a certain dull disposedness , which , on the death of her husband three year before , have prompt in he a vacillating notion of marry she , in accordance with the understanding often express between they during the day of his first ardor . at that early time Grandcourt would willingly have pay for the freedom to be win by a divorce ; but the husband would not oblige he , not want to be marry again himself , and not wish to have his domestic habit print in evidence . 

 the altered poise which the year have bring in Mrs. Glasher be just the reverse . at first she be comparatively careless about the possibility of marriage . it be enough that she have escape from a disagreeable husband and find a sort of bliss with a lover who have completely fascinate her young , handsome , amorous , and live in the good style , with equipage and conversation of the kind to be expect in young man of fortune who have see everything . she be an impassione , vivacious woman , fond of adoration , exasperate by five year of marital rudeness ; and the sense of release be so strong upon she that it still anxiety for more than she actually enjoy . an equivocal position be of no importance to she then ; she have no envy for the honor of a dull , disregarded wife : the one spot which spoil her vision of her new pleasant world , be the sense that she leave her three - year - old boy , who die two year afterward , and whose first tone say " mamma " retain a difference from those of the child that come after . but now the year have bring many change besides those in the contour of her cheek and throat ; and that Grandcourt should marry she have become her dominant desire . the equivocal position which she have not mind about for herself be now tell upon she through her child , whom she love with a devotion charge with the add passion of atonement . she have no repentance except in this direction . if Grandcourt marry she , the child would be none the bad off for what have pass : they would see their mother in a dignified position , and they would be at no disadvantage with the world : her son could be make his father 's heir . it be the yearning for this result which give the supreme importance to Grandcourt 's feeling for she ; her love for he have long resolve itself into anxiety that he should give she the unique , permanent claim of a wife , and she expect no other happiness in marriage than the satisfaction of her maternal love and pride include her pride for herself in the presence of her child . for the sake of that result she be prepare even with a tragic firmness to endure anything quietly in marriage ; and she have acuteness enough to cherish Grandcourt 's flickering purpose negatively , by not molest he with passionate appeal and with scene - making . in she , as in every one else who want anything of he , his incalculable turn , and his tendency to harden under beseech , have create a reasonable dread : a slow discovery , of which no presentiment have be give in the bearing of a youthful lover with a fine line of face and the soft manner . but reticence have necessarily cost something to this impassioned woman , and she be the bitterer for it . there be no quailing even that force on the helpless and injure which have not an ugly obverse : the withhold sting be gather venom . she be absolutely dependent on Grandcourt ; for though he have be always liberal in expense for she , he have keep everything voluntary on his part ; and with the goal of marriage before she , she would ask for nothing less . he have say that he would never settle anything except by will ; and when she be think of alternative for the future it often occur to she that , even if she do not become Grandcourt 's wife , he might never have a son who would have a legitimate claim on he , and the end might be that her son would be make heir to the good part of his estate . no son at that early age could promise to have more of his father 's physique . but she become Grandcourt 's wife be so far from be an extravagant notion of possibility , that even Lush have entertain it , and have say that he would as soon bet on it as on any other likelihood with regard to his familiar companion . Lush , indeed , on infer that Grandcourt have a preconception of use his residence at diplow in order to win Miss Arrowpoint , have think it well to fan that project , take it as a tacit renunciation of the marriage with Mrs. Glasher , which have long be a mark for the hovering and wheeling of Grandcourt 's caprice . but both prospect have be negative by Gwendolen 's appearance on the scene ; and it be natural enough for Mrs. Glasher to enter with eagerness into Lush 's plan of hinder that new danger by set up a barrier in the mind of the girl who be be seek as a bride . she enter into it with an eagerness which have passion in it as well as purpose , some of the store - up venom deliver itself in that way .

 CHAPTER XXXI . 

  " a wild dedication of yourself 
  to unpath'd water , undreame shore . " 
  SHAKESPEARE . 
 on the day when Gwendolen Harleth be married and become Mrs. Grandcourt , the morning be clear and bright , and while the sun be low a slight frost crisp the leaf . the bridal party be worth see , and half Pennicote turn out to see it , line the pathway up to the church . an old friend of the rector 's perform the marriage ceremony , the rector himself act as father , to the great advantage of the procession . only two face , it be remark , show sign of sadness Mrs. Davilow 's and Anna 's . the mother 's delicate eyelid be pink , as if she have be cry half the night ; and no one be surprised that , splendid as the match be , she should feel the parting from a daughter who be the flower of her child and of her own life . it be less understand why Anna should be trouble when she be be so well set off by the bridesmaid 's dress . every one else seem to reflect the brilliancy of the occasion the bride most of all . of she it be agree that as to figure and carriage she be worthy to be a " lady o ' title " : as to face , perhaps it might be think that a title require something more rosy ; but the bridegroom himself not be fresh - colored being indeed , as the miller 's wife observe , very much of her own husband 's complexion the match be the more complete . anyhow he must be very fond of she ; and it be to be hop that he would never cast it up to she that she have be go out to service as a governess , and her mother to live at Sawyer 's cottage vicissitude which have be much speak of in the village . the miller 's daughter of fourteen could not believe that high gentry behave badly to their wife , but her mother instruct she " oh , child , man 's man : gentle or simple , they be much of a muchness . I have   hear my mother say Squire Pelton use to take his dog and a long whip into his wife 's room , and flog them there to frighten she ; and my mother be lady's - maid there at the very time . " 

 " that be unlucky talk for a wedding , Mrs. Girdle , " say the tailor . " a quarrel may end wi ' the whip , but it begin wi ' the tongue , and it be the woman have get the most o ' that . " 

 " the Lord give it them to use , I suppose , " say Mrs. Girdle . " he never mean you to have it all your own way . " 

 " by what I can make out from the gentleman as attend to the grooming at Offendene , " say the tailor , " this Mr. Grandcourt have wonderful little tongue . everything must be do dummy - like without his ordering . " 

 " then he be the more whip , I doubt , " say Mrs. Girdle . " she be get tongue enough , I warrant she . see , there they come out together ! " 

 " what wonderful long corner she be get to her eye ! " say the tailor . 
 " she make you feel comical when she look at you . " 
 Gwendolen , in fact , never show more elasticity in her bearing , more lustre in her long brown glance : she have the brilliancy of strong excitement , which will sometimes come even from pain . it be not pain , however , that she be feel : she have work herself up to much the same condition as that in which she stand at the gambling - table when Deronda be look at she , and she begin to lose . there be an enjoyment in it : whatever uneasiness a grow conscience have create be disregard as an ailment might have be , amidst the gratification of that ambitious vanity and desire for luxury within she which it would take a great deal of slow poisoning to kill . this morning she could not have say truly that she repent her acceptance of Grandcourt , or that any fear in hazy perspective could hinder the glow effect of the immediate scene in which she be the central object . that she be do something wrong that a punishment might be hang over she that the woman to whom she have give a promise and break it , be think of she in bitterness and misery with a just reproach that Deronda with his way of look into thing very likely despise she for marry Grandcourt , as he have despise she for gambling above all , that the cord which unite she with this lover and which she have heretofore hold by the hand , be now be fling over her neck , all this yeasty mingling of dimly understand fact with vague but deep impression , and with image half real , half fantastic , have be disturb she during the week of her engagement . be that agitate experience nullify this morning ? no : it be surmount and thrust down with a sort of exult defiance as she feel herself stand at the game of life with many eye upon she , dare everything to win much or if to lose , still with ťclat and a sense of importance . but this morning a lose destiny for herself do not press upon she as a fear : she think that she be enter on a full power of manage circumstance with all the official strength of marriage , which some woman make so poor a use of . that intoxication of youthful egoism out of which she have be shake by trouble , humiliation , and a new sense of culpability , have return upon she under a newly - feed strength of the old fume . she do not in the least present the ideal of the tearful , tremulous bride . Poor Gwendolen , whom some have judge much too forward and instruct in the world 's way ! with her erect head and elastic footstep she be walk among illusion ; and yet , too , there be an under - consciousness of she that she be a little intoxicated .

 there be a railway journey of some fifty mile before the new husband and wife reach the station near ryeland . the sky have veil itself since the morning , and it be hardly more than twilight when they enter the park - gate , but still Gwendolen , look out of the carriage - window as they drive rapidly along , could see the grand outline and the nearer beauty of the scene the long wind drive border with evergreen back by huge gray stem : then the opening of wide grassy space and undulation stud with dark clump ; till at last come a wide level where the white house could be see , with a hang wood for a back - ground , and the rise and sink balustrade of a terrace in front .

 CHAPTER XXXII . 

 in all age it hath be a favorite text that a potent love hath the nature of an isolated fatality , whereto the mind 's opinion and wonte resolve be altogether alien ; as , for example , Daphnis his frenzy , wherein it have little avail he to have be convince of Heraclitus his doctrine ; or the philtre - breed passion of Tristan , who , though he have be as deep as Duns Scotus , would have have his reasoning mar by that cup too much ; or Romeo in his sudden taking for Juliet , wherein any objection he might have hold against Ptolemy have make little difference to his discourse under the balcony . yet all love be not such , even though potent ; nay , this passion hath as large scope as any for ally itself with every operation of the soul : so that it shall acknowledge an effect from the imagine light of unproven firmament , and have its scale set to the grander orbit of what hath be and shall be . 

 Deronda , on his return to town , could assure Sir Hugo of his have lodge in Grandcourt 's mind a distinct understanding that he could get fifty thousand pound by give up a prospect which be probably distant , and not absolutely certain ; but he have no further sign of Grandcourt 's disposition in the matter than that he be evidently inclined to keep up friendly communication . 

 " and what do you think of the future bride on a nearer survey ? " say 
 Sir Hugo . 
 " I think well of she than I do in Leubronn . Roulette be not a good setting for she ; it bring out something of the demon . at Diplow she seem much more womanly and attractive less hard and self - possess . I think her mouth and eye have quite a different expression . "

 " I can stay in town , sir . " 

 " no , no . Lady Mallinger and the child can not   do without you at Christmas . only do not   make mischief unless you can get up a duel , and manage to shoot Grandcourt , which might be worth a little inconvenience . " 

 " I do not   think you ever see I flirt , " say Deronda , not amuse .

 look tenderly at the woman , and talk to they in a Jesuitical way . 
 you be a dangerous young fellow a kind of Lovelace who will make the

 what be the use of be exasperate at a tasteless joke ? only the exasperation come before the reflection on utility . few friendly remark be more annoying than the information that we be always seem to do what we never mean to do . Sir Hugo 's notion of flirt , it be to be hop , be rather peculiar ; for his own part , Deronda be sure that he have never flirt . but he be glad that the baronet have no knowledge about the repurchase of Gwendolen 's necklace to feed his taste for this kind of rallying . 

 he would be on his guard in future ; for example , in his behavior at Mrs. Meyrick 's , where he be about to pay his first visit since his arrival from Leubronn . for Mirah be certainly a creature in whom it be difficult not to show a tender kind of interest both by look and speech .

I have not hear a word from her lip that give I a doubt about she . she be quite contented and full of gratitude . my daughter be learn from she , and they hope to get her other pupil ; for she be anxious not to eat the bread of idleness , but to work , like my girl . " 

 but Mrs. Meyrick do not enter into particular which would have require she to say that Amy and Mab , who have accompany Mirah to the synagogue , find the jewish faith less reconcilable with their wish in her case than in that of Scott 's Rebecca . they keep silence out of delicacy to Mirah , with whom her religion be too tender a subject to be touch lightly ; but after a while Amy , who be much of a practical reformer , could not restrain a question . 

 " excuse I , Mirah , but do it seem quite right to you that the woman should sit behind rail in a gallery apart ? " 

 " yes , I never think of anything else , " say Mirah , with mild surprise . 

 " and you like well to see the man with their hat on ? " say Mab , cautiously propose the small item of difference . 

 " oh , yes . I like what I have always see there , because it bring back to I the same feeling the feeling I would not part with for anything else in the world . " 

 after this , any criticism , whether of doctrine or practice , would have seem to these generous little people an inhospitable cruelty . Mirah 's religion be of one fibre with her affection , and have never present itself to she as a set of proposition . 

 " she say herself she be a very bad jewess , and do not half know her people 's religion , " say Amy , when Mirah be go to bed . " perhaps it would gradually melt away from she , and she would pass into Christianity like the rest of the world , if she get to love we very much , and never find her mother . it be so strange to be of the Jews ' religion now . "

" I believe Mirah be cut out after the pattern of her mother . and what a joy it would be to she to have such a daughter bring back again ! but a mother 's feeling be not worth reckon , I suppose "   she shoot a mischievous glance at her own daughter , " and a dead mother be worth more that a live one ? "

 not only the Meyricks , whose various knowledge have be acquire by the irregular foraging to which clever girl have usually be reduce , but Deronda himself , with all his masculine instruction , have be rouse by this apparition of Mirah to the consciousness of know hardly anything about modern Judaism or the inner jewish history . the Chosen People have be commonly treat as a people choose for the sake of somebody else ; and their thinking as something   no matter exactly what   that ought to have be entirely otherwise ; and Deronda , like his neighbor , have regard Judaism as a sort of eccentric fossilize form which an accomplished man might dispense with study , and leave to specialist . but Mirah , with her terrified flight from one parent , and her yearn after the other , have flash on he the hitherto neglect reality that Judaism be something still throb in human life , still make for they the only conceivable vesture of the world ; and in the idle excursion on which he immediately afterward set out with Sir Hugo he begin to look for the outside of synagogue , and the title of book about the Jews . this awakening of a new interest this pass from the supposition that we hold the right opinion on a subject we be careless about , to a sudden care for it , and a sense that our opinion be ignorance be an effectual remedy for ennui , which , unhappily , can not be secure on a physician 's prescription ; but Deronda have carry it with he , and endure his week of lounge all the well . it be on this journey that he first enter a jewish synagogue at Frankfort where his party rest on a Friday . in explore the Juden - gasse , which he have see long before , he remember well enough its picturesque old house ; what his eye chiefly dwelt on now be the human type there ; and his thought , busily connect they with the past phase of their race , stir that fibre of historic sympathy which have help to determine in he certain trait worth mention for those who be interested in his future . true , when a young man have a fine person , no eccentricity of manner , the education of a gentleman , and a present income , it be not customary to feel a pry curiosity about his way of thinking , or his peculiar taste . he may very well be settle in life as an agreeable clever young fellow without pass a special examination on those head . later , when he be get rather slovenly and portly , his peculiarity be more distinctly discern , and it be take as a mercy if they be not highly objectionable . but any one wish to understand the effect of after - event on Deronda should know a little more of what he be at five - and - twenty than be evident in ordinary intercourse . 

 it happen that the very vividness of his impression have often make he the more enigmatic to his friend , and have contribute to an apparent indefiniteness in his sentiment . his early - waken sensibility and reflectiveness have develop into a many - sided sympathy , which threaten to hinder any persistent course of action : as soon as he take up any antagonism , though only in thought , he seem to himself like the Sabine warrior in the memorable story with nothing to meet his spear but flesh of his flesh , and object that he love . his imagination have so work itself to the habit of see thing as they probably appear to other , that a strong partisanship , unless it be against an immediate oppression , have become an insincerity for he . his plenteous , flexible sympathy have end by fall into one current with that reflective analysis which tend to neutralize sympathy . few man be able to keep themselves clear of vice than he ; yet he hat vice mildly , be use to think of they less in the abstract than as a part of mixed human nature have an individual history , which it be the bent of his mind to trace with understanding and pity . with the same innate balance he be fervidly democratic in his feeling for the multitude , and yet , through his affection and imagination , intensely conservative ; voracious of speculation on government and religion , yet loth to part with long - sanction form which , for he , be quick with memory and sentiment that no argument could lay dead . we fall on the lean side ; and Deronda suspect himself of love too well the lose cause of the world . martyrdom change side , and he be in danger of change with it , have a strong repugnance to take up that clue of success which the order of the world often force upon we and make it treason against the common weal to reject . and yet his fear of fall into an unreasone narrow hatred make a check for he : he apologize for the heir of privilege ; he shrink with dislike from the loser 's bitterness and the denunciatory tone of the unaccepted innovator . a too reflective and diffusive sympathy be in danger of paralyze in he that indignation against wrong and that selectness of fellowship which be the condition of moral force ; and in the last few year of confirm manhood he have become so keenly aware of this that what he most long for be either some external event , or some inward light , that would urge he into a definite line of action , and compress his wandering energy . he be cease to care for knowledge he have no ambition for practice unless they could both be gather up into one current with his emotion ; and he dread , as if it be a dwelling - place of lose soul , that dead anatomy of culture which turn the universe into a mere ceaseless answer to query , and know , not everything , but everything else about everything as if one should be ignorant of nothing concern the scent of violet except the scent itself for which one have no nostril . but how and whence be the needed event to come ? the influence that would justify partiality , and make he what he long to be , yet be unable to make himself an organic part of social life , instead of roam in it like a yearn disembody spirit , stir with a vague social passion , but without fix local habitation to render fellowship real ? to make a little difference for the well be what he be not content to live without ; but how to make it ? it be one thing to see your road , another to cut it . he find some of the fault in his birth and the way he have be bring up , which have lay no special demand on he and have give he no fixed relationship except one of a doubtful kind ; but he do not attempt to hide from himself that he have fall into a meditative numbness , and be glide far and far from that life of practically energetic sentiment which he would have proclaim   if he have be incline to proclaim anything   to be the good of all life , and for himself the only way worth live . he want some way of keep emotion and its progeny of sentiment which make the savor of life substantial and strong in the face of a reflectiveness that threaten to nullify all difference . to pound the object of sentiment into small dust , yet keep sentiment alive and active , be something like the famous recipe for make cannon to first take a round hole and then enclose it with iron ; whatever you do keep fast hold of your round hole . yet how distinguish what our will may wisely save in its completeness , from the heaping of cat - mummy and the expensive cult of enshrined putrefaction ? 

 something like this be the common under - current in Deronda 's mind while he be read law or imperfectly attend to polite conversation . meanwhile he have not set about one function in particular with zeal and steadiness . not an admirable experience , to be propose as an ideal ; but a form of struggle before break of day which some young man since the patriarch have have to pass through , with more or less of bruise if not lam . 

 I have say that under his calm exterior he have a fervor which make he easily feel the presence of poetry in everyday event ; and the form of the Juden - gasse , rouse the sense of union with what be remote , set he muse on two element of our historic life which that sense raise into the same region of poetry ; the faint beginning of faith and institution , and their obscure lingering decay ; the dust and wither remnant with which they be apt to be cover , only enhance for the awakened perception the impressiveness either of a sublimely penetrate life , as in the twin green leaf that will become the shelter tree , or of a pathetic inheritance in which all the grandeur and the glory have become a sorrow memory . 

 this imaginative stirring , as he turn out of the Juden - gasse , and continue to saunter in the warm evening air , mean to find his way to the synagogue , neutralize the repellent effect of certain ugly little incident on his way . turn into an old book - shop to ask the exact time of service at the synagogue , he be affectionately direct by a precocious jewish youth , who enter cordially into his wanting , not the fine new building of the Reformed but the old rabbinical school of the orthodox ; and then cheat he like a pure Teuton , only with more amenity , in his charge for a book quite out of request as one " nicht so leicht zu bekommen . " meanwhile at the opposite counter a deaf and grisly tradesman be cast a flinty look at certain card , apparently combine advantage of business with religion , and shoutingly propose to he in Jew - dialect by a dingy man in a tall coat hang from neck to heel , a bag in hand , and a broad low hat surmount his choose nose who have no soon disappear than another dingy man of the same pattern issue from the background gloom of the shop and also shout in the same dialect . in fact , Deronda see various queer - look Israelites not altogether without guile , and just distinguishable from queer - look Christians of the same mixed morale . in his anxiety about Mirah 's relative , he have lately be think of vulgar Jews with a sort of personal alarm . but a little comparison will often diminish our surprise and disgust at the aberration of Jews and other dissident whose life do not offer a consistent or lovely pattern of their creed ; and this evening Deronda , become more conscious that he be fall into unfairness and ridiculous exaggeration , begin to use that corrective comparison : he pay his thaler too much , without prejudice to his interest in the Hebrew destiny , or his wish to find the Rabbinische Schule , which he arrive at by sunset , and enter with a good congregation of man . 

 he happen to take his seat in a line with an elderly man from whom he be distant enough to glance at he more than once as rather a noticeable figure his ample white beard and feel hat frame a profile of that fine contour which may as easily be italian as Hebrew . he return Deronda 's notice till at last their eye meet ; an undesirable chance with unknown person , and a reason to Deronda for not look again ; but he immediately find an open prayer - book push toward he and have to bow his thank . however , the congregation have muster , the reader have mount to the almemor or platform , and the service begin . Deronda , have look enough at the german translation of the Hebrew in the book before he to know that he be chiefly hear Psalms and Old Testament passage or phrase , give himself up to that strong effect of chant liturgy which be independent of detailed verbal meaning like the effect of an Allegri 's Miserere or a Palestrina 's Magnificat . the most powerful movement of feel with a liturgy be the prayer which seek for nothing special , but be a yearning to escape from the limitation of our own weakness and an invocation of all good to enter and abide with we ; or else a self - oblivious lifting up of Gladness , a Gloria in excelsis that such good exist ; both the yearning and the exaltation gather their utmost force from the sense of communion in a form which have express they both , for long generation of struggle fellow - man . the Hebrew liturgy , like other , have its transition of litany , lyric , proclamation , dry statement and blessing ; but this evening , all be one for Deronda : the chant of the Chazaris or Reader 's grand wide - range voice with its passage from monotony to sudden cry , the outburst of sweet boy ' voice from the little choir , the devotional swaying of man 's body backward and forward , the very commonness of the building and shabbiness of the scene where a national faith , which have penetrate the thinking of half the world , and mould the splendid form of that world 's religion , be find a remote , obscure echo all be blent for he as one expression of a bind history , tragic and yet glorious . he wonder at the strength of his own feeling ; it seem beyond the occasion what one might imagine to be a divine influx in the darkness , before there be any vision to interpret . the whole scene be a coherent strain , its burden a passionate regret , which , if he have know the liturgy for the Day of Reconciliation , he might have clothe in its authentic burden ; " happy the eye which see all these thing ; but verily to hear only of they afflict our soul . happy the eye that see our temple and the joy of our congregation ; but verily to hear only of they afflict our soul . happy the eye that see the finger when tune every kind of song ; but verily to hear only of they afflict our soul . " 

 but with the cessation of the devotional sound and the movement of many indifferent face and vulgar figure before he there dart into his mind the frigid idea that he have probably be alone in his feeling , and perhaps the only person in the congregation for whom the service be more than a dull routine . there be just time for this chilling thought before he have bow to his civil neighbor and be move away with the rest when he feel a hand on his arm , and turn with the rather unpleasant sensation which this abrupt sort of claim be apt to bring , he see close to he the white - bearded face of that neighbor , who say to he in German , " excuse I , young gentleman allow I what be your parentage your mother 's family her maiden name ? "

 the questioner look at he dubiously still for an instant , then just lift his hat and turn away ; whether under a sense of have make a mistake or of have be repulse , Deronda be uncertain . in his walk back to the hotel he try to still any uneasiness on the subject by reflect that he could not have act differently . how could he say that he do not know the name of his mother 's family to that total stranger ? who indeed have take an unwarrantable liberty in the abruptness of his question , dictate probably by some fancy of likeness such as often occur without real significance . the incident , he say to himself , be trivial ; but whatever import it might have , his inward shrink on the occasion be too strong for he to be sorry that he have cut it short . it be a reason , however , for his not mention the synagogue to the Mallingers in addition to his usual inclination to reticence on anything that the baronet would have be likely to call quixotic enthusiasm . hardly any man could be more good - natured than Sir Hugo ; indeed in his kindliness especially to woman , he do action which other would have call romantic ; but he never take a romantic view of they , and in general smile at the introduction of motif on a grand scale , or of reason that lie very far off . this be the point of strong difference between he and Deronda , who rarely eat at breakfast without some silent discursive flight after ground for fill up his day accord to the practice of his contemporary . 

 this halt at Frankfort be take on their way home , and its impression be keep the more actively vibrate in he by the duty of care for Mirah 's welfare . that question about his parentage , which if he have not both inwardly and outwardly shake it off as trivial , would have seem a threat rather than a promise of revelation , and reinforce his anxiety as to the effect of find Mirah 's relative and his resolve to proceed with caution . if he make any unpleasant discovery , be he bind to a disclosure that might cast a new net of trouble around she ? he have write to Mrs. Meyrick to announce his visit at four o'clock , and he find Mirah seat at work with only Mrs. Meyrick and Mab , the open piano , and all the glorious company of engraving . the dainty neatness of her hair and dress , the glow of tranquil happiness in a face where a painter need have change nothing if he have want to put it in front of the host sing " peace on earth and good will to man , " make a contrast to his first vision of she that be delightful to Deronda 's eye . Mirah herself be think of it , and immediately on their greeting say
