 " I agree with Mirah , " say Mrs. Meyrick . " Saint Anybody be a bad saint to pray to . " 

 " besides , anybody could not have bring I to you , " say Mirah , smile at Mrs. Meyrick . " and I would rather be with you than with any one else in the world except my mother . I wonder if ever a poor little bird , that be lose and could not fly , be take and put into a warm nest where be a mother and sister who take to it so that everything come naturally , as if it have be always there . I hardly think before that the world could ever be as happy and without fear as it be to I now . " she look meditative a moment , and then say , ' sometimes I be a little afraid . " 

 " what be it you be afraid of ? " say Deronda with anxiety . 

 " that when I be turn at the corner of a street I may meet my father . it seem dreadful that I should be afraid of meet he . that be my only sorrow , " say Mirah , plaintively . 

 " it be surely not very probable , " say Deronda , wish that it be less so ; then , not to let the opportunity escape " would it be a great grief to you now if you be never to meet your mother ? " 

 she do not answer immediately , but meditate again , with her eye fix on the opposite wall . then she turn they on Deronda and say firmly , as if she have arrive at the exact truth , " I want she to know that I have always love she , and if she be alive I want to comfort she . she may be dead . if she be I should long to know where she be bury ; and to know whether my brother live , so that we can remember she together . but I will try not to grieve . I have think much for so many year of she be dead . and I shall have she with I in my mind , as I have always have . we can never be really part . I think I have never sin against she . I have always try not to do what would hurt she . only , she might be sorry that I be not a good Jewess . " 

 " in what way be you not a good Jewess ? " say Deronda . 

 " I be ignorant , and we never observe the law , but live among Christians just as they do . but I have hear my father laugh at the strictness of the Jews about their food and all custom , and their not like Christians . I think my mother be strict ; but she could never want I not to like those who be well to I than any of my own people I have ever know . I think I could obey in other thing that she wish but not in that . it be so much easy to I to share in love than in hatred . I remember a play I read in German since I have be here it have come into my mind where the heroine say something like that . " 

 " Antigone , " say Deronda . 

 " ah , you know it . but I do not believe that my mother would wish I not to love my good friend . she would be grateful to they . " here Mirah have turn to Mrs. Meyrick , and with a sudden lighting up of her whole countenance , she say , " oh , if we ever do meet and know each other as we be now , so that I could tell what would comfort she I should be so full of blessedness my soul would know no want but to love she ! " 

 " God bless you , child ! " say Mrs. Meyrick , the word escape involuntarily from her motherly heart . but to relieve the strain of feel she look at Deronda and say , " it be curious that Mirah , who remember her mother so well it be as if she see she , can not recall her brother the least bit except the feeling of have be carry by he when she be tired , and of his be near she when she be in her mother 's lap . it must be that he be rarely at home . he be already grow up . it be a pity her brother should be quite a stranger to she . " 

 " he be good ; I feel sure Ezra be good , " say Mirah , eagerly . " he love my mother he would take care of she . I remember more of he than that . I remember my mother 's voice once call , ' Ezra ! ' and then his answering from a distance ' Mother ! ' " Mirah have change her voice a little in each of these word and have give they a love intonation " and then he come close to we . I feel sure he be good . I have always take comfort from that . " 

 it be impossible to answer this either with agreement or doubt . Mrs. Meyrick and Deronda exchange a quick glance : about this brother she feel as painfully dubious as he do . but Mirah go on , absorb in her memory 

 " be it not wonderful how I remember the voice well than anything else ? I think they must go deeply into we than other thing . I have often fancy heaven might be make of voice . " 

 " like your singing yes , " say Mab , who have hitherto keep a modest silence , and now speak bashfully , as be she will not in the presence of Prince Camaralzaman " Ma , do ask Mirah to sing . Mr. Deronda have not hear she . " 

 " would it be disagreeable to you to sing now ? " say Deronda , with a more deferential gentleness than he have ever be conscious of before . 

 " oh , I shall like it , " say Mirah . " my voice have come back a little with rest . " 

 perhaps her ease of manner be due to something more than the simplicity of her nature . the circumstance of her life make she think of everything she do as work demand from she , in which affectation have nothing to do ; and she have begin her work before self - consciousness be bear . 

 she immediately rise and go to the piano a somewhat worn instrument that seem to get the well of its infirmity under the firm touch of her small finger as she prelude . Deronda place himself where he could see she while she sing ; and she take everything as quietly as if she have be a child go to breakfast . 

 imagine she it be always good to imagine a human creature in whom bodily loveliness seem as properly one with the entire being as the bodily loveliness of those wondrous transparent orb of life that we find in the sea imagine she with her dark hair brush from her temple , but yet show certain tiny ring there which have cunningly find their own way back , the mass of it hang behind just to the nape of the little neck in curly fibre , such as renew themselves at their own will after be bath into straightness like that of water - grass . then see the perfect cameo her profile make , cut in a duskish shell , where by some happy fortune there pierce a gem - like darkness for the eye and eyebrow ; the delicate nostril define enough to be ready for sensitive movement , the finished ear , the firm curve of the chin and neck , enter into the expression of a refinement which be not feebleness . 

 she sing Beethoven 's " per pieta non dirmi addio " with a subdued but search pathos which have that essential of perfect singing , the make one oblivious of art or manner , and only possess one with the song . it be the sort of voice that give the impression of be mean like a bird 's wooing for an audience near and beloved . Deronda begin by look at she , but feel himself presently cover his eye with his hand , want to seclude the melody in darkness ; then he refrain from what might seem oddity , and be ready to meet the look of mute appeal which she turn toward he at the end . 

 " I think I never enjoy a song more than that , " he say , gratefully . 

 " you like my singing ? I be so glad , " she say , with a smile of delight . " it have be a great pain to I , because it fail in what it be want for . but now we think I can use it to get my bread . I have really be teach well . and now I have two pupil , that Miss Meyrick find for I . they pay I nearly two crown for their two lesson . " 

 " I think I know some lady who would find you many pupil after Christmas , " say Deronda . " you would not mind sing before any one who wish to hear you ? " 

 " oh no , I want to do something to get money . I could teach reading and speak , Mrs. Meyrick think . but if no one would learn of I , that be difficult . " Mirah smile with a touch of merriment he have not see in she before . " I dare say I should find her poor I mean my mother . I should want to get money for she . and I can not always live on charity ; though " here she turn so as to take all three of her companion in one glance " it be the sweetest charity in all the world . " 

 " I should think you can get rich , " say Deronda , smile . " great lady will perhaps like you to teach their daughter , we shall see . but now do sing again to we . " 

 she go on willingly , sing with ready memory various thing by Gordigiani and Schubert ; then , when she have leave the piano , Mab say , entreatingly , " oh , Mirah , if you would not mind sing the little hymn . " 

 " it be too childish , " say Mirah . " it be like lisping . " 

 " what be the hymn ? " say Deronda . 

 " it be the hebrew hymn she remember her mother sing over she when she lie in her cot , " say Mrs. Meyrick . 

 " I should like very much to hear it , " say Deronda , " if you think I be worthy to hear what be so sacred . " 

 " I will sing it if you like , " say Mirah , " but I do not   sing real word only here and there a syllable like hers the rest be lisp . do you know Hebrew ? because if you do , my singing will seem childish nonsense . " 

 Deronda shake his head . " it will be quite good Hebrew to I . " 

 Mirah cross her little foot and hand in her easy attitude , and then lift up her head at an angle which seem to be direct to some invisible face bent over she , while she sing a little hymn of quaint melancholy interval , with syllable that really seem childish lisping to her audience ; the voice in which she give it forth have gather even a sweeter , more cooing tenderness than be hear in her other song . 

 " if I be ever to know the real word , I should still go on in my old way with they , " say Mirah , when she have repeat the hymn several time . 

 " why not ? " say Deronda . " the lisp syllable be very full of meaning . " 

 " yes , indeed , " say Mrs. Meyrick . " a mother hear something of a lisp in her child 's talk to the very last . their word be not just what everybody else say , though they may be spell the same . if I be to live till my Hans get old , I should still see the boy in he . a mother 's love , I often say , be like a tree that have get all the wood in it , from the very first it make . " 

 " be not that the way with friendship , too ? " say Deronda , smile . " we must not let the mother be too arrogant . " 

 the little woman shake her head over her darning . 

 " it be easy to find an old mother than an old friend . friendship begin with liking or gratitude root that can be pull up . Mother 's love begin deeply down . " 

 " like what you be say about the influence of voice , " say Deronda , look at Mirah . " I do not   think your hymn would have have more expression for I if I have know the word . I go to the synagogue at Frankfort before I come home , and the service impress I just as much as if I have follow the word perhaps more . " 

 " oh , be it great to you ? do it go to your heart ? " say Mirah , eagerly . " I think none but our people would feel that . I think it be all shut away like a river in a deep valley , where only heaven see I mean - " she hesitate feeling that she could not disentangle her thought from its imagery . 

 " I understand , " say Deronda . " but there be not really such a separation deeply down , as Mrs. Meyrick say . our religion be chiefly a hebrew religion ; and since Jews be man , their religious feeling must have much in common with those of other man just as their poetry , though in one sense peculiar , have a great deal in common with the poetry of other nation . still it be to be expect that a Jew would feel the form of his people 's religion more than one of another race and yet " here Deronda hesitate in his turn " that be perhaps not always so . " 

 " ah no , " say Mirah , sadly . " I have see that . I have see they mock . be it not like mock your parent ? like rejoice in your parent ' shame ? " 

 " some mind naturally rebel against whatever they be bring up in , and like the opposite ; they see the fault in what be near to they , " say Deronda apologetically . 

 " but you be not like that , " say Mirah , look at he with unconscious fixedness . 

 " no , I think not , " say Deronda ; " but you know I be not bring up as a Jew . " 

 " ah , I be always forget , " say Mirah , with a look of disappointed recollection , and slightly blush . 

 Deronda also feel rather embarrassed , and there be an awkward pause , which he put an end to by say playfully 

 " whichever way we take it , we have to tolerate each other ; for if we all go in opposition to our teaching , we must end in difference , just the same . " 

 " to be sure . we should go on forever in zig - zags , " say Mrs. Meyrick . " I think it be very weak - minded to make your creed up by the rule of the contrary . still one may honor one 's parent , without follow their notion exactly , any more than the exact cut of their clothing . my father be a Scotch Calvinist and my mother be a french Calvinist ; I be neither quite Scotch , nor quite french , nor two Calvinists roll into one , yet I honor my parent ' memory . " 

 " but I could not make myself not a Jewess , " say Mirah , insistently , " even if I change my belief . " 

 " no , my dear . but if Jews and Jewesses go on change their religion , and make no difference between themselves and Christians , there would come a time when there would be no Jews to be see , " say Mrs. Meyrick , take that consummation very cheerfully . 

 " oh , please not to say that , " say Mirah , the tear gather . " it be the first unkind thing you ever say . I will not begin that . I will never separate myself from my mother 's people . I be force to fly from my father ; but if he come back in age and weakness and want , and need I , should I say , ' this be not my father ' ? if he have shame , I must share it . it be he who be give to I for my father , and not another . and so it be with my people . I will always be a Jewess . I will love Christians when they be good , like you . but I will always cling to my people . I will always worship with they . " 

 as Mirah have go on speak she have become possess with a sorrowful passion fervent , not violent . hold her little hand tightly clasped and look at Mrs. Meyrick with beseech , she seem to Deronda a personification of that spirit which impel man after a long inheritance of profess Catholicism to leave wealth and high place and risk their life in flight , that they might join their own people and say , " I be a Jew . " 

 " Mirah , Mirah , my dear child , you mistake I ! " say Mrs. Meyrick , alarmed . " God forbid I should want you to do anything against your conscience . I be only say what might be if the world go on . but I have well have leave the world alone , and not want to be over - wise . forgive I , come ! we will not try to take you from anybody you feel have more right to you . " 

 " I would do anything else for you . I owe you my life , " say Mirah , not yet quite calm . 

 " hush , hush , now , " say Mrs. Meyrick . " I have be punish enough for wag my tongue foolishly make an almanac for the Millennium , as my husband use to say . " 

 " but everything in the world must come to an end some time . we must bear to think of that , " say Mab , unable to hold her peace on this point . she have already suffer from a bondage of tongue which threaten to become severe if Mirah be to be too much indulge in this inconvenient susceptibility to innocent remark . 

 Deronda smile at the irregular , blonde face , bring into strange contrast by the side of Mirah 's smile , Mab think , rather sarcastically as he say , " that ' prospect of everything come to an end will not guide we far in practice . Mirah 's feeling , she tell we , be concern with what be . " 

 Mab be confused and wish she have not speak , since Mr. Deronda seem to think that she have find fault with Mirah ; but to have speak once be a tyrannous reason for speak again , and she say 

 " I only mean that we must have courage to hear thing , else there be hardly anything we can talk about . " Mab feel herself unanswerable here , incline to the opinion of Socrates : " what motive have a man to live , if not for the pleasure of discourse ? " 

 Deronda take his leave soon after , and when Mrs. Meyrick go outside with he to exchange a few word about Mirah , he say , " Hans be to share my chamber when he come at Christmas . " 

 " you have write to Rome about that ? " say Mrs. Meyrick , her face light up . " how very good and thoughtful of you ! you mention Mirah , then ? " 

 " yes , I refer to she . I conclude he know everything from you . " 

 " I must confess my folly . I have not yet write a word about she . I have always be mean to do it , and yet have end my letter without say a word . and I tell the girl to leave it to I . however ! thank you a thousand time . " 

 Deronda divine something of what be in the mother 's mind , and his divination reinforce a certain anxiety already present in he . his inward colloquy be not soothe . he say to himself that no man could see this exquisite creature without feel it possible to fall in love with she ; but all the fervor of his nature be engage on the side of precaution . there be personage who feel themselves tragic because they march into a palpable morass , drag another with they , and then cry out against all the god . Deronda 's mind be strongly set against imitate they . 

 " I have my hand on the rein now , " he think , " and I will not drop they . I shall go there as little as possible . " 

 he see the reason act themselves out before he . how could he be Mirah 's guardian and claim to unite with Mrs. Meyrick , to whose charge he have commit she , if he show himself as a lover whom she do not love whom she would not marry ? and if he encourage any germ of lover 's feeling in himself it would lead up to that issue . Mirah 's be not a nature that would bear divide against itself ; and even if love win her consent to marry a man who be not of her race and religion , she would never be happy in act against that strong native bias which would still reign in her conscience as remorse . 

 Deronda see these consequence as we see any danger of mar our own work well begin . it be a delight to have rescue this child acquaint with sorrow , and to think of have place her little foot in protect path . the creature we help to save , though only a half - rear linnet , bruise and lose by the wayside how we watch and fence it , and dote on its sign of recovery ! our pride become love , our self be a not - self for whose sake we become virtuous , when we set to some hide work of reclaim a life from misery and look for our triumph in the secret joy " this one be the well for I . " 

 " I would as soon hold out my finger to be bite off as set about spoil her peace , " say Deronda . " it be one of the rare bit of fortune that I should have have friend like the Meyricks to place she with generous , delicate friend without any loftiness in their way , so that her dependence on they be not only safety but happiness . there could be no refuge to replace that , if it be break up . but what be the use of my take the vow and settle everything as it should be , if that marplot Hans come and upset it all ? " 

 few thing be more likely . Hans be make for mishap : his very limb seem more breakable than other people 's his eye more of a resort for uninvited fly and other irritate guest . but it be impossible to forbid Hans 's come to London . he be intend to get a studio there and make it his chief home ; and to propose that he should defer come on some ostensible ground , conceal the real motive of win time for Mirah 's position to become more confirmed and independent , be impracticable . have no other resource Deronda try to believe that both he and Mrs. Meyrick be foolishly trouble themselves about one of those endless thing call probability , which never occur ; but he do not quite succeed in his trying ; on the contrary , he find himself go inwardly through a scene where on the first discovery of Han 's inclination he give he a very energetic warning suddenly check , however , by the suspicion of personal feeling that his warmth might be create in Hans . he could come to no result , but that the position be peculiar , and that he could make no further provision against danger until they come nearer . to save an unhappy Jewess from drown herself , would not have seem a startling variation among police report ; but to discover in her so rare a creature as Mirah , be an exceptional event which might well bring exceptional consequence . Deronda would not let himself for a moment dwell on any supposition that the consequence might enter deeply into his own life . the image of Mirah have never yet have that penetrate radiation which would have be give to it by the idea of her love he . when this sort of effluence be absent from the fancy   whether from the fact or not   a man may go far in devotedness without perturbation . 

 as to the search for Mirah 's mother and brother , Deronda take what she have say to - day as a warrant for defer any immediate measure . his conscience be not quite easy in this desire for delay , any more than it be quite easy in his not attempt to learn the truth about his own mother : in both case he feel that there might be an unfulfilled duty to a parent , but in both case there be an overpowering repugnance to the possible truth , which throw a turning weight into the scale of argument . 

 " at least , I will look about , " be his final determination . " I may find some special jewish machinery . I will wait till after Christmas . " 

 what should we all do without the calendar , when we want to put off a disagreeable duty ? the admirable arrangement of the solar system , by which our time be measure , always supply we with a term before which it be hardly worth while to set about anything we be disinclined to . 

 CHAPTER XXXIII . 

 " no man , " say a Rabbi , by way of indisputable instance , " may turn the bone of his father and mother into spoon " sure that his hearer feel the check against that form of economy . the market for spoon have never expand enough for any one to say , " why not ? " and to argue that human progress lie in such an application of material . the only check to be allege be a sentiment , which will coerce none who do not hold that sentiment be the well part of the world 's wealth . 

 Deronda meanwhile take to a less fashionable form of exercise than rid in Rotten row . he go often ramble in those part of London which be most inhabit by common Jews . he walk to the synagogue at time of service , he look into shop , he observe face : a process not very promising of particular discovery . why do he not address himself to an influential Rabbi or other member of a jewish community , to consult on the chance of find a mother name Cohen , with a son name Ezra , and a lost daughter name Mirah ? he think of do so after Christmas . the fact be , notwithstanding all his sense of poetry in common thing , Deronda , where a keen personal interest be arouse , could not , more than the rest of we , continuously escape suffer from the pressure of that hard unaccommodate Actual , which have never consult our taste and be entirely unselect . Enthusiasm , we know , dwell at ease among idea , tolerate garlic breathe in the middle age , and see no shabbiness in the official trapping of classic procession : it get squeamish when ideal press upon it as something warmly incarnate , and can hardly face they without faint . lie dreamily in a boat , imagine one 's self in quest of a beautiful maiden 's relative in Cordova elbow by Jews in the time of Ibn - Gebirol , all the physical incident can be bear without shock . or if the scenery of St. Mary Axe and Whitechapel be imaginatively transport to the border of the Rhine at the end of the eleventh century , when in the ear listen for the signal of the Messiah , the Hep ! Hep ! Hep ! of the Crusaders come like the bay of blood - hound ; and in the presence of those devilish missionary with sword and firebrand the crouch figure of the revile Jew turn round erect , heroic , flash with sublime constancy in the face of torture and death what would the dingy shop and unbeautiful face signify to the thrill of contemplative emotion ? but the fervor of sympathy with which we contemplate a grandiose martyrdom be feeble compare with the enthusiasm that keep unslacked where there be no danger , no challenge nothing but impartial midday fall on commonplace , perhaps half - repulsive , object which be really the beloved idea make flesh . here undoubtedly lie the chief poetic energy : in the force of imagination that pierce or exalt the solid fact , instead of float among cloud - picture . to glory in a prophetic vision of knowledge cover the earth , be an easy exercise of believe imagination than to see its beginning in newspaper placard , star at you from the bridge beyond the corn - field ; and it might well happen to most of we dainty people that we be in the thick of the battle of Armageddon without be aware of anything more than the annoyance of a little explosive smoke and struggle on the ground immediately about we . 

 it lie in Deronda 's nature usually to contemn the feeble , fastidious sympathy which shrink from the broad life of mankind ; but now , with Mirah before he as a living reality , whose experience he have to care for , he see every common Jew and Jewess in the light of a comparison with she , and have a presentiment of the collision between her idea of the unknown mother and brother and the discover fact a presentiment all the keener in he because of a suppress consciousness that a not unlike possibility of collision might lie hide in his own lot . not that he would have look with more complacency of expectation at wealthy Jews , outdo the lord of the Philistines in their sport ; but since there be no likelihood of Mirah 's friend be find among that class , their habit do not immediately affect he . in this mood he ramble , without expectation of a more pregnant result than a little preparation of his own mind , perhaps for future theorizing as well as practice very much as if , Mirah be relate to Welsh miner , he have go to look more closely at the way of those people , not without wish at the same time to get a little light of detail on the history of Strikes . 

 he really do not long to find anybody in particular ; and when , as his habit be , he look at the name over a shop door , he be well content that it be not Ezra Cohen . I confess , he particularly desire that Ezra Cohen should not keep a shop . wish be hold to be ominous ; accord to which belief the order of the world be so arrange that if you have an impious objection to a squint , your offspring be the more likely to be bear with one ; also , that if you happen to desire a squint you would not get it . this desponding view of probability the hopeful entirely reject , take their wish as good and sufficient security for all kind of fulfilment . who be absolutely neutral ? Deronda happen one morning to turn into a little side street out of the noise and obstruction of Holborn , feel the scale dip on the despond side . 

 he be rather tired of the street and have pause to hail a hansom cab which he see come , when his attention be catch by some fine old clasp in chase silver display in the window at his right hand . his first thought be that Lady Mallinger , who have a strictly protestant taste for such catholic spoil , might like to have these missal - clasp turn into a bracelet : then his eye travel over the other content of the window , and he see that the shop be that kind of pawnbroker 's where the lead be give to jewelry , lace and all equivocal object introduce as bric - a - brac . a placard in one corner announce Watches and Jewelry exchange and repair . but his survey have be notice from within , and a figure appear at the door , look round at he and say in a tone of cordial encouragement , " good day , sir . " the instant be enough for Deronda to see the face , unmistakably jewish , belong to a young man about thirty , and wince from the shopkeeper 's persuasiveness that would probably follow , he have no soon return the " good day , " than he pass to the other side of the street and beckon to the cabman to draw up there . from that station he see the name over the shop window Ezra Cohen . 

 there might be a hundred Ezra Cohens letter above shop window , but Deronda have not see they . probably the young man interested in a possible customer be Ezra himself ; and he be about the age to be expect in Mirah 's brother , who be grow up while she be still a little child . but Deronda 's first endeavor as he drive homeward be to convince himself that there be not the slight warrantable presumption of this Ezra be Mirah 's brother ; and next , that even if , in spite of good reasoning , he turn out to be that brother , while on inquiry the mother be find to be dead , it be not his Deronda 's duty to make know the discovery to Mirah . in inconvenient disturbance of this conclusion there come his lately - acquire knowledge that Mirah would have a religious desire to know of her mother 's death , and also to learn whether her brother be live . how far be he justify in determine another life by his own notion ? be it not his secret complaint against the way in which other have order his own life , that he have not open daylight on all its relation , so that he have not , like other man , the full guidance of primary duty ? 

 the immediate relief from this inward debate be the reflection that he have not yet make any real discovery , and that by look into the fact more closely he should be certify that there be no demand on he for any decision whatever . he intend to return to that shop as soon as he could conveniently , and buy the clasp for Lady Mallinger . but he be hinder for several day by Sir Hugo , who , about to make an after - dinner speech on a burn topic , want Deronda to forage for he on the legal part of the question , besides waste time every day on argument which always end in a draw battle . as on many other question , they hold different side , but Sir Hugo do not mind this , and when Deronda put his point well , say , with a mixture of satisfaction and regret 

 " confound it , Dan ! why do not   you make an opportunity of say these thing in public ? you be wrong , you know . you will not   succeed . you have   get the massive sentiment the heavy artillery of the country against you . but it be all the well ground for a young man to display himself on . when I be your age , I should have take it . and it would be quite as well for you to be in opposition to I here and there . it would throw you more into relief . if you would seize an occasion of this sort to make an impression , you might be in Parliament in no time . and you know that would gratify I . " 

 " I be sorry not to do what would gratify you , sir , " say Deronda . " but 
 I can not persuade myself to look at politic as a profession . " 
 " why not ? if a man be not bear into public life by his position in the country , there be no way for he but to embrace it by his own effort . the business of the country must be do her Majesty 's Government carry on , as the old Duke say . and it never could be , my boy , if everybody look at politic as if they be prophecy , and demand an inspire vocation . if you be to get into Parliament , it will not   do to sit still and wait for a call either from heaven or constituent . " 

 " I do not   want to make a living out of opinion , " say Deronda ; " especially out of borrow opinion . not that I mean to blame other man . I dare say many well fellow than I do not   mind get on to a platform to praise themselves , and give their word of honor for a party . " 

 " I will tell you what , Dan , " say Sir Hugo , " a man who set his face against every sort of humbug be simply a three - cornered , impracticable fellow . there be a bad style of humbug , but there be also a good style one that oil the wheel and make progress possible . if you be to rule man , you must rule they through their own idea ; and I agree with the Archbishop at Naples who have a St. Januarius procession against the plague . it be no use have an order in Council against popular shallowness . there be no action possible without a little acting . " 

 " one may be oblige to give way to an occasional necessity , " say Deronda . " but it be one thing to say , ' in this particular case I be force to put on this foolscap and grin , ' and another to buy a pocket foolscap and practice myself in grin . I can not   see any real public expediency that do not keep an ideal before it which make a limit of deviation from the direct path . but if I be to set up for a public man I might mistake my success for public expediency . " 

 it be after this dialogue , which be rather jarring to he , that Deronda set out on his meditate second visit to Ezra Cohen 's . he enter the street at the end opposite to the Holborn entrance , and an inward reluctance slacken his pace while his thought be transfer what he have just be say about public expediency to the entirely private difficulty which bring he back again into this unattractive thoroughfare . it might soon become an immediate practical question with he how far he could call it a wise expediency to conceal the fact of close kindre . such question turn up constantly in life be often decide in a rough - and - ready way ; and to many it will appear an over - refinement in Deronda that he should make any great point of a matter confine to his own knowledge . but we have see the reason why he have come to regard concealment as a bane of life , and the necessity of concealment as a mark by which line of action be to be avoid . the prospect of be urge against the confirm habit of his mind be naturally grate . he even pause here and there before the most plausible shop - window for a gentleman to look into , half inclined to decide that he would not increase his knowledge about that modern Ezra , who be certainly not a leader among his people a hesitation which prove how , in a man much give to reasoning , a bare possibility may weigh more than the well - clothe likelihood ; for Deronda 's reasoning have decide that all likelihood be against this man 's be Mirah 's brother . 

 one of the shop - window he pause before be that of a second - hand book - shop , where , on a narrow table outside , the literature of the age be represent in judicious mixture , from the immortal verse of Homer to the mortal prose of the railway novel . that the mixture be judicious be apparent from Deronda 's finding in it something that he want namely , that wonderful bit of autobiography , the life of the Polish Jew , Salomon Maimon ; which , as he could easily slip it into his pocket , he take from its place , and enter the shop to pay for , expect to see behind the counter a grimy personage show that nonchalance about sale which seem to belong universally to the second - hand book - business . in most other trade you find generous man who be anxious to sell you their ware for your own welfare ; but even a Jew will not urge Simson 's Euclid on you with an affectionate assurance that you will have pleasure in read it , and that he wish he have twenty more of the article , so much be it in request . one be lead to fear that a secondhand bookseller may belong to that unhappy class of man who have no belief in the good of what they get their living by , yet keep conscience enough to be morose rather than unctuous in their vocation . 

 but instead of the ordinary tradesman , he see , on the dark background of book in the long narrow shop , a figure that be somewhat startling in its unusualness . a man in threadbare clothing , whose age be difficult to guess from the dead yellowish flatness of the flesh , something like an old ivory carving be seat on a stool against some bookshelf that project beyond the short counter , do nothing more remarkable than read yesterday 's Times ; but when he let the paper rest on his lap and look at the incoming customer , the thought glance through Deronda that precisely such a physiognomy as that might possibly have be see in a prophet of the Exile , or in some New hebrew poet of the mediaval time . it be a fine typical jewish face , work into intensity of expression apparently by a strenuous eager experience in which all the satisfaction have be indirect and far off , and perhaps by some bodily suffering also , which involve that absence of ease in the present . the feature be clear - cut , not large ; the brow not high but broad , and fully define by the crisp black hair . it might never have be a particularly handsome face , but it must always have be forcible ; and now with its dark , far - off gaze , and yellow pallor in relief on the gloom of the backward shop , one might have imagine one 's self come upon it in some past prison of the Inquisition , which a mob have suddenly burst upon ; while the look fix on an incidental customer seem eager and question enough to have be turn on one who might have be a messenger either of delivery or of death . the figure be probably familiar and unexciting enough to the inhabitant of this street ; but to Deronda 's mind it bring so strange a blending of the unwonted with the common , that there be a perceptible interval of mutual observation before he ask his question ; " what be the price of this book ? "

 but immediately the strange Jew rise from his sitting posture , and Deronda feel a thin hand press his arm tightly , while a hoarse , excited voice , not much above a loud whisper , say

 " well , sir , I have   accommodate gentleman of distinction I be proud to say it . I would not   exchange my business with any in the world . there be none more honorable , nor more charitable , nor more necessary for all class , from the good lady who want a little of the ready for the baker , to a gentleman like yourself , sir , who may want it for amusement .  if an amiable self - satisfaction be the mark of earthly bliss , Solomon in all his glory be a pitiable mortal compare with Mr. Cohen clearly one of those person , who , be in excellent spirit about themselves , be willing to cheer stranger by let they know it . while he be deliver himself with lively rapidity , he take the baby from his wife and hold it on his arm present his feature to be explore by its small fist .  " I have a fine diamond ring to offer as security not with I at this moment , unfortunately , for I be not in the habit of wear it . but I will come again this evening and bring it with I . fifty pound at once would be a convenience to I . "

 when Deronda arrive at five o'clock , the shop be close and the door be open for he by the christian servant . when she show he into the room behind the shop he be surprised at the prettiness of the scene . the house be old , and rather extensive at the back : probably the large room he how enter be gloomy by daylight , but now it be agreeably light by a fine old brass lamp with seven oil - light hang above the snow - white cloth spread on the central table , the ceiling and wall be smoky , and all the surrounding be dark enough to throw into relief the human figure , which have a venetian glow of color . the grandmother be array in yellowish brown with a large gold chain in lieu of the necklace , and by this light her yellow face with its darkly - mark eyebrow and frame roll of gray hair look as handsome as be necessary for picturesque effect . Young Mrs. Cohen be clothe in red and black , with a string of large artificial pearl wound round and round her neck : the baby lie asleep in the cradle under a scarlet counterpane ; Adelaide Rebekah be in braided amber , and Jacob Alexander be in black velveteen with scarlet stocking . as the four pair of black eye all glisten a welcome at Deronda , he be almost ashamed of the supercilious dislike these happy - look creature have raise in he by daylight . nothing could be more cordial than the greeting he receive , and both mother and grandmother seem to gather more dignity from be see on the private hearth , show hospitality . he look round with some wonder at the old furniture : the oaken bureau and high side - table must surely be mere matter of chance and economy , and not due to the family taste . a large dish of blue and yellow ware be set up on the side - table , and flank it be two old silver vessel ; in front of they a large volume in darken vellum with a deep - rib back . in the corner at the farther end be an open door into an inner room , where there be also a light .

 can this be part of the religious ceremony ? think Deronda , not know what might be expect of the ancient hero . but he hear a " yes " from the next room , which make he look toward the open door ; and there , to his astonishment , he see the figure of the enigmatic Jew whom he have this morning meet with in the book - shop . their eye meet , and Mordecai look as much surprised as Deronda neither in his surprise make any sign of recognition . but when Mordecai be seat himself at the end of the table , he just bent his head to the guest in a cold and distant manner , as if the disappointment of the morning remain a disagreeable association with this new acquaintance .

Mordecai have no handsome Sabbath garment , but instead of the threadbare rusty black coat of the morning he wear one of light drab , which look as if it have once be a handsome loose paletot now shrunk with washing ; and this change of clothing give a still strong accentuation to his dark - haired , eager face which might have belong to the prophet Ezekiel also probably not modish in the eye of contemporary . it be noticeable that the thin tail of the fried fish be give to Mordecai ; and in general the sort of share assign to a poor relation no doubt a ' survival " of prehistoric practice , not yet generally admit to be superstitious . 

 Mr. Cohen keep up the conversation with much liveliness , introduce as subject always in taste   the Jew be proud of his loyalty   the Queen and the Royal Family , the Emperor and Empress of the French into which both grandmother and wife enter with z . " I have a fine piece of work to take care of , mother ; she might have be squeeze flat though she be pretty near as lusty then as she be now .  the father wink at his guest and say , " have your nose put on the grindstone . "

" 

 " I be sorry to say , not at all . "

" I be not afraid of take that ring of yours at my own valuation . but now , " he add , with a certain drop in his voice to a low , more familiar nasal , " what do you want for it ? "

 " good . I will make you out the ticket by - and - by , " say Cohen , indifferently . then he hold up his finger as a sign that conversation must be defer . he , Mordecai and Jacob put on their hat , and Cohen open a thanksgiving , which be carry on by response , till Mordecai deliver himself alone at some length , in a solemn chant tone , with his chin slightly uplifted and his thin hand clasp easily before he . not only in his accent and tone , but in his freedom from the self - consciousness which have reference to other ' approbation , there could hardly have be a strong contrast to the Jew at the other end of the table . it be an unaccountable conjunction the presence among these common , prosperous , shopkeepe type , of a man who , in an emaciated threadbare condition , impose a certain awe on Deronda , and an embarrassment at not meet his expectation .

Deronda , feel that it would be hardly delicate to protract his visit beyond the settlement of the business which be its pretext , have to take his leave , with no more decide result than the advance of forty pound and the pawn - ticket in his breast - pocket , to make a reason for return when he come up to town after Christmas . he be resolve that he would then endeavor to gain a little more insight into the character and history of Mordecai ; from whom also he might gather something decisive about the Cohens for example , the reason why it be forbid to ask Mrs. Cohen the elder whether she have a daughter . 

 BOOK V. MORDECAI . 

 CHAPTER XXXV . 

 be uneasiness of conscience measure by extent of crime , human history have be different , and one should look to see the contriver of greedy war and the mighty marauder of the money - market in one troop of self - lacerate penitent with the mean robber and cut - purse and the murderer that doth his butchery in small with his own hand . no doubt wickedness hath its reward to distribute ; but who so win in this devil 's game must need be base , more cruel , more brutal than the order of this planet will allow for the multitude bear of woman , the most of these carry a form of conscience a fear which be the shadow of justice , a pity which be the shadow of love that hindereth from the prize of serene wickedness , itself difficult of maintenance in our composite flesh .

" I suppose some of we go on fast than other : and I be sure she be a creature who keep strong trace of anything that have once impress she . that little affair of the necklace , and the idea that somebody think her gambling wrong , have evidently bite into she . but such impressibility lead both way : it may drive one to desperation as soon as to anything well . and whatever fascination Grandcourt may have for capricious taste good heaven ! who can believe that he would call out the tender affection in daily companionship ? one might be tempt to horsewhip he for the sake of get some show of passion into his face and speech . " without the aid of sacred ceremony or costume , her feeling have turn this man , only a few year old than herself , into a priest ; a sort of trust less rare than the fidelity that guard it . young reverence for one who be also young be the most coercive of all : there be the same level of temptation , and the high motive be believe in as a full force not suspect to be a mere residue from weary experience .

 meanwhile Deronda have be fasten and lead off by Mr. Vandernoodt , who wish for a brisker walk , a cigar , and a little gossip . since we can not tell a man his own secret , the restraint of be in his company often breed a desire to pair off in conversation with some more ignorant person , and Mr. Vandernoodt presently say

 Most of we remember Retzsch 's drawing of destiny in the shape of Mephistopheles play at chess with man for his soul , a game in which we may imagine the clever adversary make a feint of unintended move so as to set the beguile mortal on carry his defensive piece away from the true point of attack . the fiend make preparation his favorite object of mockery , that he may fatally persuade we against our take out waterproof when he be well aware the sky be go to clear , foresee that the imbecile will turn this delusion into a prejudice against waterproof instead of give a close study to the weather - sign . it be a peculiar test of a man 's metal when , after he have painfully adjust himself to what seem a wise provision , he find all his mental precaution a little beside the mark , and his excellent intention no well than miscalculate dovetail , accurately cut from a wrong starting - point . his magnanimity have get itself ready to meet misbehavior , and find quite a different call upon it . something of this kind happen to Deronda . 

 his first impression be one of pure pleasure and amusement at find his sitting - room transform into an atelier strew with miscellaneous drawing and with the content of two chest from Rome , the low half of the window darken with baize , and the blonde Hans in his weird youth as the preside genius of the littered place his hair long than of old , his face more whimsically creased , and his high voice as usual get high under the excitement of rapid talk . the friendship of the two have be keep up warmly since the memorable Cambridge time , not only by correspondence but by little episode of companionship abroad and in England , and the original relation of confidence on one side and indulgence on the other have be develop in practice , as be will not to be the case where such spiritual borrowing and lending have be well begin .

 CHAPTER XXXVIII . 

 there be who hold that the deep tragedy be a Prometheus Bound not after but before he have well get the celestial fire into the narthex whereby it might be convey to mortal : thrust by the Kratos and Bia of institute method into a solitude of despise idea , fasten in throb helplessness by the fatal pressure of poverty and disease a solitude where many pass by , but none regard . 

 " second - sight " be a flag over dispute ground . but it be matter of knowledge that there be person whose yearning , conception nay , travel conclusion continually take the form of image which have a foreshadow power ; the deed they would do start up before they in complete shape , make a coercive type ; the event they hunger for or dread rise into vision with a seed - like growth , feed itself fast on unnumbered impression . they be not always the less capable of the argumentative process , nor less sane than the commonplace calculator of the market : sometimes it may be that their nature have manifold opening , like the hundred - gate Thebes , where there may naturally be a great and more miscellaneous inrush than through a narrow beadle - watch portal . no doubt there be abject specimen of the visionary , as there be a minim mammal which you might imprison in the finger of your glove . that small relative of the elephant have no harm in he ; but what great mental or social type be free from specimen whose insignificance be both ugly and noxious ? one be afraid to think of all that the genus " patriot " embrace ; or of the elbowing there might be at the day of judgment for those who rank as author , and bring volume either in their hand or on truck . 

 this apology for inevitable kinship be mean to usher in some fact about Mordecai , whose figure have bite itself into Deronda 's mind as a new question which he feel an interest in get answer . but the interest be no more than a vaguely - expectant suspense : the consumptive - look Jew , apparently a fervid student of some kind , get his crust by a quiet handicraft , like Spinoza , fit into none of Deronda 's anticipation . 

 it be otherwise with the effect of their meeting on Mordecai . for many winter , while he have be conscious of an ebbing physical life , and as widen spiritual loneliness , all his passionate desire have concentrate itself in the yearning for some young ear into which he could pour his mind as a testament , some soul kindre enough to accept the spiritual product of his own brief , painful life , as a mission to be execute . it be remarkable that the hopefulness which be often the beneficent illusion of consumptive patient , be in Mordecai wholly divert from the prospect of bodily recovery and carry into the current of this yearning for transmission . the yearning , which have pant upward from out of over - whelm discouragement , have grow into a hope the hope into a confident belief , which , instead of be check by the clear conception he have of his hasten decline , take rather the intensity of expectant faith in a prophecy which have only brief space to get fulfil in . 

 some year have now go since he have first begin to measure man with a keen glance , search for a possibility which become more and more a distinct conception . such distinctness as it have at first be reach chiefly by a method of contrast : he want to find a man who differ from himself . trace reason in that self for the rebuff he have meet with and the hindrance that beset he , he imagine a man who would have all the element necessary for sympathy with he , but in an embodiment unlike his own : he must be a Jew , intellectually cultured , morally fervid in all this a nature ready to be plenishe from Mordecai 's ; but his face and frame must be beautiful and strong , he must have be use to all the refinement of social life , his voice must flow with a full and easy current , his circumstance be free from sordid need : he must glorify the possibility of the Jew , not sit and wonder as Mordecai do , bear the stamp of his people amid the sign of poverty and wan breath . sensitive to physical characteristic , he have , both abroad and in England , look at picture as well as man , and in a vacant hour he have sometimes linger in the National Gallery in search of painting which might feed his hopefulness with grave and noble type of the human form , such as might well belong to man of his own race . but he return in disappointment . the instance be scatter but thinly over the gallery of Europe , in which the fortune or selection even of the chief master have give to art a face at once young , grand , and beautiful , where , if there be any melancholy , it be no feeble passivity , but enter into the foreshadow capability of heroism . 

 some observant person may perhaps remember his emaciated figure , and dark eye deep in their socket , as he stand in front of a picture that have touch he either to new or habitual meditation : he commonly wear a cloth cap with black fur round it , which no painter would have ask he to take off . but spectator would be likely to think of he as an odd - look Jew who probably get money out of picture ; and Mordecai , when he look at they , be perfectly aware of the impression he make . experience have render he morbidly alive to the effect of a man 's poverty and other physical disadvantage in cheapen his idea , unless they be those of a Peter the Hermit who have a tocsin for the rabble . but he be too sane and generous to attribute his spiritual banishment solely to the excusable prejudice of other ; certain incapacity of his own have make the sentence of exclusion ; and hence it be that his imagination have construct another man who would be something more ample than the second soul bestow , accord to the notion of the Cabbalists , to help out the insufficient first who would be a bloom human life , ready to incorporate all that be worthy in an existence whose visible , palpable part be burn itself fast away . his inward need for the conception of this expand , prolonged self be reflect as an outward necessity . the thought of his heart   that ancient phrase good shadow the truth   seem to he too precious , too closely interweave with the growth of thing not to have a further destiny . and as the more beautiful , the strong , the more executive self take shape in his mind , he love it beforehand with an affection half identify , half contemplative and grateful . 

 Mordecai 's mind work so constantly in image , that his coherent train of thought often resemble the significant dream attribute to sleeper by wake person in their most inventive moment : nay , they often resemble genuine dream in their way of break off the passage from the know to the unknown . thus , for a long while , he habitually think of the be answer to his need as one distantly approach or turn his back toward he , darkly paint against a golden sky . the reason of the golden sky lie in one of Mordecai 's habit . he be keenly alive to some poetic aspect of London ; and a favorite resort of his , when strength and leisure allow , be to some of the bridge , especially about sunrise or sunset . even when he be bend over watch - wheel and trinket , or seat in a small upper room look out on dingy brick and dingy crack window , his imagination spontaneously plant he on some spot where he have a far - stretch scene ; his thought go on in wide space ; and whenever he could , he try to have in reality the influence of a large sky . lean on the parapet of Blackfriar 's Bridge , and gaze meditatively , the breadth and calm of the river , with its long vista half hazy , half luminous , the grand dim mass of tall form of building which be the sign of world - commerce , the oncoming of boat and barge from the still distance into sound and color , enter into his mood and blent themselves indistinguishably with his thinking , as a fine symphony to which we can hardly be say to listen , make a medium that bear up our spiritual wing . thus it happen that the figure representative of Mordecai 's longing be mentally see darken by the excess of light in the aerial background . but in the inevitable progress of his imagination toward full detail , he cease to see the figure with its back toward he . it begin to advance , and a face become discernible ; the word youth , beauty , refinement , jewish birth , noble gravity , turn into hardly individual but typical form and color : gather from his memory of face see among the Jews of Holland and Bohemia , and from the painting which revive that memory . reverently let it be say of this mature spiritual need that it be akin to the boy 's and girl 's picturing of the future beloved ; but the stirring of such young desire be feeble compare with the passionate current of an ideal life strain to embody itself , make intense by resistance to imminent dissolution . the visionary form become a companion and auditor ; keep a place not only in the wake imagination , but in those dream of light slumber of which it be true to say , " I sleep , but my heart waketh " when the disturbing trivial story of yesterday be charge with the impassioned purpose of year . 

 of late the urgency of irremediable time , measure by the gradual choking of life , have turn Mordecai 's trust into an agitated watch for the fulfillment that must be at hand . be the bell on the verge of tolling , the sentence about to be execute ? the deliverer 's footstep must be near the deliverer who be to rescue Mordecai 's spiritual travail from oblivion , and give it an abide - place in the good heritage of his people . an insane exaggeration of his own value , even if his idea have be as true and precious as those of Columbus or Newton , many would have count this yearning , take it as the sublimer part for a man to say , " if not I , then another , " and to hold cheap the meaning of his own life . but the full nature desire to be an agent , to create , and not merely to look on : strong love hunger to bless , and not merely to behold blessing . and while there be warmth enough in the sun to feed an energetic life , there will still be man to feel , " I be lord of this moment 's change , and will charge it with my soul . " 

 but with that mingling of inconsequence which belong to we all , and not unhappily , since it save we from many effect of mistake , Mordecai 's confidence in the friend to come do not suffice to make he passive , and he try expedient , pathetically humble , such as happen to be within his reach , for communicate something of himself . it be now two year since he have take up his abode under Ezra Cohen 's roof , where he be regard with much good - will as a compound of workman , dominie , vessel of charity , inspire idiot , man of piety , and   if he be inquire into   dangerous heretic . during that time little Jacob have advance into knickerbocker , and into that quickness of apprehension which have be already make manifest in relation to hardware and exchange . he have also advance in attachment to Mordecai , regard he as an inferior , but like he none the bad , and take his helpful cleverness as he might have take the service of an enslaved Djinn . as for Mordecai , he have give Jacob his first lesson , and his habitual tenderness easily turn into the teacher 's fatherhood . though he be fully conscious of the spiritual distance between the parent and himself , and would never have attempt any communication to they from his peculiar world , the boy move he with that idealize affection which merge the quality of the individual child in the glory of childhood and the possibility of a long future . and this feeling have draw he on , at first without premeditation , and afterward with conscious purpose , to a sort of outpoure in the ear of the boy which might have seem wild enough to any excellent man of business who overheard it . but none overhear when Jacob go up to Mordecai 's room one day , for example , in which there be little work to be do , or at an hour when the work be end , and after a brief lesson in english reading or in numeration , be induce to remain stand at his teacher 's knee , or choose to jump astride they , often to the patient fatigue of the waste limb . the inducement be perhaps the mending of a toy , or some little mechanical device in which Mordecai 's well - practice finger - tip have an exceptional skill ; and with the boy thus tether , he would begin to repeat a hebrew poem of his own , into which year before he have pour his first youthful ardor for that conception of a blend past and future which be the mistress of his soul , tell Jacob to say the word after he . 

 " the boy will get they engrave within he , " think Mordecai ; " it be a way of printing . " 

 none readier than Jacob at this fascinating game of imitate unintelligible word ; and if no oppose diversion occur he would sometimes carry on his share in it as long as the teacher 's breath would last out . for Mordecai throw into each repetition the fervor befit a sacred occasion . in such instance , Jacob would show no other distraction than reach out and survey the content of his pocket ; or draw down the skin of his cheek to make his eye look awful , and roll his head to complete the effect ; or alternately handle his own nose and Mordecai 's as if to test the relation of their mass . under all this the fervid reciter would not pause , satisfied if the young organ of speech would submit themselves . but most commonly a sudden impulse send Jacob leap away into some antic or active amusement , when , instead of follow the recitation he would return upon the foregoing word most ready to his tongue , and mouth or gabble , with a see - saw suit to the action of his limb , a verse on which Mordecai have spend some of his too scanty heart 's blood . yet he wait with such patience as a prophet need , and begin his strange printing again undiscouraged on the morrow , say inwardly 

 " my word may rule he some day . their meaning may flash out on he . it be so with a nation after many day . " 

 meanwhile Jacob 's sense of power be increase and his time enliven by a store of magical articulation with which he make the baby crow , or drive the large cat into a dark corner , or promise himself to frighten any incidental Christian of his own year . one week he have unfortunately see a street mountebank , and this carry off his muscular imitativeness in sad divergence from New Hebrew poetry , after the model of Jehuda ha - Levi . Mordecai have arrive at a fresh passage in his poem ; for as soon as Jacob have get well use to one portion , he be lead on to another , and a fresh combination of sound generally answer well in keep he fast for a few minute . the consumptive voice , generally a strong high baritone , with its variously mingle hoarseness , like a haze amidst illumination , and its occasional incipient gasp have more than the usual excitement , while it give forth hebrew verse with a meaning something like this : 

  " away from I the garment of forgetfulness . 
  wither the heart ; 
  the oil and wine from press of the Goyim , 
  poison with scorn . 
  Solitude be on the side of Mount Nebo , 
  in its heart a tomb : 
  there the bury ark and golden cherubim 
  make hidden light : 
  there the solemn gaze unchanged , 
  the wing be spread unbroken : 
  Shut beneath in silent awful speech 
  the Law lie graven . 
  Solitude and darkness be my covering , 
  and my heart a tomb ; 
  Smite and shatter it , o Gabriel ! 
  Shatter it as the clay of the founder 
  around the golden image . " 
 in the absorbing enthusiasm with which Mordecai have intone rather than speak this last invocation , he be unconscious that Jacob have cease to follow he and have start away from his knee ; but pause he see , as by a sudden flash , that the lad have throw himself on his hand with his foot in the air , mountebank fashion , and be pick up with his lip a bright farthing which be a favorite among his pocket treasure . this might have be reckon among the trick Mordecai be use to , but at this moment it jar he horribly , as if it have be a satanic grin upon his prayer . 

 " child ! child ! " he call out with a strange cry that startle Jacob to his foot , and then he sink backward with a shudder , close his eye . 

 " what ? " say Jacob , quickly . then , not get an immediate answer , he press Mordecai 's knee with a shake movement , in order to rouse he . Mordecai open his eye with a fierce expression in they , lean forward , grasp the little shoulder , and say in a quick , hoarse whisper 

 " a curse be on your generation , child . they will open the mountain and drag forth the golden wing and coin they into money , and the solemn face they will break up into ear - ring for wanton woman ! and they shall get themselves a new name , but the angel of ignominy , with the fiery brand , shall know they , and their heart shall be the tomb of dead desire that turn their life to rottenness . " 

 the aspect and action of Mordecai be so new and mysterious to Jacob they carry such a burden of obscure threat it be as if the patient , indulgent companion have turn into something unknown and terrific : the sunken dark eye and hoarse accent close to he , the thin grappling finger , shake Jacob 's little frame into awe , and while Mordecai be speak he stand tremble with a sense that the house be tumble in and they be not go to have dinner any more . but when the terrible speech have end and the pinch be relax , the shock resolve itself into tear ; Jacob lift up his small patriarchal countenance and weep aloud . this sign of childish grief at once recall Mordecai to his usual gentle self : he be not able to speak again at present , but with a maternal action he draw the curly head toward he and press it tenderly against his breast . on this Jacob , feel the danger well - nigh over , howl at ease , begin to imitate his own performance and improve upon it a sort of transition from impulse into art often observable . indeed , the next day he undertake to terrify Adelaide Rebekah in like manner , and succeed very well . 

 but Mordecai suffer a check which last long , from the consciousness of a misapplied agitation ; sane as well as excitable , he judge severely his moment of aberration into futile eagerness , and feel discredit with himself . all the more his mind be strain toward the discernment of that friend to come , with whom he would have a calm certainty of fellowship and understanding . 

 it be just then that , in his usual midday guardianship of the old book - shop , he be strike by the appearance of Deronda , and it be perhaps comprehensible now why Mordecai 's glance take on a sudden eager interest as he look at the new - comer : he see a face and frame which seem to he to realize the long - conceive type . but the disclaimer of jewish birth be for the moment a backward thrust of double severity , the particular disappointment tend to shake his confidence in the more indefinite expectation . nevertheless , when he find Deronda seat at the Cohens ' table , the disclaimer be for the moment nullify : the first impression return with add force , seem to be guarantee by this second meeting under circumstance more peculiar than the former ; and in ask Deronda if he know Hebrew , Mordecai be so possess by the new inrush of belief , that he have forget the absence of any other condition to the fulfillment of his hope . but the answer " no " strike they all down again , and the frustration be more painful than before . after turn his back on the visitor that Sabbath evening , Mordecai go through day of a deep discouragement , like that of man on a doom ship , who have strain their eye after a sail , and behold it with rejoice , behold it never advance , and say , " our sick eye make it . " but the long - contemplated figure have come as an emotional sequence of Mordecai 's firm theoretic conviction ; it have be work from the imagery of his most passionate life ; and it inevitably reappear reappear in a more specific self - assert form than ever . Deronda have that sort of resemblance to the preconceived type which a finely individual bust or portrait have to the more generalized copy leave in our mind after a long interval : we renew our memory with delight , but we hardly know with how much correction . and now , his face meet Mordecai 's inward gaze as it have always belong to the await friend , ray out , moreover , some of that influence which belong to breathe flesh ; till by - and - by it seem that discouragement have turn into a new obstinacy of resistance , and the ever - recurrent vision have the force of an outward call to disregard counter - evidence , and keep expectation awake . it be Deronda now who be see in the often painful night - watch , when we be all liable to be hold with the clutch of a single thought whose figure , never with its back turn , be see in moment of soothe reverie or soothe dozing , paint on that golden sky which be the doubly blessed symbol of advance day and of approach rest . 

 Mordecai know that the nameless stranger be to come and redeem his ring ; and , in spite of contrary chance , the wish to see he again be grow into a belief that he should see he . in the January week , he feel an increase agitation of that subdue hidden quality which hinder nervous people from any steady occupation on the eve of an anticipated change . he could not go on with his printing of Hebrew on little Jacob 's mind ; or with his attendance at a weekly club , which be another effort of the same forlorn hope : something else be come . the one thing he long for be to get as far as the river , which he could do but seldom and with difficulty . he yearn with a poet 's yearning for the wide sky , the far - reach vista of bridge , the tender and fluctuating light on the water which seem to breathe with a life that can shiver and mourn , be comfort and rejoice .

 Deronda come out of the narrow house at Chelsea in a frame of mind that make he long for some good bodily exercise to carry off what he be himself inclined to call the fume of his temper . he be go toward the city , and the sight of the Chelsea Stairs with the waiting boat at once determine he to avoid the irritate inaction of be drive in a cab , by call a wherry and take an oar .

 when the wherry be approach Blackfriars Bridge , where Deronda mean to land , it be half - past four , and the gray day be die gloriously , its western cloud all break into narrow purple strata before a wide - spread saffron clearness , which in the sky have a monumental calm , but on the river , with its change object , be reflect as a luminous movement , the alternate flash of ripple or current , the sudden glow of the brown sail , the passage of laden barge from blackness into color , make an active response to that brood glory .

I have always love this bridge : I stand on it when I be a little boy . it be a meeting - place for the spiritual messenger . it be true what the Masters say that each order of thing have its angel : that mean the full message of each from what be afar . here I have listen to the message of earth and sky ; when I be strong I use to stay and watch for the star in the deep heaven . but this time just about sunset be always what I love well . it have sink into I and dwelt with I fade , slowly fade : it be my own decline : it pause it wait , till at last it bring I my new life my new self who will live when this breath be all breathe out . " 

 Deronda do not speak . he feel himself strangely work upon . the first - prompt suspicion that Mordecai might be liable to hallucination of thought might have become a monomaniac on some subject which have give too severe a strain to his diseased organism give way to a more submissive expectancy . his nature be too large , too ready to conceive region beyond his own experience , to rest at once in the easy explanation , " madness , " whenever a consciousness show some fullness and conviction where his own be blank . it accord with his habitual disposition that he should meet rather than resist any claim on he in the shape of another 's need ; and this claim bring with it a sense of solemnity which seem a radiation from Mordecai , as utterly nullify his outward poverty and lift he into authority as if he have be that preternatural guide see in the universal legend , who suddenly drop his mean disguise and stand a manifest power . that impression be the more sanction by a sort of resolve quietude which the persuasion of fulfillment have produce in Mordecai 's manner . after they have stand a moment in silence he say , " let we go now , " and when they be rid he add , " we will get down at the end of the street and walk to the shop . you can look at the book , and Mr. Ram will be go away directly and leave we alone . " 

 it seem that this enthusiast be just as cautious , just as much alive to judgment in other mind as if he have be that antipode of all enthusiasm call " a man of the world . "

" 

 in ten minute the two man , with as intense a consciousness as if they have be two undeclared lover , feel themselves alone in the small gas - light book - shop and turn face to face , each bar his head from an instinctive feeling that they wish to see each other fully . I wish I could perpetuate those two face , as Titian 's " Tribute money " have perpetuate two type present another sort of contrast . imagine we all of we can the pathetic stamp of consumption with its brilliancy of glance to which the sharply - define structure of feature remind one of a forsaken temple , give already a far - off look as of one get unwillingly out of reach ; and imagine it on a jewish face naturally accentuate for the expression of an eager mind the face of a man little above thirty , but with that age upon it which belong to time lengthen by suffer , the hair and beard , still black , throw out the yellow pallor of the skin , the difficult breathing give more decide mark to the mobile nostril , the waste yellow hand conspicuous on the fold arm : then give to the yearn consumptive glance something of the slowly die mother 's look , when her one love son visit her bedside , and the flickering power of gladness leap out as she say , " my boy ! " for the sense of spiritual perpetuation in another resemble that maternal transference of self . 

 see such a portrait you would see Mordecai . and opposite to he be a face not more distinctively oriental than many a type see among what we call the latin race ; rich in youthful health , and with a forcible masculine gravity in its repose , that give the value of judgment to the reverence with which he meet the gaze of this mysterious son of poverty who claim he as a long - expect friend . the more exquisite quality of Deronda 's nature that keenly perceptive sympathetic emotiveness which run along with his speculative tendency be never more thoroughly test . he feel nothing that could be call belief in the validity of Mordecai 's impression concern he or in the probability of any greatly effective issue : what he feel be a profound sensibility to a cry from the depth of another and accompany that , the summon to be receptive instead of superciliously prejudging . receptiveness be a rare and massive power , like fortitude ; and this state of mind now give Deronda 's face its utmost expression of calm benignant force an expression which nourish Mordecai 's confidence and make an open way before he . he begin to speak . 

 " you can not know what have guide I to you and bring we together at this moment . you be wonder . " 

 " I be not impatient , " say Deronda . " I be ready to listen to whatever you may wish to disclose . "

" you see that I be die . you see that I be as one shut up behind bar by the wayside , who if he speak to any would be meet only by head - shaking and pity . the day be close the light be fade soon we should not have be able to discern each other . but you have come in time . I count this heart , and this breath , and this right hand " Mordecai have pathetically press his hand upon his breast , and then stretch its waste finger out before he " I count my sleep and my waking , and the work I feed my body with , and the sight that feed my eye I count they but as fuel to the divine flame . but I have do as one who wander and engrave his thought in rocky solitude , and before I could change my course come care and labor and disease , and block the way before I , and bind I with the iron that eat itself into the soul . then I say , ' how shall I save the life within I from be stifle with this stifle breath ?  " but you may misunderstand I . I know what I choose . they say , ' he feed himself on vision , ' and I deny not ; for vision be the creator and feeder of the world . I see , I measure the world as it be , which the vision will create anew . you be not listen to one who rave aloof from the life of his fellow . "

' he touch a truth there . " 

 the last word have a perceptible irony in their hoarsened tone .

" 

 " how do you know that ? " say Daniel , with an habitual shrinking which make he remove his hand from Mordecai 's , who also relax his hold , and fall back into his former lean position .

 before Mordecai could answer , Mr. Ram re - enter to take his place behind the counter . he be an elderly son of Abraham , whose childhood have fall on the evil time at the beginning of this century , and who remain amid this smart and instructed generation as a preserve speciman , soak through and through with the effect of the poverty and contempt which be the common heritage of most English Jews seventy year ago . he have none of the oily cheerfulness observable in Mr. Cohen 's aspect : his very feature broad and chubby show that tendency to look mongrel without due cause , which , in a miscellaneous London neighborhood , may perhaps be compare with the marvel of imitation in insect , and may have be nature 's imperfect effort on behalf of the pure Caucasian to shield he from the shame and spit to which purer feature would have be expose in the time of zeal .  but Deronda and Mordecai be soon in the street together , and without any explicit agreement as to their direction , be walk toward Ezra Cohen 's .

" but there be a tavern not far from here where I sometimes go to a club . it be the Hand and Banner , in the street at the next turning , five door down . we can have the parlor there any evening . "

 " no ; I need nothing . my outer life be as nought . I will take nothing less precious from you than your soul 's brotherhood . I will think of nothing else yet . but I be glad you be rich . you do not need money on that diamond ring . you have some other motive for bring it . "
