 " this , too be probable , accord to that saying of Agathon : ' it be a part of probability that many improbable thing will happen . ' " ARISTOTLE : Poetics . 

 imagine the conflict in a mind like Deronda 's give not only to feel strongly but to question actively , on the evening after the interview with Mordecai . to a young man of much duller susceptibility the adventure might have seem enough out of the common way to divide his thought ; but it have stir Deronda so deeply , that with the usual reaction of his intellect he begin to examine the ground of his emotion , and consider how far he must resist its guidance . the consciousness that he be half dominate by Mordecai 's energetic certitude , and still more by his fervent trust , rouse his alarm . it be his characteristic bias to shrink from the moral stupidity of value lightly what have come close to he , and of miss blindly in his own life of to - day the crisis which he recognize as momentous and sacred in the historic life of man . if he have read of this incident as have happen century ago in Rome , Greece , Asia Minor , Palestine , Cairo , to some man young as himself , dissatisfied with his neutral life , and want some close fellowship , some more special duty to give he ardor for the possible consequence of his work , it would have appear to he quite natural that the incident should have create a deep impression on that far - off man , whose clothing and action would have be see in his imagination as part of an age chiefly know to we through its more serious effect . why should he be ashamed of his own agitated feeling merely because he dress for dinner , wear a white tie , and live among people who might laugh at his own any conscience in the matter , as the solemn folly of take himself too seriously ? that bugbear of circle in which the lack of grave emotion pass for wit . from such cowardice before modish ignorance and obtuseness , Deronda shrank . but he also shrink from have his course determine by mere contagion , without consent of reason ; or from allow a reverential pity for spiritual struggle to hurry he along a dimly - see path . 

 what , after all , have really happen ? he know quite accurately the answer Sir Hugo would have give : " a consumptive Jew , possess by a fanaticism which obstacle and hastening death intensify , have fix on Deronda as the antitype of some visionary image , the offspring of wed hope and despair : despair of his own life , irrepressible hope in the propagation of his fanatical belief . the instance be perhaps odd , exceptional in its form , but substantially it be not rare . Fanaticism be not so common as bankruptcy , but take in all its aspect it be abundant enough . while Mordecai be wait on the bridge for the fulfillment of his vision , another man be convince that he have the mathematical key of the universe which would supersede Newton , and regard all know physicist as conspire to stifle his discovery and keep the universe lock ; another , that he have the metaphysical key , with just that hair's - breadth of difference from the old ward which would make it fit exactly . scatter here and there in every direction you might find a terrible person , with more or less power of speech , and with an eye either glitter or preternaturally dull , on the look - out for the man who must hear he ; and in most case he have volume which it be difficult to get print , or if print to get read . this Mordecai happen to have a more pathetic aspect , a more passionate , penetrative speech than be usual with such monomaniac ; he be more poetical than a social reformer with colored view of the new moral world in parallelogram , or than an enthusiast in sewage ; still he come under the same class . it would be only right and kind to indulge he a little , to comfort he with such help as be practicable ; but what likelihood be there that his notion have the sort of value he ascribe to they ? in such case a man of the world know what to think beforehand . and as to Mordecai 's conviction that he have find a new executive self , it might be prepare for he the bad of disappointment that which present itself as final . " 

 Deronda 's ear catch all these negative whispering ; nay , he repeat they distinctly to himself . it be not the first but it be the most pressing occasion on which he have have to face this question of the family likeness among the heir of enthusiasm , whether prophet or dreamer of dream , whether the 

 " great benefactor of mankind , deliverer , " 

 or the devotee of phantasmal discovery from the first believer in his own unmanifested inspiration , down to the last inventor of an ideal machine that will achieve perpetual motion . the kinship of human passion , the sameness of mortal scenery , inevitably fill fact with burlesque and parody . error and folly have have their hecatomb of martyr . reduce the grand type of man hitherto know to an abstract statement of his quality and effort , and he appear in dangerous company : say that , like Copernicus and Galileo , he be immovably convince in the face of hiss incredulity ; but so be the contriver of perpetual motion . we can not fairly try the spirit by this sort of test . if we want to avoid give the dose of hemlock or the sentence of banishment in the wrong case , nothing will do but a capacity to understand the subject - matter on which the immovable man be convince , and fellowship with human travail , both near and afar , to hinder we from scan and deep experience lightly . shall we say , " let the age try the spirit , and see what they be worth ? " why , we be the beginning of the age , which can only be just by virtue of just judgment in separate human breast separate yet combine . even steam - engine could not have get make without that condition , but must have stay in the mind of James Watt . 

 this track of thinking be familiar enough to Deronda to have save he from any contemptuous prejudgment of Mordecai , even if their communication have be free from that peculiar claim on himself strangely usher in by some long - grow preparation in the Jew 's agitated mind . this claim , indeed , consider in what be call a rational way , might seem justifiably dismiss as illusory and even preposterous ; but it be precisely what turn Mordecai 's hold on he from an appeal to his ready sympathy into a clutch on his struggle conscience . our conscience be not all of the same pattern , an inner deliverance of fix law : they be the voice of sensibility as various as our memory   which also have their kinship and likeness . and Deronda 's conscience include sensibility beyond the common , enlarge by his early habit of think himself imaginatively into the experience of other . 

 what be the claim this eager soul make upon he ? " you must believe my belief be move by my reason hope my hope see the vision I point to behold a glory where I behold it ! " to take such a demand in the light of an obligation in any direct sense would have be preposterous to have seem to admit it would have be dishonesty ; and Deronda , look on the agitation of those moment , feel thankful that in the midst of his compassion he have preserve himself from the bondage of false concession . the claim hung , too , on a supposition which might be nay , probably be in discordance with the full fact : the supposition that he , Deronda , be of jewish blood . be there ever a more hypothetic appeal ? 

 but since the age of thirteen Deronda have associate the deep experience of his affection with what be a pure supposition , namely , that Sir Hugo be his father : that be a hypothesis which have be the source of passionate struggle within he ; by its light he have be accustom to subdue feeling and to cherish they . he have be well use to find a motive in a conception which might be disprove ; and he have be also use to think of some revelation that might influence his view of the particular duty belong to he . to be in a state of suspense , which be also one of emotive activity and scruple , be a familiar attitude of his conscience . 

 and now , suppose that wish - beget belief in his jewish birth , and that extravagant demand of discipleship , to be the foreshadowing of an actual discovery and a genuine spiritual result : suppose that Mordecai 's idea make a real conquest over Deronda 's conviction ? Nay , it be conceivable that as Mordecai need and believe that , he have find an active replenishment of himself , so Deronda might receive from Mordecai 's mind the complete ideal shape of that personal duty and citizenship which lie in his own thought like sculpture fragment certify some beauty yearn after but not traceable by divination . 

 as that possibility present itself in his meditation , he be aware that it would be call dreamy , and begin to defend it . if the influence he imagine himself submit to have be that of some honor professor , some authority in a seat of learning , some philosopher who have be accept as a voice of the age , would a thorough receptiveness toward direction have be ridicule ? only by those who hold it a sign of weakness to be oblige for an idea , and prefer to hint that they have implicitly hold in a more correct form whatever other have state with a sadly short - come explicitness . after all , what be there but vulgarity in take the fact that Mordecai be a poor jewish workman , and that he be to be meet perhaps on a sanded floor in the parlor of the Hand and Banner as a reason for determine beforehand that there be not some spiritual force within he that might have a determine effect on a white - handed gentleman ? there be a legend tell of the Emperor Domitian , that have hear of a jewish family , of the house of David , whence the ruler of the world be to spring , he send for its member in alarm , but quickly release they on observe that they have the hand of work - people be of just the opposite opinion with that Rabbi who stand wait at the gate of Rome in confidence that the Messiah would be find among the destitute who enter there . both Emperor and Rabbi be wrong in their trust of outward sign : poverty and poor clothe be no sign of inspiration , say Deronda to his inward objector , but they have go with it in some remarkable case . and to regard discipleship as out of the question because of they , would be mere dullness of imagination . 

 a more plausible reason for put discipleship out of the question be the strain of visionary excitement in Mordecai , which turn his wish into overmaster impression , and make he read outward fact as fulfillment . be such a temper of mind likely to accompany that wise estimate of consequence which be the only safeguard from fatal error , even to ennoble motive ? but it remain to be see whether that rare conjunction exist or not in Mordecai : perhaps his might be one of the nature where a wise estimate of consequence be fuse in the fire of that passionate belief which determine the consequence it believe in . the inspiration of the world have come in that way too : even strictly - measure science could hardly have get on without that forecasting ardor which feel the agitation of discovery beforehand , and have a faith in its preconception that surmount many failure of experiment . and in relation to human motif and action , passionate belief have a full efficacy . here enthusiasm may have the validity of proof , and happen in one soul , give the type of what will one day be general . 

 at least , Deronda argue , Mordecai 's visionary excitability be hardly a reason for conclude beforehand that he be not worth listen to except for pity sake . suppose he have introduce himself as one of the strict reasoner . do they form a body of man hitherto free from false conclusion and illusory speculation ? the dry argument have its hallucination , too hastily conclude that its net will now at last be large enough to hold the universe . man may dream in demonstration , and cut out an illusory world in the shape of axiom , definition , and proposition , with a final exclusion of fact sign Q.E.D. no formula for thinking will save we mortal from mistake in our imperfect apprehension of the matter to be think about . and since the unemotional intellect may carry we into a mathematical dreamland where nothing be but what be not , perhaps an emotional intellect may have absorb into its passionate vision of possibility some truth of what will be the more comprehensive massive life feeding theory with new material , as the sensibility of the artist seize combination which science explain and justifie . at any rate , presumption to the contrary be not to be trust . we must be patient with the inevitable makeshift of our human thinking , whether in its sum total or in the separate mind that have make the sum . Columbus have some impression about himself which we call superstition , and use some argument which we disapprove ; but he have also some sound physical conception , and he have the passionate patience of genius to make they tell on mankind . the world have make up its mind rather contemptuously about those who be deaf to Columbus . 

 " my contempt for they bind I to see that I do not   adopt their mistake on a small scale , " say Deronda , " and make myself deaf with the assumption that there can not be any momentous relation between this Jew and I , simply because he have clothe it in illusory notion . what I can be to he , or he to I , may not at all depend on his persuasion about the way we come together . to I the way seem make up of plainly discernible link . if I have not find Mirah , it be probable that I should not have begin to be specially interested in the Jews , and certainly I should not have go on that loitering search after an Ezra Cohen which make I pause at Ram 's book - shop and ask the price of Maimon . Mordecai , on his side , have his vision of a disciple , and he see I by their light ; I correspond well enough with the image his longing have create . he take I for one of his race . suppose that his impression the elderly Jew at Frankfort seem to have something like it suppose in spite of all presumption to the contrary , that his impression should somehow be prove true , and that I should come actually to share any of the idea he be devoted to ? this be the only question which really concern the effect of our meeting on my life . 

 " but if the issue should be quite different ? well , there will be something painful to go through . I shall almost inevitably have to be an active cause of that poor fellow 's crush disappointment . perhaps this issue be the one I have nee prepare myself for . I fear that no tenderness of mine can make his suffering light . would the alternative that I should not disappoint he be less painful to I ? " 

 here Deronda waver . feeling have lately be at work within he which have very much modify the reluctance he would formerly have have to think of himself as probably a Jew . and , if you like , he be romantic . that young energy and spirit of adventure which have help to create the world - wide legion of youthful hero go to seek the hidden token of their birth and its inheritance of task , give he a certain quivering interest in the bare possibility that he be enter on a track like all the more because the track be one of thought as well as action . 

 " the bare possibility . " he could not admit it to be more . the belief that his father be an Englishman only grow firm under the weak assault of unwarranted doubt . and that a moment should ever come in which that belief be declare a delusion , be something of which Deronda would not say , " I should be glad . " his life - long affection for Sir Hugo , strong than all his resentment , make he shrink from admit that wish . 

 which way soever the truth might lie , he repeat to himself what he have say to Mordecai that he could not without farth reason undertake to hasten its discovery . Nay , he be tempt now to regard his uncertainty as a condition to be cherish for the present . if further intercourse reveal nothing but illusion as what he be expect to share in , the want of any valid evidence that he be a Jew might save Mordecai the bad shock in the refusal of fraternity . it might even be justifiable to use the uncertainty on this point in keep up a suspense which would induce Mordecai to accept those office of friendship that Deronda long to urge on he . 

 these be the meditation that busy Deronda in the interval of four day before he could fulfill his promise to call for Mordecai at Ezra Cohen 's , Sir Hugo 's demand on he often last to an hour so late as to put the evening expedition to Holborn out of the question . 

 CHAPTER XLII . 

 " Wenn es eine Stutenleiter von Leiden giebt , so hat Israel die hŲchste Staffel erstiegen ; wen die Dauer der Schmerzen und die Geduld , mit welcher sie ertragen werden , adeln , so nehman es die Juden mit den Hochgeborenen aller Lšnder auf ; wenn eine Literatur reich genannt wird , die wenige klassische Trauerspiele besitzt , welcher Platz geb hrt dann einer Tragodie die anderthalb Jahrtausende wahrt , gedichtet und dargestellt von den Helden selber ? " ZUNZ : die Synagogale Poesie des Mittelalters . 

 " if there be rank in suffering , Israel take precedence of all the nation if the duration of sorrow and the patience with which they be bear ennoble , the Jews be among the aristocracy of every land if a literature be call rich in the possession of a few classic tragedy , what shall we say to a National Tragedy last for fifteen hundred year , in which the poet and the actor be also the hero ? " 

 Deronda have lately be read that passage of Zunz , and it occur to he by way of contrast when he be go to the Cohens , who certainly bear no obvious stamp of distinction in sorrow or in any other form of aristocracy . Ezra Cohen be not clothe in the sublime pathos of the martyr , and his taste for money - getting seem to be favor with that success which have be the most exasperating difference in the greed of Jews during all the age of their dispersion . this Jeshurun of a pawnbroker be not a symbol of the great jewish tragedy ; and yet be there not something typical in the fact that a life like Mordecai 's a frail incorporation of the national consciousness , breathe with difficult breath be nest in the self - gratulate ignorant prosperity of the Cohens ? 

 Glistening be the gladness in their face when Deronda reappear among they . Cohen himself take occasion to intimate that although the diamond ring , let alone a little long , would have breed more money , he do not mind that not a sixpence when compare with the pleasure of the woman and child in see a young gentleman whose first visit have be so agreeable that they have " do nothing but talk of it ever since . " Young Mrs. Cohen be very sorry that baby be asleep , and then very glad that Adelaide be not yet go to bed , entreat Deronda not to stay in the shop , but to go forthwith into the parlor to see " mother and the child . " he willingly accept the invitation , have provide himself with portable present ; a set of paper figure for Adelaide , and an ivory cup and ball for Jacob . 

 the grandmother have a pack of card before she and be make " plate " with the child . a plate have just be throw down and keep itself whole . 

 " stop ! " say Jacob , run to Deronda as he enter . " do not   tread on my plate . stop and see I throw it up again . " 

 Deronda comply , exchange a smile of understanding with the grandmother , and the plate bear several tossing before it come to piece ; then the visitor be allow to come forward and seat himself . he observe that the door from which Mordecai have issue on the former visit be now close , but he wish to show his interest in the Cohens before disclose a yet strong interest in their singular inmate . 

 it be not until he have Adelaide on his knee , and be set up the paper figure in their dance on the table , while Jacob be already practice with the cup and ball , that Deronda say 

 " be Mordecai in just now ? " 

 " where be he , Addy ? " say Cohen , who have seize an interval of business to come and look on . 

 " in the workroom there , " say his wife , nod toward the closed door . 

 " the fact be , sir , " say Cohen , " we do not   know what be come to he this last day or two . he be always what I may call a little touch , you know " here Cohen point to his own forehead " not quite so rational in all thing , like you and I ; but he be mostly wonderful regular and industrious so far as a poor creature can be , and take as much delight in the boy as anybody could . but this last day or two he be be move about like a sleep - walker , or else sit as still as a wax figure . " 

 " it be the disease , poor dear creature , " say the grandmother , tenderly . 
 " I doubt whether he can stand long against it . " 
 " no ; I think its only something he be get in his head . " say Mrs. Cohen the young . " he be be turn over write continually , and when I speak to he it take he ever so long to hear and answer . "

" but my wife and mother would not   part with he if he be a still bad encumbrance . it be not   that we do not   know the long and short of matter , but it be our principle . there 's fool do business at a loss and do not   know it . I be not one of them . " 

 " oh , Mordecai carry a blessing inside he , " say the grandmother . 

 " he be get something the matter inside he , " say Jacob , come up to correct this erratum of his grandmother 's . " he say he could not   talk to I , and he would not   have a bit o ' bun . "

I suppose he talk about his learning to you . it be uncommonly kind of you , sir ; for I do not   suppose there be much to be get out of it , else it would not   have leave he where he be . but there be the shop . " Cohen hurry out , and Jacob , who have be listen inconveniently near to Deronda 's elbow , say to he with oblige familiarity , " I will call Mordecai for you , if you like . " 

 " no , Jacob , " say his mother ; " open the door for the gentleman , and let he go in himself hush ! do not   make a noise . " 

 Skillful Jacob seem to enter into the play , and turn the handle of the door as noiselessly as possible , while Deronda go behind he and stand on the threshold . the small room be light only by a die fire and one candle with a shade over it . on the board fix under the window , various object of jewelry be scatter : some book be heap in the corner beyond they . " 

 immediately he turn without speak , seize his furred cap which lie near , and move to join Deronda . it be but a moment before they be both in the sitting - room , and Jacob , notice the change in his friend 's air and expression , seize he by the arm and say , " see my cup and ball ! " send the ball up close to Mordecai 's face , as something likely to cheer a convalescent . it be a sign of the relieved tension in Mordecai 's mind that he could smile and say , " fine , fine ! "

 Cohen , and he go back into the work - room and get they .

 but the grandmother only nod towards her son , who say promptly , " my side . my wife 's family be not in that line . but bless your soul ! ours be a sort of cleverness as good as gutta percha ; you can twist it which way you like . there be nothing some old gentleman will not   do if you set them to it . " here Cohen wink down at Jacob 's back , but it be doubtful whether this judicious allusiveness answer its purpose , for its subject give a nasal whinny laugh and stamp about singing , " old gentleman , old gentleman , " in chime cadence .

 " the Cohens seem to have an affection for you , " say Deronda , as soon as he and Mordecai be off the doorstep . 

 " and I for they , " be the immediate answer . " they have the heart of the Israelite within they , though they be as the horse and the mule , without understanding beyond the narrow path they tread . "

 " yes yes , I trust you . but it be true I have be uneasy , for the spirit of my youth have be stir within I , and this body be not strong enough to bear the beating of its wing . I be as a man bind and imprison through long year : behold he bring to speech of his fellow and his limb set free : he weep , he totter , the joy within he threaten to break and overthrow the tabernacle of flesh . "

 " no , that be my trouble that you do not come yesterday . for this be the evening of the club I speak of , and we might not have any minute alone until late , when all the rest be go . perhaps we have well seek another place . but I be use to that only . in new place the outer world press on I and narrow the inward vision . and the people there be familiar with my face . " 

 " I do not   mind the club if I be allow to go in , " say Deronda . " it be enough that you like this place well . if we have not enough time I will come again . what sort of club be it ? " 

 " it be call ' the Philosophers . ' they be few like the cedar of Lebanon poor man give to thought . but none so poor as I be : and sometimes visitor of high worldly rank have be bring . we be allow to introduce a friend , who be interested in our topic . each order beer or some other kind of drink , in payment for the room . Most of they smoke . I have go when I could , for there be other man of my race who come , and sometimes I have break silence . I have please myself with a faint likeness between these poor philosopher and the master who hand down the thought of our race the great Transmitters , who labor with their hand for scant bread , but preserve and enlarge for we the heritage of memory , and save the soul of Israel alive as a seed among the tomb . the heart please itself with faint resemblance . " 

 " I shall be very glad to go and sit among they , if that will suit you . it be a sort of meeting I should like to join in , " say Deronda , not without relief in the prospect of an interval before he go through the strain of his next private conversation with Mordecai . 

 in three minute they have open the glaze door with the red curtain , and be in the little parlor , hardly much more than fifteen foot square , where the gaslight shine through a slight haze of smoke on what to Deronda be a new and striking scene . half - a - dozen man of various age , from between twenty and thirty to fifty , all shabbily dress , most of they with clay pipe in their mouth , be listen with a look of concentrated intelligence to a man in a pepper - and - salt dress , with blonde hair , short nose , broad forehead and general breadth , who , hold his pipe slightly uplifted in the left hand , and beat his knee with the right , be just finish a quotation from Shelley   the comparison of the avalanche in his " Prometheus Unbound " 

  " as thought by thought be pile , till some great truth 
  be loosen , and the nation echo round . " 
 the entrance of the new - comer break the fixity of attention , and call for re - arrangement of seat in the too narrow semicircle round the fire - place and the table hold the glass , spare pipe and tobacco . this be the sober of club ; but sobriety be no reason why smoking and " take something " should be less imperiously need as a means of get a decent status in company and debate . Mordecai be receive with welcome voice which have a slight cadence of compassion in they , but naturally all glance pass immediately to his companion .

 Mordecai . " he have travel and study much . " 
 " be the gentleman anonymous ? be he a great ' unknown ? ' " say the broad - cheste quoter of Shelley , with a humorous air .

 in fact pure english blood   if leech or lancet can furnish we with the precise product   do not declare itself predominantly in the party at present assemble . Miller , the broad man , an exceptional second - hand bookseller who know the inside of book , have at least grand - parent who call themselves german , and possibly far - away ancestor who deny themselves to be Jews ; Buchan , the saddler , be Scotch ; Pash , the watchmaker , be a small , dark , vivacious , triple - bake Jew ; Gideon , the optical instrument maker , be a Jew of the red - haired , generous - feature type easily pass for Englishmen of unusually cordial manner : and Croop , the dark - eyed shoemaker , be probably more celtic than he know . only three would have be discernable everywhere as Englishman : the wood - inlayer Goodwin , well - build , open - faced , pleasant - voice ; the florid laboratory assistant Marrables ; and Lily , the pale , neat - face copying - clerk , whose light - brown hair be set up in a small parallelogram above his well - fill forehead , and whose shirt , take with an otherwise seedy costume , have a freshness that might be call insular , and perhaps even something narrow . 

 certainly a company select of the select among poor man , be draw together by a taste not prevalent even among the privileged heir of learn and its institution ; and not likely to amuse any gentleman in search of crime or low comedy as the ground of interest in people whose weekly income be only divisible into shilling . Deronda , even if he have not be more than usually inclined to gravity under the influence of what be pende between he and Mordecai , would not have set himself to find food for laughter in the various shade of departure from the tone of polished society sure to be observable in the air and talk of these man who have probably snatch knowledge as most of we snatch indulgence , make the utmost of scant opportunity . he look around he with the quiet air of respect habitual to he among equal , order whisky and water , and offer the content of his cigar - case , which , characteristically enough , he always carry and hardly ever use for his own behoof , have reason for not smoke himself , but like to indulge other . perhaps it be his weakness to be afraid of seem straight - laced , and turn himself into a sort of diagram instead of a growth which can exercise the guide attraction of fellowship . that he make a decidedly win impression on the company be prove by their show themselves no less at ease than before , and desirous of quickly resume their interrupted talk . 

 " this be what I call one of our touch - and - go night , sir , " say Miller , who be implicitly accept as a sort of moderator on address Deronda by way of explanation , and nod toward each person whose name he mention . " sometimes we stick pretty close to the point . but tonight our friend Pash , there , bring up the law of progress ; and we get on statistic ; then Lily , there , say we know well enough before count that in the same state of society the same sort of thing would happen , and it be no more wonder that quantity should remain the same , than that quality should remain the same , for in relation to society number be quality the number of drunkard be a quality in society the number be an index to the quality , and give we no instruction , only set we to consider the cause of difference between different social state Lily say this , we go off on the cause of social change , and when you come in I be go upon the power of idea , which I hold to be the main transforming cause . " 

 " I do not   hold with you there , Miller , " say Goodwin , the inlayer , more concerned to carry on the subject than to wait for a word from the new guest . " for either you mean so many sort of thing by idea that I get no knowledge by what you say , any more than if you say light be a cause ; or else you mean a particular sort of idea , and then I go against your meaning as too narrow . for , look at it in one way , all action man put a bit of thought into be idea say , sow seed , or make a canoe , or bake clay ; and such idea as these work themselves into life and go on grow with it , but they can not   go apart from the material that set they to work and make a medium for they . it be the nature of wood and stone yield to the knife that raise the idea of shape they , and with plenty of wood and stone the shaping will go on . I look at it , that such idea as be mixed straight away with all the other element of life be powerful along with them . the slow the mixing , the less power they have . and as to the cause of social change , I look at it in this way idea be a sort of parliament , but there be a commonwealth outside and a good deal of the commonwealth be work at change without know what the parliament be do . " 

 " but if you take ready mixing as your test of power , " say Pash , ' some of the least practical idea beat everything . they spread without be understand , and enter into the language without be think of . " 

 " they may act by change the distribution of gas , " say Marrables ; " instrument be get so fine now , man may come to register the spread of a theory by observed change in the atmosphere and correspond change in the nerve . " 

 " yes , " say Pash , his dark face light up rather impishly , " there be the idea of nationality ; I dare say the wild ass be snuff it , and get more gregarious . "

 " grant , Mordecai , " say Pash , quite good - humoredly . " and as the feeling of nationality be die , I take the idea to be no well than a ghost , already walk to announce the death . " 

 " a sentiment may seem to be die and yet revive into strong life , " say Deronda . " nation have revive . we may live to see a great outburst of force in the Arabs , who be be inspire with a new zeal . " 

 " amen , amen , " say Mordecai , look at Deronda with a delight which be the beginning of recovered energy : his attitude be more upright , his face be less worn . 

 " that may hold with backward nation , " say Pash , " but with we in Europe the sentiment of nationality be destine to die out . it will last a little long in the quarter where oppression last , but nowhere else . the whole current of progress be set against it . " 

 " ay , " say Buchan , in a rapid thin Scotch tone which be like the letting in of a little cool air on the conversation , " ye've   do well to bring we round to the point . Ye're all agree that society change not always and everywhere but on the whole and in the long run . now , with all deference , I would beg t ' observe that we have get to examine the nature of change before we have a warrant to call they progress , which word be suppose to include a bettering , though I apprehend it to be ill - choose for that purpose , since mere motion onward may carry we to a bog or a precipice . and the question I would put be three : be all change in the direction of progress ? if not , how shall we discern which change be progress and which not ? and thirdly , how far and in what way can we act upon the course of change so as to promote it where it be beneficial , and divert it where it be injurious ? " 

 but Buchan 's attempt to impose his method on the talk be a failure . 
 Lily immediately say 
 " change and progress be merge in the idea of development . the law of development be be discover , and change take place accord to they be necessarily progressive ; that be to say , it we have any notion of progress or improvement oppose to they , the notion be a mistake . " 

 " I really can not   see how you arrive at that sort of certitude about change by call they development , " say Deronda . " there will still remain the degree of inevitableness in relation to our own will and act , and the degree of wisdom in hastening or retarding ; there will still remain the danger of mistake a tendency which should be resist for an inevitable law that we must adjust ourselves to , which seem to I as bad a superstition or false god as any that have be set up without the ceremony of philosophising . " 

 " that be a truth , " say Mordecai . " woe to the man who see no place for resistance in this generation ! I believe in a growth , a passage , and a new unfolding of life whereof the seed be more perfect , more charge with the element that be pregnant with diviner form . the life of a people grow , it be knit together and yet expand , in joy and sorrow , in thought and action ; it absorb the thought of other nation into its own form , and give back the thought as new wealth to the world ; it be a power and an organ in the great body of the nation . but there may come a check , an arrest ; memory may be stifle , and love may be faint for the lack of they ; or memory may shrink into withered relic the soul of a people , whereby they know themselves to be one , may seem to be die for want of common action . but who shall say , ' the fountain of their life be dry up , they shall forever cease to be a nation ? ' who shall say it ? not he who feel the life of his people stir within his own . shall he say , ' that way event be wend , I will not resist ? ' his very soul be resistance , and be as a seed of fire that may enkindle the soul of multitude , and make a new pathway for event . " 

 " I do not   deny patriotism , " say Gideon , " but we all know you have a particular meaning , Mordecai . you know Mordecai 's way of thinking , I suppose . " here Gideon have turn to Deronda , who sit next to he , but without wait for an answer he go on . " I be a rational Jew myself . I stand by my people as a sort of family relation , and I be for keep up our worship in a rational way . I do not   approve of our people get baptise , because I do not   believe in a Jew 's conversion to the Gentile part of Christianity . and now we have political equality , there be no excuse for a pretense of that sort . but I be for get rid of all of our superstition and exclusiveness . there be no reason now why we should not   melt gradually into the population we live among . that be the order of the day in point of progress . I would as soon my child marry Christians as Jews . and I be for the old maxim , ' a man 's country be where he be well off . ' " 

 " that country be not so easy to find , Gideon , " say the rapid Pash , with a shrug and grimace . " you get ten shilling a - week more than I do , and have only half the number of child . if somebody will introduce a brisk trade in watch among the ' Jerusalem ware , ' I will go eh , Mordecai , what do you say ? " 

 Deronda , all ear for these hint of Mordecai 's opinion , be inwardly wonder at his persistence in come to this club . for an enthusiastic spirit to meet continually the fixed indifference of man familiar with the object of his enthusiasm be the acceptance of a slow martyrdom , beside which the fate of a missionary tomahawk without any considerate rejection of his doctrine seem hardly worthy of compassion . but Mordecai give no sign of shrinking : this be a moment of spiritual fullness , and he care more for the utterance of his faith than for its immediate reception . with a fervor which have no temper in it , but seem rather the rush of feeling in the opportunity of speech , he answer Pash : 

 " what I say be , let every man keep far away from the brotherhood and inheritance he despise . thousand on thousand of our race have mix with the Gentiles as Celt with Saxon , and they may inherit the blessing that belong to the Gentile . you can not follow they . you be one of the multitude over this globe who must walk among the nation and be know as Jews , and with word on their lip which mean , ' I wish I have not be bear a Jew , I disown any bond with the long travail of my race , I will outdo the Gentile in mock at our separateness , ' they all the while feel breathe on they the breath of contempt because they be Jews , and they will breathe it back poisonously . can a fresh - make garment of citizenship weave itself straightway into the flesh and change the slow deposit of eighteen century ? what be the citizenship of he who walk among a people he have no hardy kindre and fellowship with , and have lose the sense of brotherhood with his own race ? it be a charter of selfish ambition and rivalry in low greed . he be an alien of spirit , whatever he may be in form ; he suck the blood of mankind , he be not a man , share in no love , share in no subjection of the soul , he mock it all . be it not truth I speak , Pash ? " 

 " not exactly , Mordecai , " say Pash , " if you mean that I think the bad of myself for be a Jew . what I thank our father for be that there be few blockhead among we than among other race . but perhaps you be right in think the Christians do not   like I so well for it . " 

 " Catholics and Protestants have not like each other much well , " say the genial Gideon . " we must wait patiently for prejudice to die out . many of our people be on a footing with the good , and there be be a good filtering of our blood into high family . I be for make our expectation rational . " 

 " and so be I ! " say Mordecai , quickly , lean forward with the eagerness of one who plead in some decisive crisis , his long , thin hand clasp together on his lap . " I , too , claim to be a rational Jew . but what be it to be rational what be it to feel the light of the divine reason grow strong within and without ? it be to see more and more of the hide bond that bind and consecrate change as a dependent growth yea , consecrate it with kinship : the past become my parent and the future stretch toward I the appeal arm of child . be it rational to drain away the sap of special kindre that make the family of man rich in interchanged wealth , and various as the forest be various with the glory of the cedar and the palm ? when it be rational to say , ' I know not my father or my mother , let my child be alien to I , that no prayer of mine may touch they , ' then it will be rational for the Jew to say , ' I will seek to know no difference between I and the Gentile , I will not cherish the prophetic consciousness of our nationality let the hebrew cease to be , and let all his memorial be antiquarian trifle , dead as the wall - painting of a conjecture race . yet let his child learn by rote the speech of the Greek , where he abjure his fellow - citizen by the bravery of those who fight foremost at Marathon let he learn to say that be noble in the Greek , that be the spirit of an immortal nation ! but the Jew have no memory that bind he to action ; let he laugh that his nation be degrade from a nation ; let he hold the monument of his law which carry within its frame the breath of social justice , of charity , and of household sanctity let he hold the energy of the prophet , the patient care of the Masters , the fortitude of martyr generation , as mere stuff for a professorship . the business of the Jew in all thing be to be even as the rich Gentile . " 

 Mordecai throw himself back in his chair , and there be a moment 's silence . not one member of the club share his point of view or his emotion ; but his whole personality and speech have on they the effect of a dramatic representation which have some pathos in it , though no practical consequence ; and usually he be at once indulge and contradict . Deronda 's mind go back upon what must have be the tragic pressure of outward condition hinder this man , whose force he feel to be tell on himself , from make any world for his thought in the mind of other like a poet among people of a strange speech , who may have a poetry of their own , but have no ear for his cadence , no answer thrill to his discovery of the latent virtue in his mother tongue . 

 the cool Buchan be the first to speak , and hint the loss of time . " I submit , " say he , " that ye're travel away from the question I put concern progress . " 

 " say they be levant , Buchan , " say Miller , who like his joke , and would not have object to be call Voltairian . " never mind . let we have a jewish night ; we have   not have one for a long while . let we take the discussion on jewish ground . I suppose we have   no prejudice here ; we be all philosopher ; and we like our friend Mordecai , Pash , and Gideon , as well as if they be no more kin to Abraham than the rest of we . we be all relate through Adam , until far show to the contrary , and if you look into history we have   all get some discreditable forefather . so I mean no offence when I say I do not   think any great thing of the part the jewish people have play in the world . what then ? I think they be iniquitously deal by in past time . and I suppose we do not   want any man to be maltreat , white , black , brown , or yellow I know I have   just give my half - crown to the contrary . and that remind I , I have   a curious old german book I can not   read it myself , but a friend of mine be read out of it to I the other day about the prejudicie against the Jews , and the story use to be tell against them , and what do you think one be ? why , that they be punish with a bad odor in their body ; and that , say the author , date 1715   I have   just be price and mark the book this very morning   that be true , for the ancient speak of it . but then , he say , the other thing be fable , such as that the odor go away all at once when they be baptize , and that every one of the ten tribe , mind you , all the ten be concern in the crucifixion , have get a particular punishment over and above the smell : Asher , I remember , have the right arm a handbreadth short than the left , and Naphthali have pig 's ear and a smell of live pork . what do you think of that ? there be be a good deal of fun make of rabbinical fable , but in point of fable my opinion be , that all over the world it be six of one and half - a - dozen of the other . however , as I say before , I hold with the philosopher of the last century that the Jews have play no great part as a people , though Pash will have it they be clever enough to beat all the rest of the world . but if so , I ask , why have not   they do it ? " 

 " for the same reason that the clever man in the country do not   get themselves or their idea into Parliament , " say the ready Pash ; " because the blockhead be too many for them . " 

 " that be a vain question , " say Mordecai , " whether our people would beat the rest of the world . each nation have its own work , and be a member of the world , enrich by the work of each . but it be true , as Jehuda - ha - Levi first say , that Israel be the heart of mankind , if we mean by heart the core of affection which bind a race and its family in dutiful love , and the reverence for the human body which lift the need of our animal life into religion , and the tenderness which be merciful to the poor and weak and to the dumb creature that wear the yoke for we . " 

 " they be not behind any nation in arrogance , " say Lily ; " and if they have get in the rear , it have not be because they be over - modest . " 

 " oh , every nation brag in its turn , " say Miller . 

 " yes , " say Pash , " and some of they in the Hebrew text . " 

 " well , whatever the Jews contribute at one time , they be a stand - still people , " say Lily . " they be the type of obstinate adherence to the superannuated . they may show good ability when they take up liberal idea , but as a race they have no development in they . " 

 " that be false ! " say Mordecai , lean forward again with his former eagerness . " let their history be know and examine ; let the seed be sift , let its beginning be trace to the weed of the wilderness the more glorious will be the energy that transform it . where else be there a nation of whom it may be as truly say that their religion and law and moral life mingle as the stream of blood in the heart and make one growth where else a people who keep and enlarge their spiritual store at the very time when they be hat with a hatred as fierce as the forest fire that chase the wild beast from his covert ? there be a fable of the Roman , that swim to save his life he hold the roll of his writing between his tooth and save they from the water . but how much more than that be true of our race ? they struggle to keep their place among the nation like hero yea , when the hand be hack off , they clung with their tooth ; but when the plow and the harrow have pass over the last visible sign of their national covenant , and the fruitfulness of their land be stifle with the blood of the sower and planter , they say , ' the spirit be alive , let we make it a last habitation last because movable so that it may be carry from generation to generation , and our son unborn may be rich in the thing that have be , and possess a hope build on an unchangeable foundation . ' they say it and they work it , though often breathe with scant life , as in a coffin , or as lie wound amid a heap of slain . hoot and scar like the unknown dog , the Hebrew make himself envy for his wealth and wisdom , and be bleed of they to fill the bath of Gentile luxury ; he absorb knowledge , he diffuse it ; his dispersed race be a new Phoenicia work the mine of Greece and carry their product to the world . the native spirit of our tradition be not to stand still , but to use record as a seed and draw out the compressed virtue of law and prophecy ; and while the Gentile , who have say , ' what be yours be ours , and no long your , ' be read the letter of our law as a dark inscription , or be turn its parchment into shoe - sol for an army rabid with lust and cruelty , our master be still enlarge and illuminate with fresh - fed interpretation . but the dispersion be wide , the yoke of oppression be a spiked torture as well as a load ; the exile be force afar among brutish people , where the consciousness of his race be no clear to he than the light of the sun to our father in the roman persecution , who have their hiding - place in a cave , and know not that it be day save by the dimmer burning of their candle . what wonder that multitude of our people be ignorant , narrow , superstitious ? what wonder ? " 

 here Mordecai , whose seat be next the fireplace , rise and lean his arm on the little shelf ; his excitement have rise , though his voice , which have begin with unusual strength , be get hoarser . 

 " what wonder ? the night be unto they , that they have no vision ; in their darkness they be unable to divine ; the sun be go down over the prophet , and the day be dark above they ; their observance be as nameless relic . but which among the chief of the Gentile nation have not an ignorant multitude ? they scorn our people 's ignorant observance ; but the most accursed ignorance be that which have no observance sink to the cunning greed of the fox , to which all law be no more than a trap or the cry of the worrying hound . there be a degradation deep down below the memory that have wither into superstition . in the multitude of the ignorant on three continent who observe our rite and make the confession of the divine Unity , the soul of Judaism be not dead . revive the organic centre : let the unity of Israel which have make the growth and form of its religion be an outward reality . look toward a land and a polity , our dispersed people in all the end of the earth may share the dignity of a national life which have a voice among the people of the East and the West which will plant the wisdom and skill of our race so that it may be , as of old , a medium of transmission and understanding . let that come to pass , and the live warmth will spread to the weak extremity of Israel , and superstition will vanish , not in the lawlessness of the renegade , but in the illumination of great fact which widen feeling , and make all knowledge alive as the young offspring of beloved memory . " 

 Mordecai 's voice have sink , but with the hectic brilliancy of his gaze it be not the less impressive . his extraordinary excitement be certainly due to Deronda 's presence : it be to Deronda that he be speak , and the moment have a testamentary solemnity for he which rally all his power . yet the presence of those other familiar man promote expression , for they embody the indifference which give a resistant energy to his speech . not that he look at Deronda : he seem to see nothing immediately around he , and if any one have grasp he he would probably not have know it . again the former word come back to Deronda 's mind , " you must hope my hope see the vision I point to behold a glory where I behold it . " they come now with gather pathos . before he stand , as a living , suffer reality , what hitherto he have only see as an effort of imagination , which , in its comparative faintness , yet carry a suspicion , of be exaggerated : a man steep in poverty and obscurity , weaken by disease , consciously within the shadow of advance death , but live an intense life in an invisible past and future , careless of his personal lot , except for its possible make some obstruction to a conceive good which he would never share except as a brief inward vision a day afar off , whose sun would never warm he , but into which he throw his soul 's desire , with a passion often want to the personal motif of healthy youth . it be something more than a grandiose transfiguration of the parental love that toil , renounce , endure , resist the suicidal prompting of despair all because of the little one , whose future become present to the yearn gaze of anxiety . 

 all eye be fix on Mordecai as he sit down again , and none with unkindness ; but it happen that the one who feel the most kindly be the most prompt to speak in opposition . this be the genial and rational Gideon , who also be not without a sense that he be address the guest of the evening . he say 

 " you have your own way of look at thing , Mordecai , and as you say , your own way seem to you rational . I know you do not   hold with the restoration of Judea by miracle , and so on ; but you be as well aware as I be that the subject have be mix with a heap of nonsense both by Jews and Christians . and as to the connection of our race with Palestine , it have be pervert by superstition till it be as demoralize as the old poor - law . the raff and scum go there to be maintain like able - bodied pauper , and to be take special care of by the angel Gabriel when they die . it be no use fight against fact . we must look where they point ; that be what I call rationality . the most learned and liberal man among we who be attach to our religion be for clear our liturgy of all such notion as a literal fulfillment of the prophecy about restoration , and so on . prune it of a few useless rite and literal interpretation of that sort , and our religion be the simple of all religion , and make no barrier , but a union , between we and the rest of the world . " 

 " as plain as a pike - staff , " say Pash , with an ironical laugh . " you pluck it up by the root , strip off the leaf and bark , shave off the knot , and smooth it at top and bottom ; put it where you will , it will do no harm , it will never sprout . you may make a handle of it , or you may throw it on the bonfire of scour rubbish . I do not   see why our rubbish be to be hold sacred any more than the rubbish of Brahmanism or Buddhism . " 

 " no , " say Mordecai , " no , Pash , because you have lose the heart of the Jew . community be feel before it be call good . I praise no superstition , I praise the living fountain of enlarging belief . what be growth , completion , development ? you begin with that question , I apply it to the history of our people . I say that the effect of our separateness will not be complete and have its high transformation unless our race take on again the character of a nationality . that be the fulfillment of the religious trust that mould they into a people , whose life have make half the inspiration of the world . what be it to I that the ten tribe be lose untraceably , or that multitude of the child of Judah have mix themselves with the Gentile population as a river with river ? behold our people still ! their skirt spread afar ; they be tear and soiled and trodden on ; but there be a jeweled breastplate . let the wealthy man , the monarch of commerce , the learn in all knowledge , the skilful in all art , the speaker , the political counselor , who carry in their vein the Hebrew blood which have maintain its vigor in all climate , and the pliancy of the Hebrew genius for which difficulty mean new device let they say , ' we will lift up a standard , we will unite in a labor hard but glorious like that of Moses and Ezra , a labor which shall be a worthy fruit of the long anguish whereby our father maintain their separateness , refuse the ease of falsehood . ' they have wealth enough to redeem the soil from debauched and paupered conqueror ; they have the skill of the statesman to devise , the tongue of the orator to persuade . and be there no prophet or poet among we to make the ear of Christian Europe tingle with shame at the hideous obloquy of christian strife which the Turk gaze at as at the fighting of beast to which he have lend an arena ? there be store of wisdom among we to found a new jewish polity , grand , simple , just , like the old a republic where there be equality of protection , an equality which shine like a star on the forehead of our ancient community , and give it more than the brightness of western freedom amid the despotism of the East . then our race shall have an organic centre , a heart and brain to watch and guide and execute ; the outraged Jew shall have a defense in the court of nation , as the outraged Englishman of America . and the world will gain as Israel gain . for there will be a community in the van of the East which carry the culture and the sympathy of every great nation in its bosom : there will be a land set for a halting - place of enmity , a neutral ground for the East as Belgium be for the West . difficulty ? I know there be difficulty . but let the spirit of sublime achievement move in the great among our people , and the work will begin . " 

 " ay , we may safely admit that , Mordecai , " say Pash . " when there be great man on ' Change , and high - fly professor convert to your doctrine , difficulty will vanish like smoke . " 

 Deronda , incline by nature to take the side of those on whom the arrow of scorn be fall , could not help reply to Pash 's outfling , and say 

 " if we look back to the history of effort which have make great change , it be astonish how many of they seem hopeless to those who look on in the beginning . 

 " take what we have all hear and see something of the effort after the unity of Italy , which we be sure soon to see accomplish to the very last boundary . look into Mazzini 's account of his first yearning , when he be a boy , after a restore greatness and a new freedom to Italy , and of his first effort as a young man to rouse the same feeling in other young man , and get they to work toward a united nationality . almost everything seem against he ; his countryman be ignorant or indifferent , government hostile , Europe incredulous . of course the scorner often seem wise . yet you see the prophecy lie with he . as long as there be a remnant of national consciousness , I suppose nobody will deny that there may be a new stirring of memory and hope which may inspire arduous action . " 

 " amen , " say Mordecai , to whom Deronda 's word be a cordial . " what be need be the leaven what be need be the seed of fire . the heritage of Israel be beat in the pulse of million ; it live in their vein as a power without understanding , like the morning exultation of herd ; it be the inborn half of memory , move as in a dream among writing on the wall , which it see dimly but can not divide into speech . let the torch of visible community be light ! let the reason of Israel disclose itself in a great outward deed , and let there be another great migration , another choosing of Israel to be a nationality whose member may still stretch to the end of the earth , even as the son of England and Germany , whom enterprise carry afar , but who still have a national hearth and a tribunal of national opinion . will any say ' it can not be ' ? baruch Spinoza have not a faithful jewish heart , though he have suck the life of his intellect at the breast of jewish tradition . he lay bare his father 's nakedness and say , ' they who scorn he have the high wisdom . ' yet Baruch Spinoza confess , he see not why Israel should not again be a choose nation . who say that the history and literature of our race be dead ? be they not as live as the history and literature of Greece and Rome , which have inspire revolution , enkindle the thought of Europe , and make the unrighteous power tremble ? these be an inheritance dig from the tomb . ours be an inheritance that have never cease to quiver in million of human frame . " 

 Mordecai have stretch his arm upward , and his long thin hand quiver in the air for a moment after he have cease to speak . Gideon be certainly a little move , for though there be no long pause before he make a remark in objection , his tone be more mild and deprecatory than before ; Pash , meanwhile , press his lip together , rub his black head with both his hand and wrinkle his brow horizontally , with the expression of one who differ from every speaker , but do not think it worth while to say so . there be a sort of human paste that when it come near the fire of enthusiasm be only bake into hard shape . 

 " it may seem well enough on one side to make so much of our memory and inheritance as you do , Mordecai , " say Gideon ; " but there be another side . it be not   all gratitude and harmless glory . our people have inherit a good deal of hatred . there be a pretty lot of curse still fly about , and stiff settle rancor inherit from the time of persecution . how will you justify keep one sort of memory and throw away the other ? there be ugly debt stand on both side . " 

 " I justify the choice as all other choice be justify , " say Mordecai . " I cherish nothing for the jewish nation , I seek nothing for they , but the good which promise good to all the nation . the spirit of our religious life , which be one with our national life , be not hatred of aught but wrong . the Master have say , an offence against man be bad than an offence against God . but what wonder if there be hatred in the breast of Jews , who be child of the ignorant and oppress what wonder , since there be hatred in the breast of Christians ? our national life be a grow light . let the central fire be kindle again , and the light will reach afar . the degraded and scorn of our race will learn to think of their sacred land , not as a place for saintly beggary to await death in loathsome idleness , but as a republic where the jewish spirit manifest itself in a new order found on the old , purify and enrich by the experience our great son have gather from the life of the age . how long be it ? only two century since a vessel carry over the ocean the beginning of the great north american nation . the people grow like meeting water they be various in habit and sect there come a time , a century ago , when they need a polity , and there be hero of peace among they . what have they to form a polity with but memory of Europe , correct by the vision of a well ? let our wise and wealthy show themselves hero . they have the memory of the East and West , and they have the full vision of a well . a new Persia with a purified religion magnify itself in art and wisdom . so will a new Judaea , poise between East and West a covenant of reconciliation . will any say , the prophetic vision of your race have be hopelessly mixed with folly and bigotry : the angel of progress have no message for Judaism it be a half - bury city for the pay worker to lay open the water be rush by it as a forsaken field ? I say that the strong principle of growth lie in human choice . the son of Judah have to choose that God may again choose they . the messianic time be the time when Israel shall will the planting of the national ensign . the Nile overflow and rush onward : the Egyptian could not choose the overflow , but he choose to work and make channel for the fructifying water , and Egypt become the land of corn . shall man , whose soul be set in the royalty of discernment and resolve , deny his rank and say , I be an onlooker , ask no choice or purpose of I ? that be the blasphemy of this time . the divine principle of our race be action , choice , resolve memory . let we contradict the blasphemy , and help to will our own well future and the well future of the world not renounce our high gift and say , ' let we be as if we be not among the population ; ' but choose our full heritage , claim the brotherhood of our nation , and carry into it a new brotherhood with the nation of the Gentiles . the vision be there ; it will be fulfil . " 

 with the last sentence , which be no more than a loud whisper , Mordecai let his chin sink on his breast and his eyelid fall . no one speak . it be not the first time that he have insist on the same idea , but he be see to - night in a new phase . the quiet tenacity of his ordinary self differ as much from his present exaltation of mood as a man in private talk , give reason for a revolution of which no sign be discernable , differ from one who feel himself an agent in a revolution begin . the dawn of fulfillment bring to his hope by Deronda 's presence have work Mordecai 's conception into a state of impassioned conviction , and he have find strength in his excitement to pour forth the unlocked flood of emotive argument , with a sense of haste as at a crisis which must be seize . but now there have come with the quiescence of fatigue a sort of thankful wonder that he have speak a contemplation of his life as a journey which have come at last to this bourne . after a great excitement , the ebbing strength of impulse be apt to leave we in this aloofness from our active self . and in the moment after Mordecai have sink his head , his mind be wander along the path of his youth , and all the hope which have end in bring he hither . 

 every one feel that the talk be end , and the tone of phlegmatic discussion make unseasonable by Mordecai 's high - pitch solemnity . it be as if they have come together to hear the blowing of the shophar , and have nothing to do now but to disperse . the movement be unusually general , and in less than ten minute the room be empty of all except Mordecai and Deronda . " good - night " have be give to Mordecai , but it be evident he have not hear they , for he remain rapt and motionless . Deronda would not disturb this needful rest , but wait for a spontaneous movement . 

 CHAPTER XLIII . 

  " my spirit be too weak ; mortality 
  Weighs heavily on I like unwilling sleep , 
  and each imagine pinnacle and steep 
  of godlike hardship tell I I must die 
  like a sick eagle look at the sky . " 
  KEATS . 
 after a few minute the unwonted stillness have penetrate Mordecai 's consciousness , and he look up at Deronda , not in the least with bewilderment and surprise , but with a gaze full of reposing satisfaction . Deronda rise and place his chair nearer , where there could be no imagined need for raise the voice . Mordecai feel the action as a patient feel the gentleness that ease his pillow . he begin to speak in a low tone , as if he be only think articulately , not try to reach an audience . 

 " in the doctrine of the Cabbala , soul be bear again and again in new body till they be perfect and purify , and a soul liberate from a wear - out body may join the fellow - soul that need it , that they may be perfect together , and their earthly work accomplish . then they will depart from the mortal region , and leave place for new soul to be bear out of the store in the eternal bosom . it be the linger imperfection of the soul already bear into the mortal region that hinder the birth of new soul and the preparation of the messianic time : thus the mind have give shape to what be hide , as the shadow of what be know , and have speak truth , though it be only in parable . when my long - wander soul be liberate from this weary body , it will join your , and its work will be perfect . " 

 Mordecai 's pause seem an appeal which Deronda 's feeling would not let he leave unanswered . he try to make it truthful ; but for Mordecai 's ear it be inevitably fill with unspoken meaning . he only say 

 " everything I can in conscience do to make your life effective I will do . " 

 " I know it , " say Mordecai , in a tone of quiet certainty which dispense with further assurance . " I hear it . you see it all you be by my side on the mount of vision , and behold the path of fulfillment which other deny . " 

 he be silent a moment or two , and then go on meditatively 

 " you will take up my life where it be break . I feel myself back in that day when my life be break . the bright morning sun be on the quay it be at Trieste the garment of man from all nation shine like jewel the boat be push off the greek vessel that would land we at Beyrout be to start in an hour . I be go with a merchant as his clerk and companion . I say , I shall behold the land and people of the East , and I shall speak with a full vision . I breathe then as you do , without labor ; I have the light step and the endurance of youth , I could fast , I could sleep on the hard ground . I have wed poverty , and I love my bride for poverty to I be freedom . my heart exult as if it have be the heart of Moses ben Maimon , strong with the strength of three score year , and know the work that be to fill they . it be the first time I have be south ; the soul within I feel its former sun ; and stand on the quay , where the ground I stand on seem to send forth light , and the shadow have an azure glory as of spirit become visible , I feel myself in the flood of a glorious life , wherein my own small year - count existence seem to melt , so that I know it not ; and a great sob arise within I as at the rush of water that be too strong a bliss . so I stand there await my companion ; and I see he not till he say : ' Ezra , I have be to the post and there be your letter . ' " 

 " Ezra ! " exclaim Deronda , unable to contain himself . 

 " Ezra , " repeat Mordecai , affirmatively , engross in memory . " I be expect a letter ; for I write continually to my mother . and that sound of my name be like the touch of a wand that recall I to the body wherefrom I have be release as it be to mingle with the ocean of human existence , free from the pressure of individual bondage . I open the letter ; and the name come again as a cry that would have disturb I in the bosom of heaven , and make I yearn to reach where that sorrow be ' Ezra , my son ! ' "

I leave the sunshine , and travel into freeze cold . in the last stage I spend a night in exposure to cold and snow . and that be the beginning of this slow death . "

 CHAPTER XLVI . 

 " if any one should importune I to give a reason why I love he , I feel it could no otherwise be express than by make answer , ' because it be he , because it be I. ' there be , beyond what I be able to say , I know not what inexplicable power that bring on this union . " MONTAIGNE : on Friendship . 

 the time have come to prepare Mordecai for the revelation of the restore sister and for the change of abode which be desirable before Mirah 's meeting with her brother . Mrs. Meyrick , to whom Deronda have confide everything except Mordecai 's peculiar relation to himself , have be active in help he to find a suitable lodging in Brompton , not many minute ' walk from her own house , so that the brother and sister would be within reach of her motherly care . her happy mixture of scottish fervor and gallic liveliness have enable she to keep the secret close from the girl as well as from Hans , any betrayal to they be likely to reach Mirah in some way that would raise an agitate suspicion , and spoil the important opening of that work which be to secure her independence , as we rather arbitrarily call one of the more arduous and dignified form of our dependence . and both Mrs. Meyrick and Deronda have more reason than they could have express for desire that Mirah should be able to maintain herself . perhaps " the little mother " be rather help in her secrecy by some dubiousness in her sentiment about the remarkable brother describe to she ; and certainly if she feel any joy and anticipatory admiration , it be due to her faith in Deronda 's judgment . the consumption be a sorrowful fact that appeal to her tenderness ; but how be she to be very glad of an enthusiasm which , to tell the truth , she could only contemplate as jewish pertinacity , and as rather an undesirable introduction among they all of a man whose conversation would not be more modern and encouraging than that of Scott 's covenanter ? her mind be anything but prosaic , and have her soberer share of Mab 's delight in the romance of Mirah 's story and of her abode with they ; but the romantic or unusual in real life require some adaptation . we sit up at night to read about Sakya - Mouni , St. Francis , or Oliver Cromwell ; but whether we should be glad for any one at all like they to call on we the next morning , still more , to reveal himself as a new relation , be quite another affair . besides , Mrs. Meyrick have hop , as her child do , that the intensity of Mirah 's feeling about Judaism would slowly subside , and be merge in the gradually deepen current of love interchange with her new friend . in fact , her secret favorite continuation of the romance have be no discovery of jewish relation , but something much more favorable to the hope she discern in Hans . and now here be a brother who would dip Mirah 's mind over again in the deep dye of jewish sentiment . she could not help say to Deronda

I be sure he be good . but I never do like anything fanatical . I suppose I hear a little too much preaching in my youth and lose my palate for it . " 

 " I do not   think you will find that Mordecai obtrude any preaching , " say Deronda . " he be not what I should call fanatical . I call a man fanatical when his enthusiasm be narrow and hoodwinked , so that he have no sense of proportion , and become unjust and unsympathetic to man who be out of his own track . Mordecai be an enthusiast ; I should like to keep that word for the high order of mind those who care supremely for grand and general benefit to mankind . he be not a strictly orthodox Jew , and be full of allowance for other ; his conformity in many thing be an allowance for the condition of other Jews . the people he live with be as fond of he as possible , and they can not   in the least understand his idea . " 

 " oh , well , I can live up to the level of the pawnbroker 's mother , and like he for what I see to be good in he ; and for what I do not   see the merit of I will take your word . accord to your definition , I suppose one might be fanatical in worship common - sense ; for my poor husband use to say the world would be a poor place if there be nothing but common - sense in it . however , Mirah 's brother will have good bedding that I have take care of ; and I shall have this extra window paste up with paper to prevent draught . "   the conversation be take place in the destine lodging .
