 CHAPTER XLVIII . 

 ' tis hard and ill - pay task to order all thing beforehand by the rule of our own security , as be well hint by Machiavelli concern Caesar Borgia , who , saith he , have think of all that might occur on his father 's death , and have provide against every evil chance save only one : it have never come into his mind that when his father die , his own death would quickly follow . 

 Grandcourt 's importance as a subject of this realm be of the grandly passive kind which consist in the inheritance of land . political and social movement touch he only through the wire of his rental , and his most careful biographer need not have read up on Schleswig - Holstein , the policy of Bismarck , trade - union , household suffrage , or even the last commercial panic . he glance over the good newspaper column on these topic , and his view on they can hardly be say to have want breadth , since he embrace all Germans , all commercial man , and all voter liable to use the wrong kind of soap , under the general epithet of " brute ; " but he take no action on these much - agitated question beyond look from under his eyelid at any man who mention they , and retain a silence which serve to shake the opinion of timid thinker . 

 but Grandcourt , within his own sphere of interest , show some of the quality which have enter into triumphal diplomacy of the wild continental sort . 

 no movement of Gwendolen in relation to Deronda escape he . he would have deny that he be jealous ; because jealousy would have imply some doubt of his own power to hinder what he have determine against . that his wife should have more inclination to another man 's society than to his own would not pain he : what he require be that she should be as fully aware as she would have be of a locked hand - cuff , that her inclination be helpless to decide anything in contradiction with his resolve . however much of vacillate whim there might have be in his entrance on matrimony , there be no vacillating in his interpretation of the bond . he have not repent of his marriage ; it have really bring more of aim into his life , new object to exert his will upon ; and he have not repent of his choice . his taste be fastidious , and Gwendolen satisfy it : he would not have like a wife who have not receive some elevation of rank from he ; nor one who do not command admiration by her mien and beauty ; nor one whose nail be not of the right shape ; nor one the lobe of whose ear be at all too large and red ; nor one who , even if her nail and ear be right , be at the same time a ninny , unable to make spirited answer . these requirement may not seem too exact to refined contemporary whose own ability to fall in love have be hold in suspense for lack of indispensable detail ; but few perhaps may follow he in his contentment that his wife should be in a temper which would dispose she to fly out if she dare , and that she should have be urge into marry he by other feeling than passionate attachment . still , for those who prefer command to love , one do not see why the habit of mind should change precisely at the point of matrimony . 

 Grandcourt do not feel that he have choose the wrong wife ; and have take on himself the part of husband , he be not go in any way to be fool , or allow himself to be see in a light that could be regard as pitiable . this be his state of mind not jealousy ; still , his behavior in some respect be as like jealousy as yellow be to yellow , which color we know may be the effect of very different cause . 

 he have come up to town early than usual because he wish to be on the spot for legal consultation as to the arrangement of his will , the transference of mortgage , and that transaction with his uncle about the succession to Diplow , which the bait of ready money , adroitly dangle without importunity , have finally win he to agree upon . but another acceptable accompaniment of his being in town be the presentation of himself with the beautiful bride whom he have choose to marry in spite of what other people might have expect of he . it be true that Grandcourt go about with the sense that he do not care a languid curse for any one 's admiration : but this state of not - care , just as much as desire , require its related object namely , a world of admire or envy spectator : for if you be fond of look stonily at smile person the person must be and they must smile a rudimentary truth which be surely forget by those who complain of mankind as generally contemptible , since any other aspect of the race must disappoint the voracity of their contempt . Grandcourt , in town for the first time with his wife , have his non - caring abstinence from curse enlarge and diversify by splendid reception , by conspicuous ride and drive , by presentation of himself with she on all distinguished occasion . he wish she to be seek after ; he like that " fellow " should be eager to talk with she and escort she within his observation ; there be even a kind of lofty coquetry on her part that he would not have object to . but what he do not like be her way in relation to Deronda . 

 after the musical party at Lady Mallinger 's , when Grandcourt have observe the dialogue on the settee as keenly as Hans have do , it be characteristic of he that he name Deronda for invitation along with the Mallinger 's , tenaciously avoid the possible suggestion to anybody concerned that Deronda 's presence or absence could be of the least importance to he ; and he make no direct observation to Gwendolen on her behavior that evening , lest the expression of his disgust should be a little too strong to satisfy his own pride . but a few day afterward he remark , without be careful of the a propos 

 " nothing make a woman more of a gawky than look out after people and show temper in public . a woman ought to have good manner . else it be intolerable to appear with she . " 

 Gwendolen make the expect application , and be not without alarm at the notion of be a gawky . for she , too , with her melancholy distaste for thing , prefer that her distaste should include admirer . but the sense of overhang rebuke only intensify the strain of expectation toward any meeting with Deronda . the novelty and excitement of her town life be like the hurry and constant change of foreign travel ; whatever might be the inward despondency , there be a programme to be fulfil , not without gratification to many - sided self . but , as always happen with a deep interest , the comparatively rare occasion on which she could exchange any word with Deronda have a diffusive effect in her consciousness , magnify their communication with each other , and therefore enlarge the place she imagine it to have in his mind . how could Deronda help this ? he certainly do not avoid she ; rather he wish to convince she by every delicate indirect mean that her confidence in he have not be indiscreet since it have not lower his respect . moreover he like be near she how could it be otherwise ? she be something more than a problem : she be a lovely woman , for the turn of whose mind and fate he have a care which , however futile it might be , keep solicit he as a responsibility , perhaps all the more that , when he dare to think of his own future , he see it lie far away from this splendid sad - hearted creature , who , because he have once be impel to arrest her attention momentarily , as he might have seize her arm with warning to hinder she from step where there be danger , have turn to he with a beseech persistent need . 

 one instance in which Grandcourt stimulate a feeling in Gwendolen that he would have like to suppress without seem to care about it , have relation to Mirah . Gwendolen 's inclination linger over the project of the singing lesson as a sort of obedience to Deronda 's advice , but day follow day with that want of perceive leisure which belong to life where there be no work to mark off interval ; and the continual liability to Grandcourt 's presence and surveillance seem to flatten every effort to the level of the boredom which his manner express ; his negative mind be as diffusive as fog , cling to all object , and spoil all contact .

" if I be to have misery anyhow , " be the bitter refrain of her rebellious dream , " I have well have the misery that I can keep to myself .  and always among the image that drive she back to submission be Deronda . the idea of herself separate from her husband , give Deronda a change , perturb , painful place in her consciousness : instinctively she feel that the separation would be from he too , and in the prospective vision of herself as a solitary , dubiously - regard woman , she feel some tingling bashfulness at the remembrance of her behavior towards he . the association of Deronda with a dubious position for herself be intolerable . and what would he say if he know everything ? probably that she ought to bear what she have bring on herself , unless she be sure that she could make herself a well woman by take any other course . and what sort of woman be she to be solitary , sicken of life , look at with a suspicious kind of pity ? even if she could dream of success in get that dreary freedom . Mrs. Grandcourt " run away " would be a more pitiable creature than Gwendolen Harleth condemn to teach the bishop 's daughter , and to be inspect by Mrs. Mompert .

 Day after day the same pattern of thinking be repeat . there come nothing to change the situation no new element in the sketch only a recurrence which engrave it . the May week go on into June , and still Mrs. Grandcourt be outwardly in the same place , present herself as she be expect to do in the accustomed scene , with the accustomed grace , beauty , and costume ; from church at one end of the week , through all the scale of desirable reception , to opera at the other . Church be not markedly distinguished in her mind from the other form of self - presentation , for marriage have include no instruction that enable she to connect liturgy and sermon with any large order of the world than that of unexplained and perhaps inexplicable social fashion . while a laudable zeal be labor to carry the light of spiritual law up the alley where law be chiefly know as the policeman , the brilliant Mrs. Grandcourt , condescend a little to a fashionable rector and conscious of a feminine advantage over a learn dean , be , so far as pastoral care and religious fellowship be concern , in as complete a solitude as a man in a lighthouse . 

 can we wonder at the practical submission which hide her constructive rebellion ? the combination be common enough , as we know from the number of person who make we aware of it in their own case by a clamorous unwearied statement of the reason against their submit to a situation which , on inquiry , we discover to be the least disagreeable within their reach . Poor Gwendolen have both too much and too little mental power and dignity to make herself exceptional . no wonder that Deronda now mark some hardening in a look and manner which be school daily to the suppression of feeling . 

 for example . one morning , rid in Rotten Row with Grandcourt by her side , she see stand against the railing at the turn , just face they , a dark - eyed lady with a little girl and a blonde boy , whom she at once recognize as the being in all the world the most painful for she to behold . she and Grandcourt have just slacken their pace to a walk ; he be on the outer side be the nearer to the unwelcome vision , and Gwendolen have not presence of mind to do anything but glance away from the dark eye that meet hers piercingly toward Grandcourt , who wheel past the group with an unmoved face , give no sign of recognition .

Gwendolen be not a woman who could easily think of her own death as a near reality , or front for herself the dark entrance on the untried and invisible . it seem more possible that Grandcourt should die : and yet not likely . the power of tyranny in he seem a power of live in the presence of any wish that he should die . the thought that his death be the only possible deliverance for she be one with the thought that deliverance would never come the double deliverance from the injury with which other being might reproach she and from the yoke she have bring on her own neck . no ! she foresee he always live , and her own life dominate by he ; the " always " of her young experience not stretch beyond the few immediate year that seem immeasurably long with her passionate weariness . the thought of his dying would not subsist : it turn as with a dream - change into the terror that she should die with his throttling finger on her neck avenge that think . fantasy move within she like ghost , make no break in her more acknowledge consciousness and find no obstruction in it : dark ray do their work invisibly in the broad light .

 this be the letter which Sir Hugo put into Deronda 's hand : 

 to MY SON , DANIEL DERONDA . 

 my good friend and your , Sir Hugo Mallinger , will have tell you that I wish to see you . my health be shake , and I desire there should be no time lose before I deliver to you what I have long withhold . let nothing hinder you from be at the Albergo dell ' Italia in Genoa by the fourteenth of this month . wait for I there . I be uncertain when I shall be able to make the journey from Spezia , where I shall be stay . that will depend on several thing . wait for I the Princess Halm - Eberstein . bring with you the diamond ring that Sir Hugo give you . I shall like to see it again . your unknown mother , 

 LEONORA halm - eberstein . 

 this letter with its colorless wording give Deronda no clue to what be in reserve for he ; but he could not do otherwise than accept Sir Hugo 's reticence , which seem to imply some pledge not to anticipate the mother 's disclosure ; and the discovery that his life - long conjecture have be mistake check far surmise . Deronda could not hinder his imagination from take a quick flight over what seem possibility , but he refuse to contemplate any of they as more likely than another , lest he should be nurse it into a dominant desire or repugnance , instead of simply prepare himself with resolve to meet the fact bravely , whatever it might turn out to be . 

 in this state of mind he could not have communicate to any one the reason for the absence which in some quarter he be oblige to mention beforehand , least of all to Mordecai , whom it would affect as powerfully as it do himself , only in rather a different way . if he be to say , " I be go to learn the truth about my birth , " Mordecai 's hope would gather what might prove a painful , dangerous excitement . to exclude supposition , he speak of his journey as be undertake by Sir Hugo 's wish , and throw as much indifference as he could into his manner of announce it , say he be uncertain of its duration , but it would perhaps be very short . 

 " I will ask to have the child Jacob to stay with I , " say Mordecai , comfort himself in this way , after the first mournful glance . 

 " I will drive round and ask Mrs. Cohen to let he come , " say Mirah . 

 " the grandmother will deny you nothing , " say Deronda . " I be glad you be a little wrong as well as I , " he add , smile at Mordecai . " you think that old Mrs. Cohen would not bear to see Mirah . " 

 " I undervalue her heart , " say Mordecai . " she be capable of rejoice that another 's plant bloom though her own be wither . " 

 " oh , they be dear good people ; I feel as if we all belong to each other , " say Mirah , with a tinge of merriment in her smile . 

 " what should you have feel if that Ezra have be your brother ? " say Deronda , mischievously a little provoke that she have take kindly at once to people who have cause he so much prospective annoyance on her account . 

 Mirah look at he with a slight surprise for a moment , and then say , " he be not a bad man I think he would never forsake any one . " but when she utter the word she blush deeply , and glance timidly at Mordecai , turn away to some occupation . her father be in her mind , and this be a subject on which she and her brother have a painful mutual consciousness . " if he should come and find we ! " be a thought which to Mirah sometimes make the street daylight as shadowy as a haunted forest where each turn screen for she an imaginary apparition . 

 Deronda feel what be her involuntary allusion , and understand the blush . how could he be slow to understand feeling which now seem near than ever to his own ? for the word of his mother 's letter imply that his filial relation be not to be free from painful condition ; indeed , singularly enough that letter which have bring his mother nearer as a living reality have throw she into more remoteness for his affection . the tender yearning after a be whose life might have be the bad for not have his care and love , the image of a mother who have not have all her due , whether of reverence or compassion , have long be secretly present with he in his observation of all the woman he have come near . but it seem now that this picturing of his mother might fit the fact no well than his former conception about Sir Hugo . he wonder to find that when this mother 's very hand - writing have come to he with word hold her actual feeling , his affection have suddenly shrink into a state of comparative neutrality toward she . a veiled figure with enigmatic speech have thrust away that image which , in spite of uncertainty , his clinging thought have gradually model and make the possessor of his tenderness and duteous longing . when he set off to Genoa , the interest really uppermost in his mind have hardly so much relation to his mother as to Mordecai and Mirah . 

 " God bless you , Dan ! " Sir Hugo have say , when they shake hand . " whatever else change for you , it can not   change my be the old friend you have know , and the one who have all along feel the most for you . I could not   have love you well if you have be my own - only I should have be well pleased with thinking of you always as the future master of the Abbey instead of my fine nephew ; and then you would have see it necessary for you to take a political line . however thing must be as they may . " it be a defensive movement of the baronet 's to mingle purposeless remark with the expression of serious feeling . 

 when Deronda arrive at the Italia in Genoa , no Princess Halm - Eberstein be there ; but on the second day there be a letter for he , say that her arrival might happen within a week , or might be defer a fortnight and more ; she be under circumstance which make it impossible for she to fix her journey more precisely , and she entreat he to wait as patiently as he could . 

 with this indefinite prospect of suspense on matter of supreme moment to he , Deronda set about the difficult task of seek amusement on philosophic ground , as a means of quiet excited feeling and give patience a lift over a weary road . his former visit to the superb city have be only cursory , and leave he much to learn beyond the prescribed round of sight - seeing , by spend the cool hour in observant wander about the street , the quay , and the environ ; and he often take a boat that he might enjoy the magnificent view of the city and harbor from the sea . all sight , all subject , even the expect meeting with his mother , find a central union in Mordecai and Mirah , and the idea immediately associate with they ; and among the thought that most fill his mind while his boat be push about within view of the grand harbor be that of the multitudinous Spanish Jews century ago drive destitute from their spanish home , suffer to land from the crowded ship only for a brief rest on this grand quay of Genoa , overspread it with a pall of famine and plague die mother and die child at their breast father and son a - gaze at each other 's haggardness , like group from a hundred hunger - tower turn out beneath the midday sun . inevitably dreamy construction of a possible ancestry for himself would weave themselves with historic memory which have begin to have a new interest for he on his discovery of Mirah , and now , under the influence of Mordecai , have become irresistibly dominant . he would have seal his mind against such construction if it have be possible , and he have never yet fully admit to himself that he wish the fact to verify Mordecai 's conviction : he inwardly repeat that he have no choice in the matter , and that wish be folly nay , on the question of parentage , wish seem part of that meanness which disown kinship : it be a disowning by anticipation . what he have to do be simply to accept the fact ; and he have really no strong presumption to go upon , now that he be assure of his mistake about Sir Hugo . there have be a resolve concealment which make all inference untrustworthy , and the very name he bear might be a false one . if Mordecai be wrong if he , the so - call Daniel Deronda , be hold by tie entirely aloof from any such course as his friend 's pathetic hope have mark out ? he would not say " I wish " ; but he could not help feel on which side the sacrifice lay . 

 across these two importunate thought , which he resist as much as one can resist anything in that unstrung condition which belong to suspense , there come continually an anxiety which he make no effort to banish dwelling on it rather with a mournfulness , which often seem to we the good atonement we can make to one whose need we have be unable to meet . the anxiety be for Gwendolen . in the wonderful mixture of our nature there be a feeling distinct from that exclusive passionate love of which some man and woman   by no means all   be capable , which yet be not the same with friendship , nor with a merely benevolent regard , whether admire or compassionate : a man , say for it be a man who be here concern hardly represent to himself this shade of feeling toward a woman more nearly than in word , " I should have love she , if " : the " if " cover some prior growth in the inclination , or else some circumstance which have make an inward prohibitory law as a stay against the emotion ready to quiver out of balance . the " if " in Deronda 's case carry reason of both kind ; yet he have never throughout his relation with Gwendolen be free from the nervous consciousness that there be something to guard against not only on her account but on his own some precipitancy in the manifestation of impulsive feeling some ruinous inroad of what be but momentary on the permanent choose treasure of the heart some spoil of her trust , which work upon he now as if it have be the retreat cry of a creature snatch and carry out of his reach by swift horseman or swifter wave , while his own strength be only a strong sense of weakness . how could his feeling for Gwendolen ever be exactly like his feeling for other woman , even when there be one by whose side he desire to stand apart from they ? strangely the figure enter into the picture of his present and future ; strangely   and now it seem sadly   their two lot have come in contact , her narrowly personal , his charge with far - reach sensibility , perhaps with durable purpose , which be hardly more present to she than the reason why man migrate be present to the bird that come as usual for the crumb and find they no more . not that Deronda be too ready to imagine himself of supreme importance to a woman ; but her word of insistance that he must " remain near she must not forsake she " continually recur to he with the clearness and importunity of imagine sound , such as Dante have say pierce we like arrow whose point carry the sharpness of pity

 Day after day pass , and the very air of Italy seem to carry the consciousness that war have be declare against Austria , and every day be a hurrying march of crowded Time toward the world - change battle of Sadowa . meanwhile , in Genoa , the noon be get hot , the converging outer road get deep with white dust , the oleander in the tub along the wayside garden look more and more like fatigued holiday - maker , and the sweet evening change her office scatter abroad those whom the midday have send under shelter , and sow all path with happy social sound , little tinkling of mule - bell and whirring of thrumbed string , light footstep and voice , if not leisurely , then with the hurry of pleasure in they ; while the encircle height , crown with fort , skirt with fine dwelling and garden , seem also to come forth and gaze in fullness of beauty after their long siesta , till all strong color melt in the stream of moonlight which make the Streets a new spectacle with shadow , both still and move , on cathedral step and against the faáade of massive palace ; and then slowly with the descend moon all sink in deep night and silence , and nothing shine but the port light of the great Lanterna in the blackness below , and the glimmer star in the blackness above . Deronda , in his suspense , watch this revolving of the day as he might have watch a wonderful clock where the striking of the hour be make solemn with antique figure advance and retreat in monitory procession , while he still keep his ear open for another kind of signal which would have its solemnity too : he be begin to sicken of occupation , and find himself contemplate all activity with the aloofness of a prisoner await ransom . in his letter to Mordecai and Hans , he have avoid writing about himself , but he be really get into that state of mind to which all subject become personal ; and the few book he have bring to make he a refuge in study be become unreadable , because the point of view that life would make for he be in that agitate moment of uncertainty which be close upon decision . 

 many night be watch through by he in gaze from the open window of his room on the double , faintly pierce darkness of the sea and the heaven ; often in struggle under the oppressive skepticism which represent his particular lot , with all the importance he be allow Mordecai to give it , as of no more lasting effect than a dream a set of change which make passion to he , but beyond his consciousness be no more than an imperceptible difference of mass and shadow ; sometimes with a reaction of emotive force which give even to sustained disappointment , even to the fulfilled demand of sacrifice , the nature of a satisfied energy , and spread over his young future , whatever it might be , the attraction of devoted service ; sometimes with a sweet irresistible hopefulness that the very good of human possibility might befall he the blending of a complete personal love in one current with a large duty ; and sometimes again in a mood of rebellion   what human creature escape it ?   against thing in general because they be thus and not otherwise , a mood in which Gwendolen and her equivocal fate move as busy image of what be amiss in the world along with the concealment which he have feel as a hardship in his own life , and which be act in he now under the form of an afflict doubtfulness about the mother who have announce herself coldly and still keep away . 

 but at last she be come . one morning in his third week of wait there be a new kind of knock at the door . a servant in Chasseurs livery enter and deliver in French the verbal message that , the Princess Halm - Eberstein have arrive , that she be go to rest during the day , but would be oblige if Monsieur would dine early , so as to be at liberty at seven , when she would be able to receive he .

 when Deronda present himself at the door of his mother 's apartment in the Italia he feel some revival of his boyhood with its premature agitation . the two servant in the antechamber look at he markedly , a little surprised that the doctor their lady have come to consult be this strike young gentleman whose appearance give even the severe line of an evening dress the credit of adornment . but Deronda could notice nothing until , the second door be open , he find himself in the presence of a figure which at the other end of the large room stand await his approach . 

 she be cover , except as to her face and part of her arm , with black lace hang loosely from the summit of her whiten hair to the long train stretch from her tall figure . her arm , naked to the elbow , except for some rich bracelet , be fold before she , and the fine poise of her head make it look handsomer than it really be . but Deronda feel no interval of observation before he be close in front of she , hold the hand she have put out and then raise it to his lip . she still keep she hand in his and look at he examiningly ; while his chief consciousness be that her eye be pierce and her face so mobile that the next moment she might look like a different person . for even while she be examine he there be a play of the brow and nostril which make a tacit language . Deronda dare no movement , not able to conceive what sort of manifestation her feeling demand ; but he feel himself change color like a girl , and yet wonder at his own lack of emotion ; he have live through so many ideal meeting with his mother , and they have seem more real than this ! he could not even conjecture in what language she would speak to he . he imagine it would not be English . suddenly , she let fall his hand , and place both her on his shoulder , while her face give out a flash of admiration in which every worn line disappear and seem to leave a restore youth . 

 " you be a beautiful creature ! " she say , in a low melodious voice , with syllable which have what might be call a foreign but agreeable outline . " I know you would be . " then she kiss he on each cheek , and he return the kiss . but it be something like a greeting between royalty . 

 she pause a moment while the line be come back into her face , and then say in a cold tone , " I be your mother . but you can have no love for I . " 

 " I have think of you more than of any other be in the world , " say 
 Deronda , his voice tremble nervously . 
 " I be not like what you think I be , " say the mother decisively , withdraw her hand from his shoulder , and fold her arm as before , look at he as if she invite he to observe she . he have often picture her face in his imagination as one which have a likeness to his own : he see some of the likeness now , but amidst more striking difference . she be a remarkable look be . what be it that give her son a painful sense of aloofness ? her wear beauty have a strangeness in it as if she be not quite a human mother , but a Melusina , who have tie with some world which be independent of our . 

 " I use to think that you might be suffer , " say Deronda , anxious above all not to wound she . " I use to wish that I could be a comfort to you . "

" she point to a seat near she ; and then discern some distress in Deronda 's face , she add , more gently , " I be not suffer at this moment . I be at ease now . I be able to talk . " 

 Deronda seat himself and wait for she to speak again . it seem as if he be in the presence of a mysterious fate rather than of the long - for mother . he be begin to watch she with wonder , from the spiritual distance to which she have throw he .

I do not think I deprive you of anything worth have . " 

 " you can not wish I to believe that your affection would not have be worth have , " say Deronda , find that she pause as if she expect he to make some answer . 

 " I do not   mean to speak ill of myself , " say the princess , with proud impetuosity , " but I have not much affection to give you . I do not want affection . I have be stifle with it . I want to live out the life that be in I , and not to be hamper with other life . you wonder what I be . I be no princess then . " she rise with a sudden movement , and stand as she have do before . Deronda immediately rise too ; he feel breathless . 

 " no princess in this tame life that I live in now . I be a great singer , and I act as well as I sing . all the rest be poor beside I . man follow I from one country to another . I be live a myriad life in one . I do not want a child . " 

 there be a passionate self - defence in her tone . she have cast all precedent out of her mind . Precedent have no excuse for she and she could only seek a justification in the intense word she could find for her experience . she seem to fle out the last word against some possible reproach in the mind of her son , who have to stand and hear they clutch his coat - collar as if he be keep himself above water by it , and feel his blood in the sort of commotion that might have be excited if he have see she go through some strange rite of a religion which give a sacredness to crime . what else have she to tell he ? she go on with the same intensity and a sort of pale illumination in her face . 

 " I do not want to marry . I be force into marry your father force , I mean , by my father 's wish and command ; and besides , it be my good way of get some freedom . I could rule my husband , but not my father . I have a right to be free . I have a right to seek my freedom from a bondage that I hat . " 

 she seat herself again , while there be that subtle movement in her eye and close lip which be like the suppress continuation of speech . Deronda continue stand , and after a moment or two she look up at he with a less defiant pleading as she say

 but the mother be equally shake by an anger differently mixed , and her frame be less equal to any repression . the shake with she be visibly physical , and her eye look the large for her pallid excitement as she say violently

 " you do not know what you secure I . how could you choose my birthright for I ? " say Deronda , throw himself sideways into his chair again , almost unconsciously , and lean his arm over the back , while he look away from his mother .

 " I choose for you what I would have choose for myself . how could I know that you would have the spirit of my father in you ? how could I know that you would love what I hat ? if you really love to be a Jew . " the last word have such bitterness in they that any one overhearing might have suppose some hatred have arise between the mother and son . 

 but Deronda have recover his fuller self . he be recall his sensibility to what life have be and actually be for she whose good year be go , and who with the sign of suffer in her frame be now exert herself to tell he of a past which be not his alone but also her . his habitual shame at the acceptance of event as if they be his only , help he even here . as he look at his mother silently after her last word , his face regain some of its penetrative calm ; yet it seem to have a strangely agitate influence over she : her eye be fix on he with a sort of fascination , but not with any repose of maternal delight .

 " oh the reason of our action ! " say the Princess , with a ring of something like sarcastic scorn . " when you be as old as I be , it will not seem so simple a question ' why do you do this ? ' People talk of their motif in a cut and dry way . every woman be suppose to have the same set of motif , or else to be a monster . I be not a monster , but I have not feel exactly what other woman feel or say they feel , for fear of be think unlike other . when you reproach I in your heart for send you away from I , you mean that I ought to say I feel about you as other woman say they feel about their child .  the varied transition of tone with which this speech be deliver be as perfect as the most accomplished actress could have make they . the speech be in fact a piece of what may be call sincere acting ; this woman 's nature be one in which all feeling and all the more when it be tragic as well as real immediately become matter of conscious representation : experience immediately pass into drama , and she act her own emotion . in a minor degree this be nothing uncommon , but in the Princess the acting have a rare perfection of physiognomy , voice , and gesture . it would not be true to say that she feel less because of this double consciousness : she feel that is , her mind go through all the more , but with a difference ; each nucleus of pain or pleasure have a deep atmosphere of the excitement or spiritual intoxication which at once exalt and deaden . but Deronda make no reflection of this kind . all his thought hang on the purport of what his mother be say ; her tone and her wonderful face enter into his agitation without be note . what he long for with an awed desire be to know as much as she would tell he of the strange mental conflict under which it seem he have be bring into the world ; what his compassionate nature make the control idea within he be the suffering and the confession that breathe through her later word , and these forbid any further question , when she pause and remain silent , with her brow knit , her head turn a little away from he , and her large eye fix as if on something incorporeal . he must wait for she to speak again . she do so with strange abruptness , turn her eye upon he suddenly , and say more quickly 

 " Sir Hugo have write much about you . he tell I you have a wonderful mind you comprehend everything you be wise than he be with all his sixty year . you say you be glad to know that you be bear a Jew . I be not go to tell you that I have change my mind about that . your feeling be against mine . you do not   thank I for what I do . shall you comprehend your mother , or only blame she ? "

 " then you have become unlike your grandfather in that . " say the mother , " though you be a young copy of he in your face . he never comprehend I , or if he do , he only think of fetter I into obedience . I be to be what he call ' the jewish woman ' under pain of his curse . I be to feel everything I do not feel , and believe everything I do not believe . I be to feel awe for the bit of parchment in the mezuza over the door ; to dread lest a bit of butter should touch a bit of meat ; to think it beautiful that man should bind the tephillin on they , and woman not , to adore the wisdom of such law , however silly they might seem to I . I be to love the long prayer in the ugly synagogue , and the howling , and the gabbling , and the dreadful fast , and the tiresome feast , and my father 's endless discoursing about our people , which be a thunder without mean in my ear . I hat live under the shadow of my father 's strictness . teaching , teach for everlaste ' this you must be , ' ' that you must not be ' press on I like a frame that get tight and tight as I grow . " here her tone change to one of a more bitter incisiveness " you be glad to have be bear a Jew . you say so . that be because you have not be bring up as a Jew . that separateness seem sweet to you because I save you from it . "

I be still the same Leonora " she point with her forefinger to her breast " here within I be the same desire , the same will , the same choice , but " she spread out her hand , palm upward , on each side of she , as she pause with a bitter compression of her lip , then let her voice fall into muffled , rapid utterance " event come upon we like evil enchantment : and thought , feeling , apparition in the darkness be event be they not ? I do not   consent . we only consent to what we love . " I gather that my grandfather oppose your bent to be an artist . though my own experience have be quite different , I enter into the painfulness of your struggle . I can imagine the hardship of an enforce renunciation . "

" you be not a woman . you may try but you can never imagine what it be to have a man 's force of genius in you , and yet to suffer the slavery of be a girl . to have a pattern cut out ' this be the jewish woman ; this be what you must be ; this be what you be want for ; a woman 's heart must be of such a size and no large , else it must be press small , like chinese foot ; her happiness be to be make as cake be , by a fix receipt . I know I could rule he ; and I make he secretly promise I , before I marry he , that he would put no hindrance in the way of my be an artist . my father be on his deathbed when we be marry : from the first he have fix his mind on my marry my cousin Ephraim . and when a woman 's will be as strong as the man 's who want to govern she , half her strength must be concealment . I mean to have my will in the end , but I could only have it by seem to obey . I have an awe of my father always I have have an awe of he : it be impossible to help it . I hat to feel awed I wish I could have defy he openly ; but I never could . it be what I could not imagine : I could not act it to myself that I should begin to defy my father openly and succeed . and I never would risk failure .  " where be my grandfather 's home ? " 

 " here in Genoa , where I be marry ; and his family have live here generation ago . but my father have be in various country . "

 " my mother be English a Jewess of portuguese descent . my father marry she in England . certain circumstance of that marriage make all the difference in my life : through that marriage my father thwart his own plan . my mother 's sister be a singer , and afterward she marry the english partner of a merchant 's house here in Genoa , and they come and live here eleven year . my mother die when I be eight year old , and my father allow I to be continually with my Aunt Leonora and be teach under her eye , as if he have not mind the danger of she encourage my wish to be a singer , as she have be . but this be it I see it again and again in my father : he do not guard against consequence , because he feel sure he could hinder they if he like . before my aunt leave Genoa , I have have enough teaching to bring out the bear singer and actress within I : my father do not know everything that be do ; but he know that I be teach music and singing he know my inclination . that be nothing to he : he mean that I should obey his will . and he be resolve that I should marry my cousin Ephraim , the only one leave of my father 's family that he know . I want not to marry . I think of all plan to resist it , but at last I find that I could rule my cousin , and I consent . my father die three week after we be marry , and then I have my way ! " she utter these word almost exultantly ; but after a little pause her face change , and she say in a biting tone , " it have not last , though . my father be get his way now . "

 " it be well so . we must part again soon and you owe I no duty . I do not wish you to be bear . I say you should not know you be a Jew . "

" it would always have be well that I should have know the truth . I have always be rebel against the secrecy that look like shame . it be no shame to have jewish parent the shame be to disown it . " say the Princess , rise quickly and walk to the other side of the room , where she turn round and slowly approach he , as he , too , stand up . then she begin to speak again in a more veiled voice . " I can not   explain ; I can only say what be . " it be illness , I do not   doubt that it have be gather illness my mind have go back : more than a year ago it begin . you see my gray hair , my worn look : it have all come fast . sometimes I be in an agony of pain I dare say I shall be to - night . then it be as if all the life I have choose to live , all thought , all will , forsook I and leave I alone in spot of memory , and I can not   get away : my pain seem to keep I there . my childhood my girlhood the day of my marriage the day of my father 's death there seem to be nothing since . then a great horror come over I : what do I know of life or death ? and what my father call ' right ' may be a power that be lay hold of I that be clutch I now . well , I will satisfy he .  " will you not spare yourself this evening ? let we leave the rest till to - morrow . "

" I will confess it all , now that I have come up to it . often when I be at ease it all fade away ; my whole self come quite back ; but I know it will sink away again , and the other will come the poor , solitary , forsake remain of self , that can resist nothing . it be my nature to resist , and say , ' I have a right to resist . Joseph Kalonymos reproach I : he say you have be turn into a proud Englishman , who resent be touch by a Jew . I wish you have ! " she end , with a new marvelous alternation . it be as if her mind be break into several , one jar the other into impulsive action . 

 " who be Joseph Kalonymos ? " say Deronda , with a dart recollection of that Jew who touch his arm in the Frankfort synagogue . 

 " ah ! some vengeance send he back from the East , that he might see you and come to reproach I . he be my father 's friend . he know of your birth : he know of my husband 's death , and once , twenty year ago , after he have be away in the Levant , he come to see I and inquire about you . I tell he that you be dead : I mean you to be dead to all the world of my childhood . if I have say that your be live , he would have interfere with my plan : he would have take on he to represent my father , and have try to make I recall what I have do . what could I do but say you be dead ? the act be do . if I have tell he of it there would have be trouble and scandal and all to conquer I , who would not have be conquer . I be strong then , and I would have have my will , though there might have be a hard fight against I . I take the way to have it without any fight . I feel then that I be not really deceive : it would have come to the same in the end ; or if not to the same , to something bad . he believe I and beg that I would give up to he the chest that my father have charge I and my husband to deliver to our eld son . I know what be in the chest thing that have be din in my ear since I have have any understand thing that be thrust on my mind that I might feel they like a wall around my life my life that be grow like a tree . once , after my husband die , I be go to burn the chest . but it be difficult to burn ; and burn a chest and paper look like a shameful act . I have commit no shameful act except what Jews would call shameful . I have keep the chest , and I give it to Joseph Kalonymos . he go away mournful , and say , ' if you marry again , and if another grandson be bear to he who be depart , I will deliver up the chest to he . ' I bow in silence . I mean not to marry again no more than I mean to be the shattered woman that I be now . " 

 she cease speak , and her head sink back while she look vaguely before she . her thought be travel through the year , and when she begin to speak again her voice have lose its argumentative spirit , and have fall into a veiled tone of distress . 

 " but month ago this Kalonymos see you in the synagogue at Frankfort . he see you enter the hotel , and he go to ask your name . there be nobody else in the world to whom the name would have tell anything about I . " 

 " then it be not my real name ? " say Deronda , with a dislike even to this trifle part of the disguise which have be throw round he . 

 " oh , as real as another , " say his mother , indifferently . " the Jews have always be change their name . my father 's family have keep the name of Charisi : my husband be a Charisi . when I come out as a singer , we make it alcharisi . but there have be a branch of the family my father have lose sight of who call themselves Deronda , and when I want a name for you , and Sir Hugo say , ' let it be a foreign name , ' I think of Deronda . but Joseph Kalonymos have hear my father speak of the Deronda branch , and the name confirm his suspicion . he begin to suspect what have be do . it be as if everything have be whisper to he in the air . he find out where I be . he take a journey into Russia to see I ; he find I weak and shatter . he have come back again , with his white hair , and with rage in his soul against I . he say I be go down to the grave clothe in falsehood and robbery falsehood to my father and robbery of my own child . he accuse I of have keep the knowledge of your birth from you , and have bring you up as if you have be the son of an english gentleman . well , it be true ; and twenty year before I would have maintain that I have a right to do it . but I can maintain nothing now . no faith be strong within I . my father may have God on his side . this man 's word be like lion 's tooth upon I . my father 's threat eat into I with my pain . if I tell everything if I deliver up everything what else can be demand of I ? I can not make myself love the people I have never love be it not enough that I lose the life I do love ? " 

 she have lean forward a little in her low - toned pleading , that seem like a smother cry : her arm and hand be stretch out at full length , as if strain in beseech , Deronda 's soul be absorb in the anguish of compassion . he could not mind now that he have be repulse before . his pity make a flood of forgiveness within he . his single impulse be to kneel by she and take her hand gently , between his palm , while he say in that exquisite voice of soothe which express oneness with the sufferer 

 " Mother , take comfort ! " 

 she do not seem inclined to repulse he now , but look down at he and let he take both her hand to fold between his . gradually tear gather , but she press her handkerchief against her eye and then lean her cheek against his brow , as if she wish that they should not look at each other . 

 " be it not possible that I could be near you often and comfort you ? " say Deronda . he be under that stress of pity that propel we on sacrifice . 

 " no , not possible , " she answer , lift up her head again and withdraw her hand as if she wish he to move away . " I have a husband and five child . none of they know of your existence . " 

 Deronda feel painfully silence . he rise and stand at a little distance . 

 " you wonder why I marry , " she go on presently , under the influence of a newly - recur thought . " I mean never to marry again . I mean to be free and to live for my art . I have part with you . I have no bond . for nine year I be a queen . I enjoy the life I have long for . but something befall I . it be like a fit of forgetfulness . I begin to sing out of tune . they tell I of it . another woman be thrust herself in my place . I could not endure the prospect of failure and decline . it be horrible to I . " she start up again , with a shudder , and lift screen hand like one who dread missile . " it drive I to marry . I make believe that I prefer be the wife of a russian noble to be the great lyric actress of Europe ; I make believe I act that part . it be because I feel my greatness sink away from I , as I feel my life sink now . I would not wait till man say , ' she have well go . ' " 

 she sink into her seat again , and look at the evening sky as she go on : " I repent . it be a resolve take in desperation . that sing out of tune be only like a fit of illness ; it go away . I repent ; but it be too late . I could not go back . all thing hinder , I all thing . " 

 a new haggardness have come in her face , but her son refrain from again urge she to leave further speech till the morrow : there be evidently some mental relief for she in an outpouring such as she could never have allow herself before . he stand still while she maintain silence long than she know , and the light be perceptibly fade . at last she turn to he and say 

 " I can bear no more now . " she put out her hand , but then quickly withdraw it say , " stay . how do I know that I can see you again ? I can not bear to be see when I be in pain . " 

 she draw forth a pocket - book , and take out a letter say , " this be address to the banking - house in Mainz , where you be to go for your grandfather 's chest . it be a letter write by Joseph Kalonymos : if he be not there himself , this order of his will be obey . " 

 when Deronda have take the letter , she say , with effort but more gently than before , " kneel again , and let I kiss you . " 

 he obey , and hold his head between her hand , she kiss he solemnly on the brow . " you see , I have no life leave to love you with , " she say , in a low murmur . " but there be more fortune for you . Sir Hugo be to keep it in reserve . I give you all your father 's fortune . they can never accuse I of robbery there . " 

 " if you have need anything I would have work for you , " say Deronda , conscious of disappointed yearn a shutting out forever from long early vista of affectionate imagination . 

 " I need nothing that the skill of man can give I , " say his mother , still hold his head , and peruse his feature . " but perhaps now I have satisfy my father 's will , your face will come instead of his your young , love face . " 

 " but you will see I again ? " say Deronda , anxiously . 

 " yes perhaps . wait , wait . leave I now . " 

 CHAPTER LII .

 among Deronda 's letter the next morning be one from Hans Meyrick of four quarto page , in the small , beautiful handwriting which run in the Meyrick family . 

 MY DEAR DERONDA , in return for your sketch of italian movement and your view of the world 's affair generally , I may say that here at home the most judicious opinion go as to the effect of present cause be that " time will show . " as to the present cause of past effect , it be now see that the late swindle telegram account for the last year 's cattle plague which be a refutation of philosophy falsely so call , and justify the compensation to the farmer . my own idea that a murrain will shortly break out in the commercial class , and that the cause will subsequently disclose itself in the ready sale of all reject picture , have be call an unsound use of analogy ; but there be mind that will not hesitate to rob even the neglect painter of his solace . to my feeling there be great beauty in the conception that some bad judge might give a high price for my Berenice series , and that the man in the city would have already be punish for my ill - merit luck . 

 meanwhile I be console myself for your absence by find my advantage in it shin like Hesperus when Hyperion have depart ; sit with our Hebrew prophet , and make a study of his head , in the hour when he use to be occupy with you get credit with he as a learn young Gentile , who would have be a Jew if he could and agree with he in the general principle , that whatever be good be for that reason jewish . I never hold it my forte to be a severe reasoner , but I can see that if whatever be good be A , and b happen to be good , b must be A , however little you might have expect it beforehand . on that principle I could see the force of a pamphlet I once read to prove that all good art be protestant . however , our prophet be an uncommonly interesting sitter a well model than Rembrandt have for his Rabbi and I never come away from he without a new discovery . for one thing , it be a constant wonder to I that , with all his fiery feeling for his race and their tradition , he be no straight - laced Jew , spit after the word Christian , and enjoy the prospect that the Gentile mouth will water in vain for a slice of the roasted Leviathan , while Israel will be send up plate for more , ad libitum ,   you perceive that my study have teach I what to expect from the orthodox Jew .   I confess that I have always hold lightly by your account of Mordecai , as apologetic , and merely part of your disposition to make an antedeluvian point of view lest you should do injustice to the megatherium . but now I have give ear to he in his proper person , I find he really a sort of philosophical - allegorical - mystical believer , and yet with a sharp dialectic point , so that any argumentative rattler of pea in a bladder might soon be prick in silence by he . the mixture may be one of the jewish prerogative , for what I know . in fact , his mind seem so broad that I find my own correct opinion lie in it quite commodiously , and how they be to be bring into agreement with the vast remainder be his affair , not mine . I leave it to he to settle our basis , never yet have see a basis which be not a world - support elephant , more or less powerful and expensive to keep . my mean will not allow I to keep a private elephant . I go into mystery instead , as cheap and more lasting a sort of gas which be likely to be continually supply by the decomposition of the elephant . and if I like the look of an opinion , I treat it civilly , without suspicious inquiry . I have quite a friendly feeling toward Mordecai 's notion that a whole Christian be three - fourth a Jew , and that from the alexandrian time downward the most comprehensive mind have be jewish ; for I think of point out to Mirah that , arabic and other incident of life apart , there be really little difference between I and Maimonides . but I have lately be find out that it be your shallow lover who can not   help make a declaration . if Mirah 's way be less distract , and it be less of a heaven to be in her presence and watch she , I must long ago have fling myself at her foot , and request she to tell I , with less indirectness , whether she wish I to blow my brain out . I have a knack of hoping , which be as good as an estate in reversion , if one can keep from the temptation of turn it into certainty , which may spoil all . my Hope wander among the orchard blossom , feel the warm snow fall on it through the sunshine , and be in doubt of nothing ; but , catch sight of Certainty in the distance , see an ugly Janus - face deity , with a dubious wink on the hither side of he , and turn quickly away . but you , with your supreme reasonableness , and self - nullification , and preparation for the bad you know nothing about Hope , that immortal , delicious maiden forever court forever propitious , whom fool have call deceitful , as if it be Hope that carry the cup of disappointment , whereas it be her deadly enemy , Certainty , whom she only escape by transformation .   you observe my new vein of allegory ?   seriously , however , I must be permit to allege that truth will prevail , that prejudice will melt before it , that diversity , accompany by merit , will make itself feel as fascination , and that no virtuous aspiration will be frustrate all which , if I mistake not , be doctrine of the school , and they imply that the Jewess I prefer will prefer I . any blockhead can cite generality , but the mind - master discern the particular case they represent . 

 I be less convinced that my society make amend to Mordecai for your absence , but another substitute occasionally come in the form of Jacob Cohen . it be worth while to catch our prophet 's expression when he have that remarkable type of young Israel on his knee , and pour forth some semitic inspiration with a sublime look of melancholy patience and devoutness . sometimes it occur to Jacob that Hebrew will be more edify to he if he stop his ear with his palm , and imitate the venerable sound as hear through that muffle medium . when Mordecai gently draw down the little fist and hold they fast , Jacob 's feature all take on an extraordinary activity , very much as if he be walk through a menagerie and try to imitate every animal in turn , succeed well with the owl and the peccary . but I dare say you have see something of this . he treat I with the easy familiarity , and seem in general to look at I as a second - hand christian commodity , likely to come down in price ; remark on my disadvantage with a frankness which seem to imply some thought of future purchase . it be pretty , though , to see the change in he if Mirah happen to come in . he turn child suddenly his age usually strike one as be like the israelitish garment in the desert , perhaps near forty , yet with an air of recent production . but , with Mirah , he remind I of the dog that have be bring up by woman , and remain manageable by they only . still , the dog be fond of Mordecai too , and bring sugar - plum to share with he , fill his own mouth to rather an embarrassing extent , and watch how Mordecai deal with a small supply . judge from this modern Jacob at the age of six , my astonishment be that his race have not buy we all up long ago , and pocket our feebler generation in the form of stock and scrip , as so much slave property . there be one Jewess I should not mind be slave to . but I wish I do not imagine that Mirah get a little sad , and try all the while to hide it . it be natural enough , of course , while she have to watch the slow death of this brother , whom she have take to worship with such look of love devoutness that I be ready to wish myself in his place . 

 for the rest , we be a little merry than usual . Rex Gascoigne you remember a head you admire among my sketch , a fellow with a good upper lip , read law have get some room in town now not far off we , and have have a neat sister   upper lip also good   stay with he the last fortnight . I have introduce they both to my mother and the girl , who have find out from Miss Gascoigne that she be cousin to your Vandyke duchess ! ! ! I put the note of exclamation to mark the surprise that the information at first produce on my feeble understanding . on reflection I discover that there be not the least ground for surprise , unless I have beforehand believe that nobody could be anybody 's cousin without my know it . this sort of surprise , I take it , depend on a liveliness of the spine , with a more or less constant nullity of brain . there be a fellow I use to meet at Rome who be in an effervescence of surprise at contact with the simple information . tell he what you would that you be fond of easy boot he would always say , " no ! be you ? " with the same energy of wonder : the very fellow of whom pastoral Browne write prophetically 

  " a wretch so empty that if e'er there be 
  in nature find the least vacuity 
  ' twill be in he . " 
 I have account for it all he have a lively spine . 

 however , this cousinship with the duchess come out by chance one day that Mirah be with they at home and they be talk about the mallinger . apropo ; I be get so important that I have rival invitation . Gascoigne want I to go down with he to his father 's rectory in August and see the country round there . but I think self - interest well understand will take I to Topping Abbey , for Sir Hugo have invite I , and propose God bless he for his rashness ! that I should make a picture of his three daughter sit on a bank as he say , in the Gainsborough style . he come to my studio the other day and recommend I to apply myself to portrait . of course I know what that mean . " my good fellow , your attempt at the historic and poetic be simply pitiable . your brush be just that of a successful portrait - painter it have a little truth and a great facility in falsehood your idealism will never do for god and goddess and heroic story , but it may fetch a high price as flattery . Fate , my friend , have make you the hinder wheel rota posterior curra , et in axe secundo run behind , because you can not   help it . " what great effort it evidently cost our friend to give we these candid opinion ! I have even know a man to take the trouble to call , in order to tell I that I have irretrievably expose my want of judgment in treat my subject , and that if I have ask he we would have lend I his own judgment . such be my ingratitude and my readiness at composition , that even while he be speak I inwardly sketch a last Judgment with that candid friend 's physiognomy on the left . but all this be away from Sir Hugo , whose manner of imply that one 's gift be not of the high order be so exceedingly good - natured and comfortable that I begin to feel it an advantage not to be among those poor fellow at the tip - top . and his kindness to I taste all the well because it come out of his love for you , old boy . his chat be uncommonly amusing . by the way , he tell I that your Vandyke duchess be go with her husband yacht to the Mediterranean . I bethink I that it be possible to land from a yacht , or to be take on to a yacht from the land . shall you by chance have an opportunity of continue your theological discussion with the fair Supralapsarian I think you say her tenet be of that complexion ? be Duke Alphonso also theological ? perhaps an Arian who object to triplicity .   stage direction . while D. be read , a profound scorn gather in his face till at the last word he fling down the letter , grasp his coat - collar in a statuesque attitude and so remain with a look generally tremendous , throughout the following soliloquy , " o night , o blackness , etc . , etc . " 

 excuse the brevity of this letter . you be not use to more from I than a bare statement of fact , without comment or digression . one fact I have omit that the Klesmers on the eve of departure have behave magnificently , shin forth as might be expect from the planet of genius and fortune in conjunction . Mirah be rich with their oriental gift . 

 what luck it will be if you come back and present yourself at the Abbey while I be there ! I be go to behave with consummate discretion and win golden opinion , but I shall run up to town now and then , just for a peep into Gad Eden . you see how far I have get in Hebrew lore up with my Lord Bolingbroke , who know no Hebrew , but " understand that sort of learn and what be writ about it . " if Mirah command , I would go to a depth below the tri - literal root . already it make no difference to I whether the point be there or not . but while her brother 's life last I suspect she would not listen to a lover , even one whose " hair be like a flock of goat on Mount Gilead " and I flatter myself that few head would bear that try comparison well than mine . so I stay with my hope among the orchard - blossom . 

 your devoted , 

 HANS MEYRICK . 

 some month before , this letter from Hans would have divide Deronda 's thought irritatingly : its romancing , about Mirah would have have an unpleasant edge , scarcely anoint with any commiseration for his friend 's probable disappointment . but thing have alter since March . Mirah be no long so critically place with regard to the Meyricks , and Deronda 's own position have be undergo a change which have just be crown by the revelation of his birth . the new opening toward the future , though he would not trust in any definite vision , inevitably shed new light , and influence his mood toward past and present ; hence , what Hans call his hope now seem to Deronda , not a mischievous unreasonableness which rouse his indignation , but an unusually persistent bird - dance of an extravagant fancy , and he would have feel quite able to pity any consequent suffering of his friend 's , if he have believe in the suffering as probable . but some of the busy thought fill that long day , which pass without his receive any new summon from his mother , be give to the argument that Hans Meyrick 's nature be not one in which love could strike the deep root that turn disappointment into sorrow : it be too restless , too readily excitable by novelty , too ready to turn itself into imaginative material , and wear its grief as a fantastic costume . " already he be begin to play at love : he be take the whole affair as a comedy , " say Deronda to himself ; " he know very well that there be no chance for he . just like he never open his eye on any possible objection I could have to receive his outpouring about Mirah . poor old Hans ! if we be under a fiery hail together he would howl like a Greek , and if I do not howl too it would never occur to he that I be as badly off as he . and yet he be tender - hearted and affectionate in intention , and I can not   say that he be not active in imagine what go on in other people but then he always imagine it to fit his own inclination . " 

 with this touch of causticity Deronda get rid of the slight heat at present raise by Hans 's naive expansiveness . the nonsense about Gwendolen , convey the fact that she be go yacht with her husband , only suggest a disturbing sequel to his own strange parting with she . but there be one sentence in the letter which raise a more immediate , active anxiety . Hans 's suspicion of a hide sadness in Mirah be not in the direction of his wish , and hence , instead of distrust his observation here , Deronda begin to conceive a cause for the sadness . be it some event that have occur during his absence , or only the grow fear of some event ? be it something , perhaps alterable , in the new position which have be make for she ? or have Mordecai , against his habitual resolve , communicate to she those peculiar cherish hope about he , Deronda , and have she quickly sensitive nature be hurt by the discovery that her brother 's will or tenacity of visionary conviction have act coercively on their friendship be hurt by the fear that there be more of pity self - suppression than of equal regard in Deronda 's relation to he ? for amidst all Mirah 's quiet renunciation , the evident thirst of soul with which she receive the tribute of equality imply a correspond pain if she find that what she have take for a purely reverential regard toward her brother have its mixture of condescension . 

 in this last conjecture of Deronda 's he be not wrong as to the quality in Mirah 's nature on which he be found the latent protest against the treatment she have all her life be subject to until she meet he . for that gratitude which would not let she pass by any notice of their acquaintance without insist on the depth of her debt to he , take half its fervor from the keen comparison with what other have think enough to render to she . Deronda 's affinity in feel enable he to penetrate such secret . but he be not near the truth in admit the idea that Mordecai have break his characteristic reticence . to no soul but Deronda himself have he yet breathe the history of their relation to each other , or his confidence about his friend 's origin : it be not only that these subject be for he too sacred to be speak of without weighty reason , but that he have discern Deronda 's shrinking at any mention of his birth ; and the severity of reserve which have hinder Mordecai from answer a question on a private affair of the Cohen family tell yet more strongly here . 

 " Ezra , how be it ? " Mirah one day say to he " I be continually go to speak to Mr. Deronda as if he be a Jew ? " 

 he smile at she quietly , and say , " I suppose it be because he treat we as if he be our brother . but he love not to have the difference of birth dwelt upon . " 

 " he have never live with his parent , Mr. Hans , say , " continue Mirah , to whom this be necessarily a question of interest about every one for whom she have a regard . 

 " seek not to know such thing from Mr. Hans , " say Mordecai , gravely , lay his hand on her curl , as he be will not . " what Daniel Deronda wish we to know about himself be for he to tell we . " 

 and Mirah feel herself rebuke , as Deronda have do . but to be rebuke in this way by Mordecai make she rather proud . 

 " I see no one so great as my brother , " she say to Mrs. Meyrick one day that she call at the Chelsea house on her way home , and , accord to her hope , find the little mother alone . " it be difficult to think that he belong to the same world as those people I use to live amongst . I tell you once that they make life seem like a madhouse ; but when I be with Ezra he make I feel that his life be a great good , though he have suffer so much ; not like I , who want to die because I have suffer a little , and only for a little while . his soul be so full , it be impossible for he to wish for death as I do . I get the same sort of feeling from he that I get yesterday , when I be tired , and come home through the park after the sweet rain have fall and the sunshine lie on the grass and flower . everything in the sky and under the sky look so pure and beautiful that the weariness and trouble and folly seem only a small part of what be , and I become more patient and hopeful . " 

 a dove - like note of melancholy in this speech cause Mrs. Meyrick to look at Mirah with new examination . after lay down her hat and push her curl flat , with an air of fatigue , she place herself on a chair opposite her friend in her habitual attitude , her foot and hand just cross ; and at a distance she might have seem a colored statue of serenity . but Mrs. Meyrick discern a new look of suppress suffering in her face , which correspond to the hint that to be patient and hopeful require some extra influence . 

 " be there any fresh trouble on your mind , my dear ? " say Mrs. Meyrick , give up her needlework as a sign of concentrated attention . 

 Mirah hesitate before she say , " I be too ready to speak of trouble , I think . it seem unkind to put anything painful into other people 's mind , unless one be sure it would hinder something bad . and perhaps I be too hasty and fearful . " 

 " oh , my dear , mother be make to like pain and trouble for the sake of their child . be it because the singing lesson be so few , and be likely to fall off when the season come to an end ? success in these thing can not   come all at once . " Mrs. Meyrick do not believe that she be touch the real grief ; but a guess that could be correct would make an easy channel for confidence . 

 " no , not that , " say Mirah , shake her head gently . " I have be a little disappointed because so many lady say they want I to give they or their daughter lesson , and then I never hear of they again , but perhaps after the holiday I shall teach in some school . besides , you know , I be as rich as a princess now . I have not touch the hundred pound that Mrs. Klesmer give I ; and I should never be afraid that Ezra would be in want of anything , because there be Mr. Deronda , " and he say , ' it be the chief honor of my life that your brother will share anything with I . ' oh , no ! Ezra and I can have no fear for each other about such thing as food and clothing . " 

 " but there be some other fear on your mind , " say Mrs. Meyrick not without divination " a fear of something that may disturb your peace ; do not   be forecast evil , dear child , unless it be what you can guard against . anxiety be good for nothing if we can not   turn it into a defense . but there be no defense against all the thing that might be . have you any more reason for be anxious now than you have a month ago ? " 

 " yes , I have , " say Mirah . " I have keep it from Ezra . I have not dare to tell he . pray forgive I that I can not   do without tell you . I have more reason for be anxious . it be five day ago now . I be quite sure I see my father . " 

 Mrs. Meyrick shrink into a small space , pack her arm across her chest and lean forward to hinder herself from pelt that father with her bad epithet . 

 " the year have change he , " Mirah go on . " he have already be much alter and wear in the time before I leave he . you remember I say how he use sometimes to cry . he be always excited one way or the other . I have tell Ezra everything that I tell you , and he say that my father have take to gambling , which make people easily distress , and then again exalt . and now it be only a moment that I see he his face be more haggard , and his clothe be shabby . he be with a much worse - look man , who carry something , and they be hurry along after an omnibus . " 

 " well , child , he do not see you , I hope ? " 

 " no . I have just come from Mrs. Raymond 's , and I be wait to cross near the Marble Arch . soon he be on the omnibus and go out of sight . it be a dreadful moment . my old life seem to have come back again , and it be bad than it have ever be before . and I could not help feel it a new deliverance that he be go out of sight without know that I be there . and yet it hurt I that I be feel so it seem hateful in I almost like word I once have to speak in a play , that ' I have warm my hand in the blood of my kindre . ' for where might my father be go ? what may become of he ? and his have a daughter who would own he in spite of all , might have hinder the bad . be there any pain like see what ought to be the good thing in life turn into the bad ? all those opposite feeling be meet and press against each other , and take up all my strength . no one could act that . acting be slow and poor to what we go through within . I do not   know how I call a cab . I only remember that I be in it when I begin to think , ' I can not tell Ezra ; he must not know . ' " 

 " you be afraid of grieve he ? " Mrs. Meyrick ask , when Mirah have pause a little . 

 " yes and there be something more , " say Mirah , hesitatingly , as if she be examine she feeling before she would venture to speak of it . " I want to tell you ; I can not tell any one else . I could not have tell my own mother : I should have close it up before she . I feel shame for my father , and it be perhaps strange but the shame be great before Ezra than before any one else in the world . he desire I to tell he all about my life , and I obey he . but it be always like a smart to I to know that those thing about my father be in Ezra 's mind . and can you believe it ? when the thought haunt I how it would be if my father be to come and show himself before we both , what seem as if it would scorch I most be see my father shrink before Ezra . that be the truth . I do not   know whether it be a right feeling . but I can not   help think that I would rather try to maintain my father in secret , and bear a great deal in that way , if I could hinder he from meet my brother . " 

 " you must not encourage that feeling , Mirah , " say Mrs. Meyrick , hastily . " it would be very dangerous ; it would be wrong . you must not have concealment of that sort . " 

 " but ought I now to tell Ezra that I have see my father ? " say Mirah , with deprecation in her tone . 

 " no , " Mrs. Meyrick answer , dubitatively . " I do not   know that it be necessary to do that . your father may go away with the bird . it be not clear that he come after you ; you may never see he again . and then your brother will have be spar a useless anxiety . but promise I that if your father see you get hold of you in any way again and you will let we all know . promise I that solemnly , Mirah . I have a right to ask it . " 

 Mirah reflect a little , then lean forward to put her hand in Mrs. Meyrick 's , and say , " since you ask it , I do promise . I will bear this feeling of shame .
