  SHELLEY . 
 Madonna Pia , whose husband , feel himself injure by she , take she to his castle amid the swampy flat of the Maremma and get rid of she there , make a pathetic figure in Dante 's Purgatory , among the sinner who repent at the last and desire to be remember compassionately by their fellow - countryman . we know little about the ground of mutual discontent between the siennese couple , but we may infer with some confidence that the husband have never be a very delightful companion , and that on the flat of the Maremma his disagreeable manner have a background which throw they out remarkably ; whence in his desire to punish his wife to the unmost , the nature of thing be so far against he that in relieve himself of she he could not avoid make the relief mutual . and thus , without any hardness to the poor tuscan lady , who have her deliverance long ago , one may feel warrant in thinking of she with a less sympathetic interest than of the well know Gwendolen who , instead of be deliver from her error or earth and cleanse from their effect in purgatory , be at the very height of her entanglement in those fatal mesh which be weave within more closely than without , and often make the inward torture disproportionate to what be discernable as outward cause . 

 in take his wife with he on a yachting expedition , Grandcourt have no intention to get rid of she ; on the contrary , he want to feel more securely that she be his to do as he like with , and to make she feel it also . moreover , he be himself very fond of yachting : its dreamy do - nothing absolutism , unmoleste by social demand , suit his disposition , and he do not in the least regard it as an equivalent for the dreariness of the Maremma . he have his reason for carry Gwendolen out of reach , but they be not reason that can seem black in the mere statement . he suspect a grow spirit of opposition in she , and his feeling about the sentimental inclination she betray for Deronda be what in another man he would have call jealously . in himself it seem merely a resolution to put an end to such foolery as must have be go on in that prearrange visit of Deronda 's which he have divine and interrupt . 

 and Grandcourt might have plead that he be perfectly justify in take care that his wife should fulfill the obligation she have accept . her marriage be a contract where all the ostensible advantage be on her side , and it be only of those advantage that her husband should use his power to hinder she from any injurious self committal or unsuitable behavior . he know quite well that she have not marry he have not overcome her repugnance to certain fact out of love to he personally ; he have win she by the rank and luxury he have to give she , and these she have get : he have fulfil his side of the contract . 

 and Gwendolen , we know , be thoroughly aware of the situation . she could not excuse herself by say that there have be a tacit part of the contract on her side namely , that she mean to rule and have her own way . with all her early indulgence in the disposition to dominate , she be not one of the narrow - brain woman who through life regard all their own selfish demand as right , and every claim upon themselves as an injury . she have a root of conscience in she , and the process of purgatory have begin for she on the green earth : she know that she have be wrong . 

 but now enter into the soul of this young creature as she find herself , with the blue Mediterranean divide she from the world , on the tiny plank - island of a yacht , the domain of the husband to whom she feel that she have sell herself , and have be pay the strict price nay , pay more than she have dare to ask in the handsome maintenance of her mother : the husband to whom she have sell her truthfulness and sense of justice , so that he hold they throttle into silence , collar and drag behind he to witness what he would , without remonstrance . 

 what have she to complain of ? the yacht be of the prettiest ; the cabin fit up to perfection , smell of cedar , soft - cushion , hang with silk , expand with mirror ; the crew such as suit an elegant toy , one of they have even ringlet , as well as a bronze complexion and fine tooth ; and Mr. Lush be not there , for he have take his way back to England as soon as he have see all and everything on board . moreover , Gwendolen herself like the sea : it do not make she ill ; and to observe the rigging of the vessel and forecast the necessary adjustment be a sort of amusement that might have gratify her activity and enjoyment of imaginary rule ; the weather be fine , and they be coast southward , where even the rain - furrow , heat - crack clay become gem - like with purple shadow , and where one may float between blue and blue in an open - eyed dream that the world have do with sorrow . 

 but what can still that hunger of the heart which sicken the eye for beauty , and make sweet - scented ease an oppression ? what sort of Moslem paradise would quiet the terrible fury of moral repulsion and cow resistance which , like an eat pain intensify into torture , concentrate the mind in that poisonous misery ? while Gwendolen , throne on her cushion at evening , and behold the glory of sea and sky soften as if with boundless love around she , be hop that Grandcourt in his march up and down be not go to pause near she , not go to look at she or speak to she , some woman , under a smoky sky , oblige to consider the price of egg in arrange her dinner , be listen for the music of a footstep that would remove all risk from her foretaste of joy ; some couple , bend cheek by cheek , over a bit of work do by the one and delighted in by the other , be reckon the earning that would make they rich enough for a holiday among the furze and heather . 

 have Grandcourt the least conception of what be go on in the breast of his wife ? he conceive that she do not love he ; but be that necessary ? she be under his power , and he be not accustomed to soothe himself , as some cheerfully - dispose person be , with the conviction that he be very generally and justly beloved . but what lie quite away from his conception be , that she could have any special repulsion for he personally . how could she ? he himself know what personal repulsion be nobody well ; his mind be much furnish with a sense of what brute his fellow - creature be , both masculine and feminine ; what odious familiarity they have , what smirk , what mode of flourish their handkerchief , what costume , what lavender water , what bulge eye , and what foolish notion of make themselves agreeable by remark which be not want . in this critical view of mankind there be an affinity between he and Gwendolen before their marriage , and we know that she have be attractingly work upon by the refined negation he present to she . hence he understand her repulsion for Lush . but how be he to understand or conceive her present repulsion for Henleigh Grandcourt ? some man bring themselves to believe , and not merely maintain , the non - existence of an external world ; a few other believe themselves object of repulsion to a woman without be tell so in plain language . but Grandcourt do not belong to this eccentric body of thinker . he have all his life have reason to take a flattering view of his own attractiveness , and to place himself in fine antithesis to the man who , he see at once , must be revolt to a woman of taste . he have no idea of moral repulsion , and could not have believe , if he have be tell it , that there may be a resentment and disgust which will gradually make beauty more detestable than ugliness , through exasperation at that outward virtue in which hateful thing can flaunt themselves or find a supercilious advantage . 

 how , then , could Grandcourt divine what be go on in Gwendolen 's breast ? 

 for their behavior to each other scandalize no observer not even the foreign maid , warrant against sea - sickness ; nor Grandcourt 's own experienced valet : still less the picturesque crew , who regard they as a model couple in high life . their companionship consist chiefly in a well - breed silence . Grandcourt have no humorous observation at which Gwendolen could refuse to smile , no chit - chat to make small occasion of dispute . he be perfectly polite in arrange an additional garment over she when needful , and in hand she any object that he perceive she to need , and she could not fall into the vulgarity of accept or reject such politeness rudely . 

 Grandcourt put up his telescope and say , " there be a plantation of sugar - cane at the foot of that rock ; should you like to look ? " 

 Gwendolen say , " yes , please , " remember that she must try and interest herself in sugar - cane as something outside her personal affair . then Grandcourt would walk up and down and smoke for a long while , pause occasionally to point out a sail on the horizon , and at last would seat himself and look at Gwendolen with his narrow immovable gaze , as if she be part of the complete yacht ; while she , conscious of be look at be exert her ingenuity not to meet his eye . at dinner he would remark that the fruit be get stale , and they must put in somewhere for more ; or , observe that she do not drink the wine , he ask she if she would like any other kind well . a lady be oblige to respond to these thing suitably ; and even if she have not shrink from quarrel on other ground , quarrel with Grandcourt be impossible ; she might as well have make angry remark to a dangerous serpent ornamentally coil in her cabin without invitation . and what sort of dispute could a woman of any pride and dignity begin on a yacht ? 

 Grandcourt have intense satisfaction in lead his wife captive after this fashion ; it give their life on a small scale a royal representation and publicity in which every thing familiar be get rid of , and every body must do what be expect of they whatever might be their private protest the protest   keep strictly private   add to the piquancy of despotism . 

 to Gwendolen , who even in the freedom of her maiden time , have have very faint glimpse of any heroism or sublimity , the medium that now thrust itself everywhere before her view be this husband and her relation to he . the being close to we , whether in love or hate , be often virtually our interpreter of the world , and some feather - head gentleman or lady whom in pass we regret to take as legal tender for a human being , may be act as a melancholy theory of life in the mind of those who live with they like a piece of yellow and wavy glass that distort form and make color an affliction . their trivial sentence , their petty standard , their low suspicion , their loveless ennui , may be make somebody else 's life no well than a promenade through a pantheon of ugly idol . Gwendolen have that kind of window before she , affect the distant equally with the near . some unhappy wife be soothe by the possibility that they may become mother ; but Gwendolen feel that to desire a child for herself would have be a consenting to the completion of the injury she have be guilty of . she be reduce to dread lest she should become a mother . it be not the image of a new sweetly - bud life that come as a vision of deliverance from the monotony of distaste : it be an image of another sort . in the irritable , fluctuate stage of despair , gleams of hope come in the form of some possible accident . to dwell on the benignity of accident be a refuge from bad temptation . 

 the embitterment of hatred be often as unaccountable to onlooker as the growth of devoted love , and it not only seem but be really out of direct relation with any outward cause to be allege . passion be of the nature of seed , and find nourishment within , tend to a predominance which determine all current toward itself , and make the whole life its tributary . and the intense form of hatred be that root in fear , which compel to silence and drive vehemence into a constructive vindictiveness , an imaginary annihilation of the detest object , something like the hide rite of vengeance with which the persecute have make a dark vent for their rage , and soothe their suffering into dumbness . such hidden rite go on in the secrecy of Gwendolen 's mind , but not with soothe effect rather with the effect of a struggle terror . side by side with the dread of her husband have grow the self - dread , which urge she to flee from the pursuing image work by her pen - up impulse . the vision of her past wrong - doing , and what it have bring on she , come with a pale ghastly illumination over every imagined deed that be a rash effort at freedom , such as she have make in her marriage . moreover , she have learn to see all her act through the impression they would make on Deronda : whatever relief might come to she , she could not sever it from the judgment of she that would be create in his mind . not one word of flattery , of indulgence , of dependence on her favor , could be fasten on by she in all their intercourse , to weaken his restraining power over she   in this way Deronda 's effort over himself be repay ; and amid the dreary uncertainty of her spoiled life the possible remedy that lie in his mind , nay , the remedy that lie in her feeling for he , make she only hope . he seem to she a terrible - browed angel , from whom she could not think of conceal any deed so as to win an ignorant regard from he : it belong to the nature of their relation that she should be truthful , for his power over she have begin in the raising of a self - discontent which could be satisfied only by genuine change . but in no concealment have she now any confidence : her vision of what she have to dread take more decidedly than ever the form of some fiercely impulsive deed , commit as in a dream that she would instantaneously wake from to find the effect real though the image have be false : to find death under her hand , but instead of darkness , daylight ; instead of satisfied hatred , the dismay of guilt ; instead of freedom , the palsy of a new terror a white dead face from which she be forever try to flee and forever hold back . she remember Deronda 's word : they be continually recur in her thought 

 " turn your fear into a safeguard . keep your dread fix on the idea of increase your remorse .        take your fear as a safeguard . it be like quickness of hearing . it may make consequence passionately present to you . " 

 and so it be . in Gwendolen 's consciousness temptation and dread meet and star like two pale phantom , each see itself in the other each obstruct by its own image ; and all the while her full self beheld the apparition and sob for deliverance from they . 

 inarticulate prayer , no more definite than a cry , often sweep out from she into the vast silence , unbroken except by her husband 's breathing or the plash of the wave or the creaking of the mast ; but if ever she think of definite help , it take the form of Deronda 's presence and word , of the sympathy he might have for she , of the direction he might give she . it be sometimes after a white - lipped fierce - eyed temptation with murder finger have make its demon - visit that these good moment of inward cry and cling for rescue would come to she , and she would lie with wide - open eye in which the rise tear seem a blessing , and the thought , " I will not mind if I can keep from get wicked , " seem an answer to the indefinite prayer . 

 so the day pass , take with they light breeze beyond and about the Balearic Isles , and then to Sardinia , and then with gentle change persuade they northward again toward Corsica . but this float , gentle - waft existence , with its apparently peaceful influence , be become as bad as a nightmare to Gwendolen . 

 " how long be we to be yacht ? " she venture to ask one day after they have be touch at Ajaccio , and the mere fact of change in go ashore have give she a relief from some of the thought which seem now to cling about the very rigging of the vessel , mix with the air in the red silk cabin below , and make the smell of the sea odious . 

 " what else should we do ? " say Grandcourt . " I be not tired of it . I do not   see why we should not   stay out any length of time . there be less to bore one in this way . and where would you go to ? I be sick of foreign place . and we shall have enough of ryeland . would you rather be at Ryeland 's ? " 

 " oh , no , " say Gwendolen , indifferently , find all place alike undescribable as soon as she imagine herself and her husband in they . " I only wonder how long you would like this . " 

 " I like yacht long than anything else , " say Grandcourt ; " and I have none last year . I suppose you be begin to tire of it . woman be so confoundedly whimsical . they expect everything to give way to they . " 

 " oh , dear , no ! " say Gwendolen , let out her scorn in a flute - like tone . " I never expect you to give way . " 

 " why should I ? " say Grandcourt , with his inward voice , look at she , and then choose an orange for they be at table . 

 she make up her mind to a length of yatching that she could not see beyond ; but the next day , after a squall which have make she rather ill for the first time , he come down to she and say 

 " there be be the devil 's own work in the night . the skipper say we shall have to stay at Genoa for a week while thing be set right . " 

 " do you mind that ? " say Gwendolen , who lie look very white amidst her white drapery . 

 " I should think so . who want to be broil at Genoa ? " 

 " it will be a change , " say Gwendolen , make a little incautious by her languor . 

 " I do not   want any change . besides , the place be intolerable ; and one can not   move along the road . I shall go out in a boat , as I use to do , and manage it myself . one can get a few hour every day in that way instead of strive in a damnable hotel . " 

 here be a prospect which hold hope in it . Gwendolen think of hour when she would be alone , since Grandcourt would not want to take she in the said boat , and in her exultation at this unlooke - for relief , she have wild , contradictory fancy of what she might do with her freedom that " run away " which she have already innumerable time see to be a bad evil than any actual endurance , now find new argument as an escape from her bad self . also , visionary relief on a par with the fancy of a prisoner that the night wind may blow down the wall of his prison and save he from desperate device , insinuate itself as a well alternative , lawful to wish for . 

 the fresh current of expectation revive her energy , and enable she to take all thing with an air of cheerfulness and alacrity that make a change mark enough to be notice by her husband . she watch through the evening light to the sinking of the moon with less of awed loneliness than be habitual to her nay , with a vague impression that in this mighty frame of thing there might be some preparation of rescue for she . why not ? since the weather have just be on her side . this possibility of hop , after her long fluctuation amid fear , be like a first return of hunger to the long - languish patient . 

 she be wake the next morning by the casting of the anchor in the port of Genoa wake from a strangely - mixed dream in which she feel herself escape over the Mont Cenis , and wonder to find it warm even in the moonlight on the snow , till suddenly she meet Deronda , who tell she to go back . 

 in an hour or so from that dream she actually meet Deronda . but it be on the palatial staircase of the Italia , where she be feel warm in her light woolen dress and straw hat ; and her husband be by her side . 

 there be a start of surprise in Deronda before he could raise his hat and pass on . the moment do not seem to favor any close greeting , and the circumstance under which they have last part make he doubtful whether Grandcourt would be civilly inclined to he . 

 the doubt might certainly have be change into a disagreeable certainty , for Grandcourt on this unaccountable appearance of Deronda at Genoa of all place , immediately try to conceive how there could have be an arrangement between he and Gwendolen . it be true that before they be well in their room , he have see how difficult it be to shape such an arrangement with any probability , be too cool - headed to find it at once easily credible that Gwendolen have not only while in London hasten to inform Deronda of the yachting project , but have post a letter to he from Marseilles or Barcelona , advise he to travel to Genoa in time for the chance of meet she there , or of receive a letter from her telling of some other destination all which must have imply a miraculous foreknowledge in she , and in Deronda a bird - like facility in fly about and perch idly . still he be there , and though Grandcourt would not make a fool of himself by fabrication that other might call preposterous , he be not , for all that , dispose to admit fully that Deronda 's presence be , so far as Gwendolen be concern , a mere accident . it be a disgusting fact ; that be enough ; and no doubt she be well pleased . a man out of temper do not wait for proof before feel toward all thing animate and inanimate as if they be in a conspiracy against he , but at once threshe his horse or kick his dog in consequence . Grandcourt feel toward Gwendolen and Deronda as if he know they to be in a conspiracy against he , and here be an event in league with they . what he take for clearly certain and so far he divine the truth be that Gwendolen be now count on an interview with Deronda whenever her husband 's back be turn . 

 as he sit take his coffee at a convenient angle for observe she , he discern something which he feel sure be the effect of a secret delight some fresh ease in move and speak , some peculiar meaning in her eye , whatever she look on . certainly her trouble have not mar her beauty . Mrs. Grandcourt be handsomer than Gwendolen Harleth : her grace and expression be inform by a great variety of inward experience , give new play to her feature , new attitude in movement and repose ; her whole person and air have the nameless something which often make a woman more interesting after marriage than before , less confident that all thing be accord to her opinion , and yet with less of deer - like shyness more fully a human being . 

 this morning the benefit of the voyage seem to be suddenly reveal themselves in a new elasticity of mien . as she rise from the table and put she two heavily - jewel hand on each side of her neck , accord to she will not , she have no art to conceal that sort of joyous expectation which make the present more bearable than usual , just as when a man mean to go out he find it easy to be amiable to the family for a quarter of an hour beforehand . it be not impossible that a terri whose pleasure be concern would perceive those amiable sign and know their meaning know why his master stand in a peculiar way , talk with alacrity , and even have a peculiar gleam in his eye , so that on the least movement toward the door , the terri would scuttle to be in time . and , in dog fashion , Grandcourt discern the sign of Gwendolen 's expectation , interpret they with the narrow correctness which leave a world of unknown feeling behind . 

 " a just ring , please , and tell Gibbs to order some dinner for we at three , " say Grandcourt , as he too rise , take out a cigar , and then stretch his hand toward the hat that lie near . " I be go to send Angus to find a little sailing - boat for we to go out in ; one that I can manage , with you at the tiller . it be uncommonly pleasant these fine evening the least boring of anything we can do . " 

 Gwendolen turn cold . there be not only the cruel disappointment ; there be the immediate conviction that her husband have determine to take she because he would not leave she out of his sight ; and probably this dual solitude in a boat be the more attractive to he because it would be wearisome to she . they be not on the plank - island ; she feel it the more possible to begin a contest . but the gleam content have die out of she . there be a change in she like that of a glacier after sunset .

 " I can not   bear to go in a boat , " say Gwendolen , angrily . 

 " that be a sudden change , " say Grandcourt , with a slight sneer . " but , since you decline , we shall stay indoor . " 

 he lay down his hat again , light his cigar , and walk up and down the room , pause now and then to look out of the window . Gwendolen 's temper tell she to persist . she know very well now that Grandcourt would not go without she ; but if he must tyrannize over she , he should not do it precisely in the way he would choose . she would oblige he to stay in the hotel . without speak again , she pass into the adjoining bedroom and throw herself into a chair with her anger , see no purpose or issue only feel that the wave of evil have rush back upon she , and drag she away from her momentary breathing - place . 

 presently Grandcourt come in with his hat on , but throw it off and sit down sideways on a chair nearly in front of she , say , in his superficial drawl 

 " have you come round yet ? or do you find it agreeable to be out of temper . you make thing uncommonly pleasant for I . " 

 " why do you want to make they unpleasant for I ? " say Gwendolen , get helpless again , and feel the hot tear rise . 

 " now , will you be good enough to say what it be you have to complain of ? " say Grandcourt , look into her eye , and use his most inward voice . " be it that I stay indoor when you stay ? " 

 she could give no answer . the sort of truth that make any excuse for her anger could not be utter . in the conflict of despair and humiliation she begin to sob , and the tear roll down her cheek a form of agitation which she have never show before in her husband 's presence . 

 " I hope this be useful , " say Grandcourt , after a moment or two . " all I can say be , it be most confoundedly unpleasant . what the devil woman can see in this kind of thing , I do not   know . you see something to be get by it , of course . all I can see be , that we shall be shut up here when we might have be have a pleasant sail . "

 this extraordinary behavior , which have evidently some relation to Deronda , give more definiteness to Grandcourt 's conclusion . he draw his chair quite close in front of she , and say , in a low tone , " just be quiet and listen , will you ? " 

 there seem to be a magical effect in this close vicinity . Gwendolen shrink and cease to sob . she keep her eyelid down and clasp her hand tightly .

" I know very well what this nonsense mean . but if you suppose I be go to let you make a fool of I , just dismiss that notion from your mind . what be you look forward to , if you can not   behave properly as my wife ? there be disgrace for you , if you like to have it , but I do not   know anything else ; and as to Deronda , it be quite clear that he hang back from you . "

 Gwendolen 's word have so clear and tremendous a meaning for herself that she think they must have express it to Grandcourt , and have no soon utter they than she dread their effect . but his soul be garrison against presentiment and fear : he have the courage and confidence that belong to domination , and he be at that moment feel perfectly satisfied that he hold his wife with bit and bridle . by the time they have be marry a year she would cease to be restive . he continue stand with his air of indifference , till she feel her habitual stifle consciousness of have an immovable obstruction in her life , like the nightmare of behold a single form that serve to arrest all passage though the wide country lie open . 

 " what decision have you come to ? " he say , presently look at she . 
 " what order shall I give ? " 
 " oh , let we go , " say Gwendolen . the wall have begin to be an imprisonment , and while there be breath in this man he would have the mastery over she . his word have the power of thumb - screw and the cold touch of the rock . to resist be to act like a stupid animal unable to measure result . 

 so the boat be order . she even go down to the quay again with he to see it before midday . Grandcourt have recover perfect quietude of temper , and have a scornful satisfaction in the attention give by the nautical group to the milord , owner of the handsome yacht which have just put in for repair , and who be an Englishman be naturally so at home on the sea that he could manage a sail with the same ease that he could manage a horse . the sort of exultation he have discern in Gwendolen this morning she now think that she discern in he ; and it be true that he have set his mind on this boating , and carry out his purpose as something that people might not expect he to do , with the gratified impulse of a strong will which have nothing well to exert itself upon . he have remarkable physical courage , and be proud of it or rather he have a great contempt for the coarser , bulkier man who generally have less . moreover , he be rule that Gwendolen should go with he . 

 and when they come down again at five o'clock , equip for their boating , the scene be as good as a theatrical representation for all beholder . this handsome , fair - skin English couple , manifest the usual eccentricity of their nation , both of they proud , pale , and calm , without a smile on their face , move like creature who be fulfil a supernatural destiny it be a thing to go out and see , a thing to paint . the husband 's chest , back , and arm , show very well in his close - fit dress , and the wife be declare to be a statue . 

 some suggestion be proffer concern a possible change in the breeze , and the necessary care in put about , but Grandcourt 's manner make the speaker understand that they be too officious , and that he know well than they . 

 Gwendolen , keep her impassable air , as they move away from the strand , feel her imagination obstinately at work . she be not afraid of any outward danger she be afraid of her own wish which be take shape possible and impossible , like a cloud of demon - face . she be afraid of her own hatred , which under the cold iron touch that have compel she to - day have gather a fierce intensity . as she sit guide the tiller under her husband 's eye , do just what he tell she , the strife within she seem like her own effort to escape from herself . she cling to the thought of Deronda : she persuade herself that he would not go away while she be there he know that she need help . the sense that he be there would save she from act out the evil within . and yet quick , quick , come image , plan of evil that would come again and seize she in the night , like fury prepare the deed that they would straightway avenge . 

 they be take out of the port and carry eastward by a gentle breeze . some cloud temper the sunlight , and the hour be always deepen toward the supreme beauty of evening . sail large and smaller change their aspect like sensitive thing , and make a cheerful companionship , alternately near and far . the grand city shine more vaguely , the mountain look out above it , and there be stillness as in an island sanctuary . yet suddenly Gwendolen let her hand fall , and say in a scarcely audible tone , " God help I ! " 

 " what be the matter ? " say Grandcourt , not distinguish the word . 

 " oh , nothing , " say Gwendolen , rouse herself from her momentary forgetfulness and resume the rope . 

 " do not   you find this pleasant ? " say Grandcourt . 

 " very . " 

 " you admit now we could not   have do anything well ? " 

 " no I see nothing well . I think we shall go on always , like the 
 Flying Dutchman , " say Gwendolen wildly . 
 Grandcourt give she one of his narrow examining glance , and then say , " if you like , we can go to Spezia in the morning , and let they take we up there . " 

 " no ; I shall like nothing well than this . " 

 " very well : we will do the same to - morrow . but we must be turn in soon . I shall put about . " 

 CHAPTER LV . 

  " Ritorna a tua scienza 
  Che vuoi , quanto la cosa e piu perfetta 
  Piu senta if bene , e cosi la doglienza . " 
  DANTE . 
 when Deronda meet Gwendolen and Grandcourt on the staircase , his mind be seriously preoccupy . he have just be summon to the second interview with his mother . 

 in two hour after his parting from she he know that the Princess Halm - Eberstein have leave the hotel , and so far as the purpose of his journey to Genoa be concern , he might himself have set off on his way to Mainz , to deliver the letter from Joseph Kalonymos , and get possession of the family chest . but mixed mental condition , which do not resolve themselves into definite reason , hinder he from departure . long after the farewell he be keep passive by a weight of retrospective feeling . he live again , with the new keenness of emotive memory , through the exciting scene which seem past only in the sense of preparation for their actual presence in his soul . he allow himself in his solitude to sob , with perhaps more than a woman 's acuteness of compassion , over that woman 's life so near to his , and yet so remote . he behold the world change for he by the certitude of tie that alter the poise of hope and fear , and give he a new sense of fellowship , as if under cover of the night he have join the wrong band of wanderer , and find with the rise of morning that the tent of his kindre be group far off . he have a quiver imaginative sense of close relation to the grandfather who have be animate by strong impulse and beloved thought , which be now perhaps be rouse from their slumber within himself . and through all this passionate meditation Mordecai and Mirah be always present , as being who clasp hand with he in sympathetic silence . 

 of such quick , responsive fibre be Deronda make , under that mantle of self - control reserve into which early experience have throw so much of his young strength . 

 when the persistent ringing of a bell as a signal remind he of the hour he think of look into Bradshaw , and make the brief necessary preparation for start by the next train thought of it , but make no movement in consequence . wish go to Mainz and what he be to get possession of there to London and the being there who make the strong attachment of his life ; but there be other wish that clung in these moment to Genoa , and they keep he where he be by that force which urge we to linger over an interview that carry a presentiment of final farewell or of overshadow sorrow . Deronda do not formally say , " I will stay over to - night , because it be Friday , and I should like to go to the evening service at the synagogue where they must all have go ; and besides , I may see the Grandcourts again .  extension , we know , be a very imperfect measure of thing ; and the length of the sun 's journeying can no more tell we how life have advance than the acreage of a field can tell we what growth may be active within it . a man may go south , and , stumble over a bone , may meditate upon it till he have find a new starting - point for anatomy ; or eastward , and discover a new key to language tell a new story of race ; or he may head an expedition that open new continental pathway , get himself maim in body , and go through a whole heroic poem of resolve and endurance ; and at the end of a few month he may come back to find his neighbor grumble at the same parish grievance as before , or to see the same elderly gentleman tread the pavement in discourse with himself , shake his head after the same percussive butcher 's boy , and pause at the same shop - window to look at the same print . if the swift thinking have about the pace of a greyhound , the slow must be suppose to move , like the limpet , by an apparent sticking , which after a good while be discern to be a slight progression . such difference be manifest in the variable intensity which we call human experience , from the revolutionary rush of change which make a new inner and outer life , to that quiet recurrence of the familiar , which have no other epoch than those of hunger and the heaven .

 " you do not   repent the choice of the law as a profession , Rex ? " say his father .

 " I do not   see that law - rubbish be bad than any other sort . it be not so bad as the rubbishy literature that people choke their mind with . it do not   make one so dull . our witty man have often be lawyer . any orderly way of look at thing as case and evidence seem to I well than a perpetual wash of odd and end bear on nothing in particular . and then , from a high point of view , the foundation and the growth of law make the most interesting aspect of philosophy and history . of course there will be a good deal that be troublesome , drudge , perhaps exasperate . but the great prize in life can not   be win easily I see that . " 

 " well , my boy , the good augury of a man 's success in his profession be that he think it the fine in the world . but I fancy it so with most work when a man go into it with a will .  who have be quite free from egoistic escape of the imagination , picture desirable consequence on his own future in the presence of another 's misfortune , sorrow , or death ? the expect promotion or legacy be the common type of a temptation which make speech and even prayer a severe avoidance of the most insistent thought , and sometimes raise an inward shame , a self - distaste that be bad than any other form of unpleasant companionship . in Rex 's nature the shame be immediate , and overspread like an ugly light all the hurrying image of what might come , which thrust themselves in with the idea that Gwendolen be again free overspread they , perhaps , the more persistently because every phantasm of a hope be quickly nullify by a more substantial obstacle . before the vision of " Gwendolen free " rise the impassable vision of " Gwendolen rich , exalted , court ; " and if in the former time , when both their life be fresh , she have turn from his love with repugnance , what ground be there for suppose that her heart would be more open to he in the future ?

Sir Hugo have begin to talk of the Grandcourts as the less difficult subject between himself and Deronda ; but they be both wish to overcome a reluctance to perfect frankness on the event which touch their relation to each other . " 

 " I desire to meet your goodness by perfect confidence , sir , " say Deronda . " but I can not   answer those question truly by a simple yes or no . much that I have hear about the past have pain I . and it have be a pain to meet and part with my mother in her suffering state , as I have be compel to do , but it be no pain it be rather a clearing up of doubt for which I be thankful , to know my parentage . as to the effect on my position , there will be no change in my gratitude to you , sir , for the fatherly care and affection you have always show I . but to know that I be bear a Jew , may have a momentous influence on my life , which I be hardly able to tell you of at present . " 

 Deronda speak the last sentence with a resolve that overcome some diffidence . he feel that the difference between Sir Hugo 's nature and his own would have , by - and - by , to disclose themselves more markedly than have ever yet be needful . the baronet give he a quick glance , and turn to walk on . after a few moment ' silence , in which he have review all the material in his memory which would enable he to interpret Deronda 's word , he say 

 " I have long expect something remarkable from you , Dan ; but , for God 's sake , do not   go into any eccentricity ! I can tolerate any man 's difference of opinion , but let he tell it I without get himself up as a lunatic . at this stage of the world , if a man want to be take seriously , he must keep clear of melodrama . do not   misunderstand I . " 

 " I must go first to Mainz to get away a chest of my grandfather 's , and perhaps to see a friend of his , " say Deronda . " although the chest have be lie there these twenty year , I have an unreasonable sort of nervous eagerness to get it away under my care , as if it be more likely now than before that something might happen to it . and perhaps I be the more uneasy , because I linger after my mother leave , instead of set out immediately . yet I can not   regret that I be here else Mrs. Grandcourt would have have none but servant to act for she . " 

 " yes , yes , " say Sir Hugo , with a flippancy which be an escape of some vexation hide under his more serious speech ; " I hope you be not go to set a dead Jew above a live Christian . " 

 Deronda color , and repress a retort . they be just turn into the Italia . 

 CHAPTER LX . 

 " but I shall say no more of this at this time ; for this be to be feel and not to be talk of ; and they who never touch it with their finger may secretly perhaps laugh at it in their heart and be never the wiser . " JEREMY TAYLOR . 

 the Roman Emperor in the legend put to death ten learn Israelites to avenge the sale of Joseph by his brother . and there have always be enough of his kidney , whose piety lie in punish who can see the justice of grudge but not of gratitude . for you shall never convince the strong feeling that it hath not the strong reason , or incline he who hath no love to believe that there be good ground for love . as we may learn from the order of word - making , wherein love precedeth lovable . 

 when Deronda present his letter at the banking - house in the Schuster Strasse at Mainz , and ask for Joseph Kalonymos , he be presently show into an inner room , where , seat at a table arrange open letter , be the white - bearded man whom he have see the year before in the synagogue at Frankfort . he wear his hat it seem to be the same old feel hat as before and near he be a packed portmanteau with a wrap and overcoat upon it . on see Deronda enter he rise , but do not advance or put out his hand . look at he with small penetrate eye which glitter like black gem in the midst of his yellowish face and white hair , he say in german 

 " good ! it be now you who seek I , young man . " 

 " yes ; I seek you with gratitude , as a friend of my grandfather 's , " say 
 Deronda , " and I be under an obligation to you for give yourself much 
 trouble on my account . " he speak without difficulty in that liberal 
 german tongue which take many strange accent to its maternal bosom . 
 Kalonymos now put out his hand and say cordially , " so you be no long angry at be something more than an Englishman ? " 

 " on the contrary . I thank you heartily for help to save I from remain in ignorance of my parentage , and for take care of the chest that my grandfather leave in trust for I . "

 he say in Hebrew , quote from one of the fine hymn in the Hebrew liturgy , " as thy goodness have be great to the former generation , even so may it be to the latter . " then after pause a little he begin , " young man , I rejoice that I be not yet set off again on my travel , and that you be come in time for I to see the image of my friend as he be in his youth no long pervert from the fellowship of your people no long shrink in proud wrath from the touch of he who seem to be claim you as a Jew . you come with thankfulness yourself to claim the kindred and heritage that wicked contrivance would have rob you of . you come with a willing soul to declare , ' I be the grandson of Daniel Charisi . ' be it not so ? " 

 " assuredly it be , " say Deronda . " but let I say that I should at no time have be incline to treat a Jew with incivility simply because he be a Jew . you can understand that I shrink from say to a stranger , ' I know nothing of my mother . ' " 

 " a sin , a sin ! " say Kalonymos , put up his hand and close his eye in disgust . " a robbery of our people as when our youth and maiden be rear for the Roman Edom . but it be frustrate . I have frustrate it . when Daniel Charisi may his Rock and his Redeemer guard he ! when Daniel Charisi be a stripling and I be a lad little above his shoulder , we make a solemn vow always to be friend . he say , ' let we bind ourselves with duty , as if we be son of the same mother . ' that be his bent from first to last as he say , to fortify his soul with bond . it be a saying of his , ' let we bind love with duty ; for duty be the love of law ; and law be the nature of the Eternal . ' so we bind ourselves . and though we be much apart in our later life , the bond have never be break . when he be dead , they seek to rob he ; but they could not rob he of I . I rescue that remainder of he which he have prize and preserve for his offspring . and I have restore to he the offspring they have rob he of . I will bring you the chest forthwith . " 

 Kalonymos leave the room for a few minute , and return with a clerk who carry the chest , set it down on the floor , draw off a leather cover , and go out again . it be not very large , but be make heavy by ornamental bracer and handle of gilt iron . the wood be beautifully incise with arabic lettering . 

 " so ! " say Kalonymos , return to his seat . " and here be the curious key , " he add , take it from a small leathern bag . " bestow it carefully . I trust you be methodic and wary . " he give Deronda the monitory and slightly suspicious look with which age be apt to commit any object to the keeping of youth . 

 " I shall be more careful of this than of any other property , " say Deronda , smile and put the key in his breast - pocket . " I never before possess anything that be a sign to I of so much cherished hope and effort . and I shall never forget that the effort be partly yours . have you time to tell I more of my grandfather ? or shall I be trespass in stay long ? " 

 " stay yet a while . in an hour and eighteen minute I start for Trieste , " say Kalonymos , look at his watch , " and presently my son will expect my attention . will you let I make you known to they , so that they may have the pleasure of show hospitality to my friend 's grandson ? they dwell here in ease and luxury , though I choose to be a wanderer . " 

 " I shall be glad if you will commend I to their acquaintance for some future opportunity , " say Deronda . " there be press claim call I to England friend who may be much in need of my presence . I have be keep away from they too long by unexpected circumstance . but to know more of you and your family would be motive enough to bring I again to Mainz . " 

 " good ! I you will hardly find , for I be beyond my threescore year and ten , and I be a wanderer , carry my shroud with I . but my son and their child dwell here in wealth and unity . the day be change for we since Karl the great fetch my ancestor from Italy to bring some tincture of knowledge to our rough german brother . I and my contemporary have have to fight for it too . our youth fall on evil day ; but this we have win ; we increase our wealth in safety , and the learning of all Germany be feed and fatten by jewish brain though they keep not always their jewish heart . have you be leave altogether ignorant of your people 's life , young man ? " 

 " no , " say Deronda , " I have lately , before I have any true suspicion of my parentage , be lead to study everything belong to their history with more interest than any other subject . it turn out that I have be make myself ready to understand my grandfather a little . " he be anxious less the time should be consume before this circuitous course of talk could lead they back to the topic he most care about . age do not easily distinguish between what it need to express and what youth need to know - distance seeming to level the object of memory ; and keenly active as Joseph Kalonymos show himself , an inkstand in the wrong place would have hinder his imagination from get to beyrout : he have be use to unite restless travel with punctilious observation . but Deronda 's last sentence answer its purpose . 

 " so - you would perhaps have be such a man as he if your education have not hinder ; for you be like he in feature : yet not altogether , young man . he have an iron will in his face : it brace up everybody about he . when he be quite young he have already get one deep upright line in his brow . I see none of that in you . Daniel Charisi use to say , ' well , a wrong will than a wavering ; well a steadfast enemy than an uncertain friend ; well a false belief than no belief at all . ' what he despise most be indifference . he have long reason than I can give you . " 

 " yet his knowledge be not narrow ? " say Deronda , with a tacit reference to the usual excuse for indecision that it come from know too much . 

 " narrow ? no , " say Kalonymos , shake his head with a compassionate smile " from his childhood upward , he drink in learn as easily as the plant suck up water . but he early take to medicine and theory about life and health . he travel to many country , and spend much of his substance in see and know . what he use to insist on be that the strength and wealth of mankind depend on the balance of separateness and communication , and he be bitterly against our people lose themselves among the gentile ; ' it be no well , ' say he , ' than the many sort of grain go back from their variety into sameness . ' he mingle all sort of learn ; and in that he be like our arabic writer in the golden time . we study together , but he go beyond I . though we be bosom friend , and he pour himself out to I , we be as different as the inside and outside of the bowl . I stand up for two notion of my own : I take Charisi 's saying as I take the shape of the tree : they be there , not to be dispute about . it come to the same thing in both of we ; we be both faithful Jews , thankful not to be Gentiles . and since I be a ripe man , I have be what I be now , for all but age - love to wander , love transaction , love to behold all thing , and care nothing about hardship . Charisi thought continually of our people 's future : he go with all his soul into that part of our religion : I , not . so we have freedom , I be content . our people wander before they be drive . young man when I be in the East , I lie much on deck and watch the great star . the sight of they satisfy I . I know they as they rise , and hunger not to know more . Charisi be satisfied with no sight , but piece it out with what have be before and what would come after . yet we love each other , and as he say , he bind our love with duty ; we solemnly pledge ourselves to help and defend each other to the last . I have fulfil my pledge . " here Kalonymos rise , and Deronda , rise also , say 

 " and in be faithful to he you have cause justice to be do to I . it would have be a robbery of I too that I should never have know of the inheritance he have prepare for I . I thank you with my whole soul . " 

 " be worthy of he , young man . what be your vocation ? " this question be put with a quick abruptness which embarrass Deronda , who do not feel it quite honest to allege his law - reading as a vocation . he answer 

 " I can not say that I have any . " 

 " get one , get one . the Jew must be diligent . you will call yourself a Jew and profess the faith of your father ? " say Kalonymos , put his hand on Deronda 's shoulder and look sharply in his face . 

 " I shall call myself a Jew , " say Deronda , deliberately , become slightly pale under the pierce eye of his questioner . " but I will not say that I shall profess to believe exactly as my father have believe . our father themselves change the horizon of their belief and learn of other race . but I think I can maintain my grandfather 's notion of separateness with communication . I hold that my first duty be to my own people , and if there be anything to be do toward restore or perfect their common life , I shall make that my vocation . " 

 it happen to Deronda at that moment , as it have often happen to other , that the need for speech make an epoch in resolve . his respect for the questioner would not let he decline to answer , and by the necessity to answer he find out the truth for himself . 

 " ah , you argue and you look forward you be Daniel Charisi 's grandson , " say Kalonymos , add a benediction in Hebrew . 

 with that they part ; and almost as soon as Deronda be in London , the aged man be again on shipboard , greet the friendly star without any eager curiosity . 

 CHAPTER LXI .

 there be another house besides the white house at Pennicote , another breast besides Rex Gascoigne 's , in which the news of Grandcourt 's death cause both strong agitation and the effort to repress it . 

 it be Hans Meyrick 's habit to send or bring in the Times for his mother 's reading . she be a great reader of news , from the widest - reach politic to the list of marriage ; the latter , she say , give she the pleasant sense of finish the fashionable novel without have read they , and see the hero and heroine happy without know what poor creature they be . on a Wednesday , there be reason why Hans always choose to bring the paper , and to do so about the time that Mirah have nearly end give Mab her weekly lesson , avow that he come then because he want to hear Mirah sing . but on the particular Wednesday now in question , after enter the house as quietly as usual with his latch - key , he appear in the parlor , shake the Times aloft with a crackling noise , in remorseless interruption of Mab 's attempt to render Lascia ch'io pianga with a remote imitation of her teacher . piano and song cease immediately ; Mirah , who have be play the accompaniment , involuntarily start up and turn round , the crackling sound , after the occasional trick of sound , have seem to she something thunderous ; and Mab say

" in a pregnant tone to some innocent person who have not embark his wisdom in the same boat with ours , and find our information flat .

Mr. Deronda would not call that lucky to pierce my brother 's heart . " 

 all three be strike with the sudden transformation . Mirah 's face , with a look of anger that might have suit Ithuriel , pale , even to the lip that be usually so rich of tint , be not far from poor Hans , who sit transfix , blush under it as if he have be a girl , while he say , nervously

 it be Mab who speak , while Mrs. Meyrick 's face seem to reflect some of Hans ' discomfort .

 han , on his side , have a mind equally busy . " 

 Hans could not but interpret this invitation as a sign of pardon . he have not enough understanding of what Mirah 's nature have be work into by her early experience , to divine how the very strength of her late excitement have make it pass more quickly into the resolute acceptance of pain . when he have say , " if you will let I , " and they go in together , half his grief be go , and he be spin a little romance of how his devotion might make he indispensable to Mirah in proportion as Deronda give his devotion elsewhere . this be quite fair , since his friend be provide for accord to his own heart ; and on the question of Judaism Hans feel thoroughly fortify : who ever hear in tale or history that a woman 's love go in the track of her race and religion ? Moslem and jewish damsel be always attract toward Christians , and now if Mirah 's heart have go forth too precipitately toward Deronda , here be another case in point . Hans be will not to make merry with his own argument , to call himself a Giaour , and antithesis the sole clue to event ; but he believe a little in what he laugh at . and thus his bird - like hope , construct on the light principle , soar again in spite of heavy circumstance . 

 they find Mordecai look singularly happy , hold a closed letter in his hand , his eye glow with a quiet triumph which in his emaciated face give the idea of a conquest over assail death . after the greeting between he and Hans , Mirah put her arm round her brother 's neck and look down at the letter in his hand , without the courage to ask about it , though she feel sure that it be the cause of his happiness . 

 " a letter from Daniel Deronda , " say Mordecai , answer her look . " brief only say that he hop soon to return . unexpected claim have detain he . the promise of see he again be like the bow in the cloud to I , " continue Mordecai , look at Hans ; " and to you it must be a gladness . for who have two friend like he ? " 

 while Hans be answer Mirah slip away to her own room ; but not to indulge in any outburst of the passion within she . if the angel , once suppose to watch the toilet of woman , have enter the little chamber with she and let she shut the door behind they , they would only have see she take off her hat , sit down and press her hand against her temple as if she have suddenly reflect that her head ache ; then rise to dash cold water on her eye and brow and hair till her backward curl be full of crystal bead , while she have dry her brow and look out like a freshly - open flower from among the dewy tress of the woodland ; then give deep sigh of relief , and put on her little slipper , sit still after that action for a couple of minute , which seem to she so long , so full of thing to come , that she rise with an air of recollection , and go down to make tea . 

 something of the old life have return . she have be use to remember that she must learn her part , must go to rehearsal , must act and sing in the evening , must hide her feeling from her father ; and the more painful her life grow , the more she have be use to hide . the force of her nature have long find its chief action in resolute endurance , and to - day the violence of feeling which have cause the first jet of anger have quickly transform itself into a steady facing of trouble , the well - know companion of her young year . but while she move about and speak as usual , a close observer might have discern a difference between this apparent calm , which be the effect of restrain energy , and the sweet genuine calm of the month when she first feel a return of her infantine happiness . 

 those who have be indulge by fortune and have always think of calamity as what happen to other , feel a blind incredulous rage at the reversal of their lot , and half believe that their wild cry will alter the course of the storm . Mirah feel no such surprise when familiar Sorrow come back from brief absence , and sit down with she accord to the old use and will not . and this habit of expect trouble rather than joy , hinder she from have any persistent belief in opposition to the probability which be not merely suggest by Hans , but be support by her own private knowledge and long - grow presentiment . an attachment between Deronda and Mrs. Grandcourt , to end in their future marriage , have the aspect of a certainty for her feeling . there have be no fault in he : fact have order themselves so that there be a tie between he and this woman who belong to another world than hers and Ezra 's nay , who seem another sort of be than Deronda , something foreign that would be a disturbance in his life instead of blend with it . well , well but if it could have be defer so as to make no difference while Ezra be there ! she do not know all the momentousness of the relation between Deronda and her brother , but she have see , and instinctively feel enough to forebode its be incongruous with any close tie to Mrs. Grandcourt ; at least this be the clothing that Mirah first give to her mortal repugnance . but in the still , quick action of her consciousness , thought go on like change state of sensation unbroken by her habitual act ; and this inward language soon say distinctly that the mortal repugnance would remain even if Ezra be secure from loss . 

 " what I have read about and sing about and see act , be happen to I this that I be feel be the love that make jealousy ; " so impartially Mirah sum up the charge against herself . but what difference could this pain of hers make to any one else ? " I could bear everything that use to be but this be bad this be bad , I use not to have horrible feeling ! " say the poor child in a loud whisper to her pillow . strange that she should have to pray against any feeling which concern Deronda ! 

 but this conclusion have be reach through an evening spend in attend to Mordecai , whose exaltation of spirit in the prospect of see his friend again , dispose he to utter many thought aloud to Mirah , though such communication be often interrupt by interval apparently fill with an inward utterance that animate his eye and give an occasional silent action to his lip . one thought especially occupy he .

 " my sister , thou hast read too many play , where the writer delight in show the human passion as indwell demon , unmixe with the relent and devout element of the soul . Thou judgest by the play , and not by thy own heart , which be like our mother 's . "

 imagine the difference in Deronda 's state of mind when he leave England and when he return to it . he have set out for Genoa in total uncertainty how far the actual bent of his wish and affection would be encourage how far the claim reveal to he might draw he into new path , far away from the track his thought have lately be pursue with a consent of desire which uncertainty make dangerous . he come back with something like a discover charter warrant the inherit right that his ambition have begin to yearn for : he come back with what be well than freedom with a duteous bond which his experience have be prepare he to accept gladly , even if it have be attend with no promise of satisfy a secret passionate longing never yet allow to grow into a hope . but now he dare avow to himself the hidden selection of his love . since the hour when he leave the house at Chelsea in full - hearted silence under the effect of Mirah 's farewell look and word their exquisite appealingness stir in he that deep - lay care for womanhood which have begin when his own lip be like a girl 's her hold on his feeling have help he to be blameless in word and deed under the difficult circumstance we know of . there seem no likelihood that he could ever woo this creature who have become dear to he amidst association that forbid wooing ;  but now the door be open , and while none enter , a well - know voice say : " Daniel Deronda may he come in ? "

 however , he send the note to Deronda 's chamber , and it find he there .

 CHAPTER LXVI . 

  " one day still fierce ' mid many a day strike calm . " 
  BROWNING : the King and the Book . 
 meanwhile Ezra and Mirah , whom Gwendolen do not include in her thinking about Deronda , be have their relation to he draw close and bring into full light . 

 the father Lapidoth have quit his daughter at the doorstep , rule by that possibility of stake something in play or bet which present itself with the handling of any sum beyond the price of stay actual hunger , and leave no care for alternative prospect or resolution . until he have lose everything he never consider whether he would apply to Mirah again or whether he would brave his son 's presence . in the first moment he have shrink from encounter Ezra as he would have shrink from any other situation of disagreeable constraint ; and the possession of Mirah 's purse be enough to banish the thought of future necessity . the gambling appetite be more absolutely dominant than bodily hunger , which can be neutralize by an emotional or intellectual excitation ; but the passion for watch chance the habitual suspensive poise of the mind in actual or imaginary play nullifie the susceptibility of other excitation . in its final , imperious stage , it seem the unjoyous dissipation of demon , seek diversion on the burn marl of perdition . 

 but every form of selfishness , however abstract and unhuman , require the support of at least one meal a day ; and though Lapidoth 's appetite for food and drink be extremely moderate , he have slip into a shabby , unfriendly form of life in which the appetite could not be satisfied without some ready money . when , in a brief visit at a house which announce " pyramid " on the window - blind , he have first double and treble and finally lose Mirah 's thirty shilling , he go out with her empty purse in his pocket , already balance in his mind whether he should get another immediate stake by pawn the purse , or whether he should go back to she give himself a good countenance by restore the purse , and declare that he have use the money in pay a score that be stand against he . besides , among the sensibility still leave strong in Lapidoth be the sensibility to his own claim , and he appear to himself to have a claim on any property his child might possess , which be strong than the justice of his son 's resentment . after all , to take up his lodging with his child be the good thing he could do ; and the more he think of meet Ezra the less he wince from it , his imagination be more wrought on by the chance of his get something into his pocket with safety and without exertion , than by the threat of a private humiliation .  CHAPTER LXVII . 

  the godhead in we wrings our noble deed 
  from our reluctant self . 
 it be an unpleasant surprise to Deronda when he return from the Abbey to find the undesirable father instal in the lodging at Brompton . " Poor Ezra feel very much as if he have a convict on leave under his charge . unless he see his father work , it be not possible to believe that he would work in good faith . but by this arrangement he fasten on himself the burden of his father 's presence , which be make painful not only through his deep , long association , but also through Lapidoth 's restlessness of temperament , which show itself the more as he become familiarized with his situation , and lose any awe he have feel of his son . the fact be , he be put a strong constraint on himself in confine his attention for the sake of win Deronda 's favor ; and like a man in an uncomfortable garment he give himself relief at every opportunity , go out to smoke , or move about and talk , or throw himself back in his chair and remain silent , but incessantly carry on a dumb language of facial movement or gesticulation : and if Mirah be in the room , he would fall into his old habit of talk with she , gossip about their former doing and companion , or repeat quirk and story , and plot of the play he use to adapt , in the belief that he could at will command the vivacity of his early time . all this be a mortal infliction to Ezra ; and when Mirah be at home she try to relieve he , by get her father down into the parlor and keep watch over he there . what duty be make of a single difficult resolve ? the difficulty lie in the daily unflinche support of consequence that mar the blessed return of morning with the prospect of irritation to be suppress or shame to be endure . and such consequence be be bear by these , as by many other heroic child of an unworthy father with the prospect , at least to Mirah , of their stretching onward through the solid part of life .

  COLERIDGE . 
 Deronda 's eagerness to confess his love could hardly have have a strong stimulus than Hans have give it in his assurance that Mirah need relief from jealousy . he go on his next visit to Ezra with the determination to be resolute in use nay , in request an opportunity of private conversation with she . if she accept his love , he feel courageous about all other consequence , and as she betroth husband he would gain a protective authority which might be a desirable defense for she in future difficulty with her father .  his foreboding would have be strengthen if he have know what be go on in the father 's mind . that amount of restlessness , that desultoriness of attention , which make a small torture to Ezra , be to lapidoth an irksome submission to restraint , only make bearable by his thinking of it as a means of by - and - by secure a well - condition freedom . he begin with the intention of await some really good chance , such as an opening for get a considerable sum from Deronda ; but all the while he be look about curiously , and try to discover where Mirah deposit her money and her key . the imperious gambling desire within he , which carry on its activity through every other occupation , and make a continuous web of imagination that hold all else in its mesh , would hardly have be under the control of a contract purpose , if he have be able to lay his hand on any sum worth capturing . but Mirah , with her practical clear - sightedness , guard against any frustration of the promise she have give to Ezra , by confide all money , except what she be immediately in want of , to Mrs. Meyrick 's care , and Lapidoth feel himself under an irritate completeness of supply in kind as in a lunatic asylum where everything be make safe against he . to have open a desk or drawer of Mirah 's , and pocket any bank - note find there , would have be to his mind a sort of domestic appropriation which have no disgrace in it ; the degree of liberty a man allow himself with other people 's property be often delicately draw , even beyond the boundary where the law begin to lay its hold which be the reason why spoon be a safe investment than mining share . " begin to search about on the floor .

 he , meet she frightened gaze , immediately catch alarm from it and answer , " I perhaps put it in my pocket , " profess to feel for it there .

 " Mirah , let I think that he be my father as well as yours that we can have no sorrow , no disgrace , no joy apart .  the change in Mirah have be gradual . she have not pass at once from anguish to the full , blessed consciousness that , in this moment of grief and shame , Deronda be give she the high tribute man can give to woman . with the first tone and the first word , she have only a sense of solemn comfort , refer this goodness of Deronda 's to his feeling for Ezra . but by degree the rapturous assurance of unhoped - for good take possession of her frame : her face glow under Deronda 's as he bend over she ; yet she look up still with intense gravity , as when she have first acknowledge with religious gratitude that he have think she " worthy of the good ; " and when he have finish , she could say nothing she could only lift up her lip to his and just kiss they , as if that be the simple " yes .   WORDSWORTH : the Prelude . 
 Sir Hugo carry out his plan of spend part of the autumn at Diplow , and by the beginning of October his presence be spread some cheerfulness in the neighborhood , among all rank and person concern , from the stately home of Brackenshaw and Quetcham to the respectable shop - parlor in Wanchester . for Sir Hugo be a man who like to show himself and be affable , a Liberal of good lineage , who confide entirely in reform as not likely to make any serious difference in english habit of feeling , one of which undoubtedly be the liking to behold society well fence and adorn with hereditary rank . hence he make Diplow a most agreeable house , extend his invitation to old Wanchester solicitor and young village curate , but also take some care in the combination of the guest , and not feed all the common poultry together , so that they should think their meal no particular compliment . easy - go Lord Brackenshaw , for example , would not mind meet Robinson the attorney , but Robinson would have be naturally pique if he have be ask to meet a set of people who pass for his equal . on all these point Sir Hugo be well informed enough at once to gain popularity for himself and give pleasure to other two result which eminently suit his disposition . the rector of Pennicote now find a reception at Diplow very different from the haughty tolerance he have undergo during the reign of Grandcourt . it be not that the baronet like Mr. Gascoigne ; it be that he desire to keep up a marked relation of friendliness with he on account of Mrs. Grandcourt , for whom Sir Hugo 's chivalry have become more and more engaged . why ? the chief reason be one that he could not fully communicate , even to Lady Mallinger for he would not tell what he think one woman 's secret to another , even though the other be his wife which show that his chivalry include a rare reticence . 

 Deronda , after he have become engaged to Mirah , feel it right to make a full statement of his position and purpose to Sir Hugo , and he choose to make it by letter . he have more than a presentiment that his fatherly friend would feel some dissatisfaction , if not pain , at this turn of his destiny . in read unwelcome news , instead of hear it , there be the advantage that one avoid a hasty expression of impatience which may afterward be repent of . Deronda dread that verbal collision which make otherwise pardonable feeling lastingly offensive . 

 and Sir Hugo , though not altogether surprised , be thoroughly vex . his immediate resource be to take the letter to Lady Mallinger , who would be sure to express an astonishment which her husband could argue against as unreasonable , and in this way divide the stress of his discontent . and in fact when she show herself astonish and distress that all Daniel 's wonderful talent , and the comfort of have he in the house , should have end in his go mad in this way about the Jews , the baronet could say 

 " oh , nonsense , my dear ! depend upon it , Dan will not make a fool of himself . he have large notion about Judaism political view which you can not   understand . no fear but Dan will keep himself head uppermost . " 

 but with regard to the prospective marriage she afford he no counter - irritant . the gentle lady observe , without rancor , that she have little dreamed of what be come when she have Mirah to sing at her musical party and give lesson to Amabel . after some hesitation , indeed , she confess it have pass through her mind that after a proper time Daniel might marry Mrs. Grandcourt because it seem so remarkable that he should be at Genoa just at that time and although she herself be not fond of widow she could not help think that such a marriage would have be well than his go altogether with the Jews . but Sir Hugo be so strongly of the same opinion that he could not correct it as a feminine mistake ; and his ill - humor at the disproof of his disagreeable conclusion on behalf of Gwendolen be leave without vent . he desire Lady Mallinger not to breathe a word about the affair till further notice , say to himself , " if it be an unkind cut to the poor thing   mean Gwendolen , the long she be without know it the well , in her present nervous state . and she will well learn it from Dan himself .
