 CHAPTER LXX . 

 in the checkered area of human experience the season be all mingle as in the golden age : fruit and blossom hang together ; in the same moment the sickle be reap and the seed be sprinkle ; one tend the green cluster and another tread the winepress . Nay , in each of our life harvest and spring - time be continually one , until himself gather we and sow we anew in his invisible field . 

 among the blessing of love there be hardly one more exquisite than the sense that in unite the beloved life to ours we can watch over its happiness , bring comfort where hardship be , and over memory of privation and suffering open the sweetest fountain of joy . Deronda 's love for Mirah be strongly imbue with that bless protectiveness . even with infantine foot she have begin to tread among thorn ; and the first time he have behold her face it have seem to he the girlish image of despair . 

 but now she be glow like a dark - tip yet delicate ivory - tint flower in the warm sunlight of content , think of any possible grief as part of that life with Deronda , which she could call by no other name than good . and he watch the sober gladness which give new beauty to her movement ; and her habitual attitude of repose , with a delight which make he say to himself that it be enough of personal joy for he to save she from pain . she know nothing of Hans 's struggle or of Gwendolen 's pang ; for after the assurance that Deronda 's hidden love have be for she , she easily explain Gwendolen 's eager solicitude about he as part of a grateful dependence on his goodness , such as she herself have know . and all Deronda 's word about Mrs. Grandcourt confirm that view of their relation , though he never touch on it except in the most distant manner . Mirah be ready to believe that he have be a rescue angel to many besides herself . the only wonder be , that she among they all be to have the bliss of be continually by his side . 

 so , when the bridal veil be around Mirah it hide no doubtful tremor only a thrill of awe at the acceptance of a great gift which require great us . and the velvet canopy never cover a more goodly bride and bridegroom , to whom their people might more wisely wish offspring ; more truthful lip never touch the sacrament marriage - wine ; the marriage - blessing never gather strong promise of fulfillment than in the integrity of their mutual pledge . naturally , they be marry accord to the jewish rite . and since no religion seem yet to have demand that when we make a feast we should invite only the high rank of our acquaintance , few , it be to be hop , will be offend to learn that among the guest at Deronda 's little wedding - feast be the entire Cohen family , with the one exception of the baby who carry on she teethe intelligently at home . how could Mordecai have bear that those friend of his adversity should have be shut out from rejoice in common with he ? 

 Mrs. Meyrick so fully understand this that she have quite reconcile herself to meet the jewish pawnbroker , and be there with her three daughter all of they enjoy the consciousness that Mirah 's marriage to Deronda crown a romance which would always make a sweet memory to they . for which of they , mother or girl , have not have a generous part in it give their good in feeling and in act to she who need ? if Hans could have be there , it would have be well ; but Mab have already observe that man must suffer for be so inconvenient ; suppose she , Kate , and Amy have all fall in love with Mr. Deronda ? but be woman they be not so ridiculous . 

 the Meyricks be reward for conquer their prejudice by hear a speech from Mr. Cohen , which have the rare quality among speech of not be quite after the usual pattern . Jacob eat beyond his year , and contribute several small whinnying laugh as a free accompaniment of his father 's speech , not irreverently , but from a lively sense that his family be distinguish itself ; while Adelaide Rebekah , in a new Sabbath frock , maintain throughout a grave air of responsibility . 

 Mordecai 's brilliant eye , sink in their large socket , dwelt on the scene with the cherish benignancy of a spirit already lift into an aloofness which nullify only selfish requirement and leave sympathy alive . but continually , after his gaze have be travel round on the other , it return to dwell on Deronda with a fresh gleam of trust affection . 

 the wedding - feast be humble , but Mirah be not without splendid wedding - gift . as soon as the betrothal have be know , there be friend who have entertain graceful device . Sir Hugo and Lady Mallinger have take trouble to provide a complete equipment for eastern travel , as well as a precious locket contain an inscription " to the bride of our dear Daniel Deronda all blessing . H. and L. M. " the Klesmers send a perfect watch , also with a pretty inscription . 

 but something more precious than gold and gem come to Deronda from the neighborhood of Diplow on the morning of his marriage . it be a letter contain these word : 

 do not think of I sorrowfully on your wedding - day . I have remember your word that I may live to be one of the good of woman , who make other glad that they be bear . I do not yet see how that can be , but you know well than i. if it ever come true , it will be because you help I . I only think of myself , and I make you grieve . it hurt I now to think of your grief . you must not grieve any more for I . it be well it shall be well with I because I have know you . 

 GWENDOLEN grandcourt . 

 the preparation for the departure of all three to the East begin at once ; for Deronda could not deny Ezra 's wish that they should set out on the voyage forthwith , so that he might go with they , instead of detain they to watch over he . he have no belief that Ezra 's life would last through the voyage , for there be symptom which seem to show that the last stage of his malady have set in . but Ezra himself have say , " never mind where I die , so that I be with you . " 

 he do not set out with they . one morning early he say to Deronda , " do not quit I to - day . I shall die before it be end . " 

 he choose to be dress and sit up in his easy chair as usual , Deronda and Mirah on each side of he , and for some hour he be unusually silent , not even make the effort to speak , but look at they occasionally with eye full of some restful meaning , as if to assure they that while this remnant of breathing - time be difficult , he feel an ocean of peace beneath he . 

 it be not till late in the afternoon , when the light be fall , that he take a hand of each in his and say , look at Deronda , " death be come to I as the divine kiss which be both parting and reunion which take I from your bodily eye and give I full presence in your soul . where thou goest , Daniel , I shall go . be it not begin ? have I not breathe my soul into you ? we shall live together . " 

 he pause , and Deronda wait , think that there might be another word for he . but slowly and with effort Ezra , press on their hand , raise himself and utter in Hebrew the confession of the divine Unity , which long for generation have be on the lip of the die Israelite . 

 he sink back gently into his chair , and do not speak again . but it be some hour before he have cease to breathe , with Mirah 's and Deronda 's arm around he . 

  " nothing be here for tear , nothing to wail 
  or knock the breast ; no weakness , no contempt , 
  Dispraise or blame ; nothing but well and fair , 
  and what may quiet we in a death so noble . "
